segments.p2-10-g48: Subject + adverbial phrase set the scene: Я (I) is the subject. и is a particle adding emphasis ('even' / 'also'); в мечтах своих подпольных is a prepositional phrase ('in my underground dreams') where свой agrees with мечтах and подпольных qualifies those dreams. иначе и не представлял себе любви is a negative past habitual: представлять себе (to imagine/hold in mind) is used in the imperfect past (представлял) with не for negation; иначе (otherwise) qualifies how he could not imagine love, and the second и before не intensifies ('not even').
segments.p2-10-g49: как introduces a comparison/manner: 'as' or 'like'. борьбою is the archaic/instrumental form (борьбою rather than борьбой) expressing manner—'by/as a struggle' (instrumental case used to indicate the means or way).
segments.p2-10-g50: Two habitual past verbs describe the usual pattern: начинал (imperfect past 'used to begin') takes ее (the feminine pronoun referring back to любовь) as its object. с + genitive (ненависти) marks the starting point ('began it with/from hatred'). кончал (imperfect past 'used to end') pairs with an instrumental phrase нравственным покорением to indicate the manner/result ('ended in/by moral subjugation').
segments.p2-10-g51: А introduces a contrast ('and/but'); потом 'afterward'. уж and the following и form an emphatic particle sequence (уж и = 'even/indeed'), heightening the inability. представить себе (perfective infinitive) with не мог (past of мочь) expresses inability in the past: 'could not even imagine.' Note the shift from imperfect (представлял) earlier to perfective представить here to stress a single act/state of imagining that he could not perform.
segments.p2-10-g52: This is the subordinate interrogative-infinitive clause completing 'could not imagine': что делать = 'what to do'. с + покоренным предметом uses с + instrumental (покоренным is a past participle agreeing in case with предметом) to mean 'with the conquered/subdued object.' предметом here is a figurative 'object' (the beloved) in the instrumental after с.
segments.p2-10-g53: Да и here is a discourse particle meaning 'and/besides' (colloquial). Что тут невероятного is a rhetorical question 'what is there unbelievable about it?'; невероятного is in the (elliptical) genitive form used in such questions.
segments.p2-10-g54: Когда introduces a subordinate clause meaning 'when/since.' Уж 'already' intensifies the past verb успел ('had time/managed to'); до того is an intensifying phrase 'to such a degree.' Растлить себя нравственно is reflexive: 'to corrupt myself morally.'
segments.p2-10-g55: До того again intensifies the extent; отвык ('became unaccustomed') takes от + genitive, here от "живой жизни" — 'from "real life"/living life.' The quoted phrase живой жизни means 'real, lived life.'
segments.p2-10-g56: ЧТО continues the content clause. Давеча is an archaic/colloquial adverb 'recently/the other day.' Вздумал + infinitive means 'took it into my head / thought to' and introduces the actions попрекать и стыдить ее тем ('to reproach and shame her by that'). Тем is an adverbial demonstrative 'by that/with that.'
segments.p2-10-g57: This clause spells out the reproach: пришла ко мне слушать 'came to me to listen.' The object "жалкие слова" is placed before the infinitive слушать ('to listen to "pitiful/paltry words"'). Ко мне is the dative directional 'to my place / to me.'
segments.p2-10-g58: А и here introduces a contrast/adding particle ('and even/moreover'). Не догадался сам is past negation 'I did not guess/realize myself'; сам is emphatic 'myself.'
segments.p2-10-g59: This continues the content clause: пришла вовсе не для того — 'she had come not at all for the purpose (of...)'. Вовсе не is strong negation 'not at all,' and для того sets up the purposive construction that follows.
segments.p2-10-g60: Чтоб introduces an infinitival purpose clause 'in order to / to ...' Жалкие слова слушать repeats the earlier object + infinitive order 'to listen to paltry words.' The infinitive слушать expresses purpose.
segments.p2-10-g61: А (but) contrasts the previous purpose with the real one: чтоб любить меня — 'but (she came) to love me.' Любить is the infinitive 'to love'; меня is the accusative object 'me.'
segments.p2-10-g62: Потому что introduces the reason 'because.' Для женщины 'for a woman'; в любви-то uses the emphatic particle -то on 'в любви' to stress 'precisely in love.' Заключается is a present passive/reflexive verb meaning 'consists in / is contained in.' Воскресение here means 'resurrection' or 'revival' in the figurative sense.
segments.p2-10-g63: A continuation of the enumeration: все спасение 'all salvation' от какой бы то ни было гибели is the idiom 'from whatever destruction/ruin' (какой бы то ни было = 'any/whatever'). И все возрождение repeats 'and all rebirth' as another synonymous term.
segments.p2-10-g64: Да here functions as a connective 'and/indeed.' Иначе ... не может is the fixed statement 'it cannot be otherwise'; проявиться is the infinitive reflexive 'to manifest itself,' and может here expresses possibility ('is possible' / 'can').
segments.p2-10-g65: Как в этом closes the clause: как = 'than / as' in the comparative expression не может, как в этом — 'cannot (be) other than in this.' В этом is the locative demonstrative 'in this (thing/situation).'
segments.p2-10-g66: Впрочем is a sentence adverb / discourse particle meaning 'however' or 'besides' that signals a slight qualification or change of tone relative to what was said before; it stands alone and is followed by a comma.
segments.p2-10-g67: This is a past-tense, first-person statement: я (subject) + не (negation) + хорошо уж так combination of degree words + ее (accusative object) + ненавидел (past imperfective verb). Уж and так together soften and nuance the negation: не очень уж так = 'not very / not so much'. Ненавидел is masculine past, imperfective aspect indicating a state or habitual feeling.
segments.p2-10-g68: A temporal subordinate clause introduced by когда ('when'). Бегал is the imperfective past of бегать meaning 'ran about' or 'moved about' (habitual/active motion), followed by the adverbial phrase по комнате ('around the room'). И links the second habitual action: в щелочку (motion into/through a little gap; diminutive) + заглядывал (imperfective 'peered/kept glancing') + за ширмы ('behind the screens'), so the clause describes repeated/ongoing physical actions simultaneous with the speaker's feeling.
segments.p2-10-g69: This is an impersonal past construction: Мне (dative 'to me') places the experiencer, only (только) narrows the focus, невыносимо (adverbial form of 'unbearable') + тяжело (adverb 'heavy/difficult') form the predicative, and было (past neuter of 'to be') completes the impersonal 'it was ... to me' structure. Together they mean 'it was unbearably hard for me'.
segments.p2-10-g70: A subordinate nominal clause introduced by что ('that') giving the content of what was unbearable; она is the subject ('she') and здесь is the locative adverb ('here'). This clause functions as the complement of было in the preceding segment.
segments.p2-10-g1: Через + accusative (Через четверть часа) is a temporal prepositional phrase meaning 'after / a period of' (here 'a quarter of an hour later'). Я бегал is past imperfective, first person singular, expressing repeated or continuous action in the past ('I was running / pacing'). Взад и вперед is an adverbial phrase 'back and forth'. В бешеном нетерпении is a locative/adverbial description formed with в + noun (бешеном нетерпении) describing the manner of his pacing, and по комнате (по + dative) indicates movement around/throughout the room.
segments.p2-10-g2: Поминутно is an adverb meaning 'minute-by-minute / every minute'. Подходил is past imperfective of подходить and here signals repeated approaching (подходил к ширмам — 'kept coming up to the screens'); к + dative marks the target (ширмам). The two verbs are coordinated by и. В + accusative (в щелочку) shows direction/entry 'into/through the little crack', and поглядывал is imperfective repeated glancing/peeking; на + accusative (на Лизу) marks the person being looked at.
segments.p2-10-g3: Она сидела is past imperfective, third person singular feminine, indicating a continuing state in the past ('she was sitting'). На + noun (на полу) indicates location 'on the floor'.
segments.p2-10-g4: Склонив is a deverbative adverbial participle (деепричастие) from склонить meaning 'having bowed / having bent', describing a simultaneous or preceding action with respect to the main verb; it takes a direct object (голову) in the accusative. На + кровать + accusative (на кровать) expresses the direction/place where the head is bent; the word order here (склонив на кровать голову) places the object after the participle for stylistic emphasis.
segments.p2-10-g5: Должно быть is an idiomatic parenthetical modal phrase meaning 'probably / it would seem'; грамматически it's the short neuter form должный + infinitive быть used as a comment or inference marker.
segments.p2-10-g6: Плакала is past imperfective, feminine singular, indicating she was weeping (ongoing/repeated action).
segments.p2-10-g7: Но introduces a contrast ('but'). Она не уходила uses the past imperfective with negation (не уходила) to indicate she did not go away / was not leaving (ongoing non-departure).
segments.p2-10-g8: А contrasts or adds to the previous clause ('and / whereas / but'), and это-то uses the demonstrative это with the emphatic particle -то to single out 'this/that' as the thing stressed. И here intensifies the statement ('and indeed / and that alone'), раздражало is past imperfective neuter singular agreeing with это, and меня is the accusative object 'me'.
segments.p2-10-g9: В этот раз is a temporal phrase (в + accusative) meaning 'on this occasion / this time'. Уже is an adverb 'already' indicating the state is true prior to or by that moment. Знала is past imperfective feminine singular of знать, 'knew' (indicating she was in possession of the knowledge).
segments.p2-10-g139: A single proper name used as a direct address (vocative function) and interjection. Russian does not mark a vocative case here, so the name stands alone with an exclamation mark to indicate calling someone.
segments.p2-10-g140: "крикнул" is the past tense, masculine singular of 'крикнуть' (perfective) meaning 'shouted/Called out'; the subject "я" follows the verb (inverted word order for narrative emphasis). "на лестницу" is на + accusative, indicating direction/target ('toward/to the staircase') of the shout.
segments.p2-10-g141: "но" is the coordinating conjunction 'but', introducing a contrast, and "несмело" is an adverb modifying the manner of the calling ('timidly'/'hesitantly'). Placed after the comma it narrows or softens how the action was done.
segments.p2-10-g142: "вполголоса" is an adverb (written as one word) meaning 'in a low voice' or 'half-whisper'; it further specifies the manner and reinforces the timid tone, placed at the end for emphasis.
segments.p2-10-g145: "Ответа не было" is an impersonal past construction meaning 'there was no answer.' The noun "Ответа" is in the genitive because Russian commonly uses the genitive with negation ('no answer'), and "не было" is the past tense of 'to be' in an impersonal form.
segments.p2-10-g146: "мне показалось" is an impersonal expression with dative "мне" + past reflexive verb "показалось," meaning 'it seemed to me.' The structure expresses a subjective impression rather than an asserted fact.
segments.p2-10-g147: Subordinate clause introduced by "что" ('that'). "я слышу" is present-tense 'I hear,' used to report a perception; "ее" is the feminine possessive 'her' modifying "шаги" ('steps/footsteps'). "на нижних ступеньках" uses на + prepositional to indicate location ('on the lower steps'), with adjective "нижних" agreeing in case, number, and gender.
segments.p2-10-g148: As before, the proper name is used alone to call someone; it functions as direct address/interjection and conveys an urgent or emotional calling.
segments.p2-10-g97: Прощайте is the plural/formal imperative (or fixed farewell formula) of прощать used here as a parting formula; in grammar it functions as a vocative/interjection-like utterance standing alone as the quoted word.
segments.p2-10-g98: проговорила is the past tense, feminine singular, perfective verb (проговорить) meaning 'uttered/said'; она is the subject pronoun in apposition to the verb. Together they form the reporting clause that follows the quoted speech.
segments.p2-10-g99: направляясь is a present active gerund (деепричастие) from направляться, meaning 'moving/heading (toward)'; к + двери is the prepositional phrase indicating direction ('toward the door'), with двери in the genitive as required after к for a noun in motion context.
segments.p2-10-g100: Я is the subject pronoun 'I'; вдруг is an adverb meaning 'suddenly' modifying the verb; подбежал is the past tense, masculine singular, perfective verb подбежать 'ran up to/approached quickly'; к ней is the prepositional phrase indicating the person toward whom he ran (к + pronoun in the dative form).
segments.p2-10-g101: схватил is past tense, masculine singular perfective of схватить 'grabbed/seized'; ее руку is the direct object (ее = 'her', руку = accusative of 'hand'), with the possessive pronoun directly modifying the noun.
segments.p2-10-g102: разжал is past tense, masculine singular, perfective of разжать 'unclench/unfasten' (here: opened her hand); ее is the object pronoun referring back to the hand—syntactically it functions as the direct object 'it' (i.e., 'unclenched it').
segments.p2-10-g103: вложил is the past tense, masculine singular, perfective of вложить 'put into/insert' — the verb is transitive and here the direct object is omitted from the sentence (elliptical), implying he put something into her opened hand.
segments.p2-10-g106: и is the coordinating conjunction 'and'; потом is an adverb 'then' indicating sequence; опять is the adverb 'again'; зажал is past tense, masculine singular of зажать, a perfective verb meaning 'clamped/pressed/held tightly'—together they state a further action done immediately after the previous one.
segments.p2-10-g107: Затем is a temporal adverb 'then/afterwards'; тотчас же is an emphatic pair meaning 'immediately' (тотчас = immediately, же = emphatic particle); отвернулся is past reflexive 'turned away'; и links to отскочил, past tense 'sprang back/away'; поскорей is a colloquial comparative/adverb meaning 'more quickly' or 'as quickly as possible'; в другой угол is a directional phrase with в + accusative (другой угол) 'into another/the other corner'.
segments.p2-10-g108: чтоб introduces a purpose clause ('so as to' / 'in order that'); не видеть is the negative infinitive 'not to see'; по крайней мере is a frozen adverbial phrase meaning 'at least' that qualifies the whole purpose clause and follows it for emphasis.
segments.p2-10-g71: "Я" is the subject pronoun 'I'. "хотел" is the past tense, masculine singular form of the imperfective verb хотеть ('to want'); here it expresses a past desire. The comma signals a pause before the subordinate clause introduced by чтоб.
segments.p2-10-g72: Чтоб (colloquial contraction of чтобы) introduces a subordinate (subjunctive/result) clause 'that / so that'. "она" is the third-person feminine subject 'she'. "исчезла" is the past tense, perfective form of исчезнуть ('to disappear/vanish'), used here to express a completed action that the speaker wished to happen.
segments.p2-10-g73: The noun "Спокойствия" appears fronted for emphasis and is in the genitive because желал often governs the genitive (желать чего-либо = to desire something). The quoted form highlights it as a named thing. "я желал" is the past-tense clause 'I desired', with желал imperfective emphasizing an ongoing/attitudinal desire.
segments.p2-10-g74: The infinitive clause "остаться один в подполье" ('to remain alone in the basement/underground') states the content of the desire; word order places the verb first for stylistic emphasis. "один" here means 'alone'. "в подполье" denotes location ('in the underground/basement'); the final "желал" repeats the past-tense verb for rhetorical effect, restating 'I wanted'.
segments.p2-10-g75: "Живая жизнь" (literally 'living life' or 'real life') is quoted and functions as the grammatical subject; here it means immediate/real life. The phrase "с непривычки" is an adverbial phrase meaning 'from inexperience / for lack of habit' (с + noun in genitive). "придавила" is the past tense, perfective of придавить ('pressed down on / oppressed'), agreeing in gender with the feminine subject; "меня" is the accusative object. "до того" forms the first part of the result construction "до того, что ..." meaning 'to such an extent that ...'.
segments.p2-10-g76: "что" introduces the result clause ('that'). "даже" is an adverbial intensifier 'even'. "дышать" is the infinitive 'to breathe' used as the semantic subject of the impersonal construction. "стало" is the neuter past of стать ('became') used impersonally to form 'became + adjective/infinitive'. "трудно" is a short-form adjective/adverb meaning 'difficult'. Together: 'that even breathing became difficult.'
segments.p2-10-g77: "Но" is the conjunction 'but/however'. "прошло" is the neuter past form of пройти used impersonally to mean 'passed' (of time). "еще" here means 'more/still', and "несколько минут" is the noun phrase 'a few minutes' in the genitive governed by несколько.
segments.p2-10-g78: "а" provides a contrastive 'and/but/while'. "она" is the pronoun 'she'. The fixed phrase "все еще" means 'still' (the entry "все" here functions idiomatically with ещё). "не" negates the verb. "подымалась" is the imperfective past form of подыматься/подниматься ('to get up/raise oneself'), so the action had not yet occurred; the imperfective highlights the ongoing/not-yet nature.
segments.p2-10-g79: "как будто" is a compound conjunction meaning 'as if / as though'. "в" is the preposition 'in'. "забытьи" (archaic/poetic nominal form) means 'oblivion' or more precisely here 'a stupor/trance'. "была" is the past-tense feminine of быть ('to be'), so the clause reads 'as if she were in a stupor.'
segments.p2-10-g10: Я is the 1st-person subject pronoun. Оскорбил is the perfective past tense, masculine singular, indicating a completed action ('offended/insulted'). Ее is the direct object in accusative (her), and окончательно is an adverb modifying the verb meaning 'definitively/for good.' The comma marks a pause before a contrasting continuation.
segments.p2-10-g11: Но is the coordinating conjunction 'but/however' introducing a contrast; here it stands alone at the end to create an abrupt, trailing opposition (ellipsis of the rest of the clause).
segments.p2-10-g14: Нечего is an impersonal predicate meaning 'there is nothing (to...)' used with an infinitive; рассказывать is the imperfective infinitive 'to tell/narrate.' Together they form the impersonal construction 'there is nothing to tell.'
segments.p2-10-g15: Она is the 3rd-person feminine subject 'she.' Догадалась is the perfective past tense, feminine singular of 'to guess/suspect,' indicating she came to that conclusion.
segments.p2-10-g16: Ч то introduces a subordinate content clause ('that'). Порыв моей страсти is the noun phrase subject ('the impulse/outburst of my passion'). Был is the past tense copula 'was.' Именно is an emphatic adverb 'precisely/namely,' and мщением is an instrumental noun used as a predicate ('vengeance')—Russian frequently uses the instrumental for predicate nouns after быть.
segments.p2-10-g17: Новым унижением is a second predicate noun in the instrumental ('a new humiliation'); ей is dative indicating the person affected ('for her'). This apposition repeats/specifies the preceding identification of the outburst as revenge.
segments.p2-10-g18: И joins another subordinate clause (introduced again by что 'that'). К is the preposition introducing the dative noun phrase that follows ('to' or 'toward'). Давешней моей is a descriptive adjective phrase ('my former/previous' or 'recent') modifying the following noun; word order puts the modifier before the noun it will modify in the next segment.
segments.p2-10-g19: Почти ('almost') modifies беспредметной ('aimless, without a specific object'), together describing ненависти in the dative. Прибавилась is an intransitive past tense meaning 'was added' (the verb agrees with the nominative subject that follows), and теперь уже ('now already') signals the temporal addition; личная is the nominative adjective 'personal' which is the logical subject placed after the verb (inverted order).
segments.p2-10-g20: Завистливая ... ненависть is the nominative subject phrase ('an envious/jealous hatred'). К ней is the prepositional phrase 'toward her' using the prepositional/object form ней. This phrase is what 'was added' to the earlier hatred.
segments.p2-10-g23: А впрочем is a discourse connective: А here is a coordinating particle 'and/but,' and впрочем means 'however' or 'moreover,' softening or qualifying the previous statement.
segments.p2-10-g24: Не is the negation particle 'not,' and утверждаю is the 1st-person singular present 'I assert' (here 'I do not assert'). Together they mean 'I do not claim/affirm.'
segments.p2-10-g25: Чтоб introduces the subordinate clause meaning 'that' (colloquial variant of чтобы), она is the subject 'she,' это все is the demonstrative + quantifier 'all this/everything,' and поняла отчетливо is the past tense verb + adverb 'understood clearly.' The whole clause means 'that she understood all this clearly.'
segments.p2-10-g26: Но зато combines to mean 'but on the other hand / however' with a compensatory sense; она вполне поняла is 'she quite/completely understood' (вполне modifying the past-tense verb).
segments.p2-10-g27: Что introduces the content clause 'that ...'; я человек мерзкий is a simple nominal predication 'I am a despicable/disgusting person,' with и linking to the next point. The verb 'to be' is omitted in present tense, as standard in Russian.
segments.p2-10-g28: Главное is a neuter nominal/adverbial parenthetical meaning 'the main thing' or 'above all,' introducing the most important point to follow.
segments.p2-10-g29: Не в состоянии is an impersonal phrase 'not in a state/capable' meaning 'unable/incapable,' followed by the infinitive любить 'to love' and the direct object ее ('her' in accusative). The whole phrase means 'incapable of loving her.'
segments.p2-10-g111: 'Я хотел было' is an idiomatic past construction meaning 'I was about to' (хотел + the particle было gives the sense of a near miss or aborted intention). 'сию минуту' is an elevated/older phrasing for 'this very minute/instant' placed before the infinitive 'солгать' ('to lie') and the dash marks the elaboration 'написать' ('to write') as the specific form of the lie.
segments.p2-10-g112: The clause begins with the conjunction 'что' introducing what he would have written. 'я сделал это' is a simple past statement ('I had done it' / 'I did it'), with 'это' referring to the deed. 'нечаянно' is an adverb modifying 'сделал' meaning 'accidentally' or 'unintentionally.'
segments.p2-10-g113: 'не помня себя' is an absolute participial phrase (present active participle 'помня' with the negator 'не') meaning literally 'not remembering oneself' — idiomatically 'not in one's right mind' or 'not knowing what one was doing.' It modifies the implied action in the previous clause.
segments.p2-10-g114: Single verbal adverb (деепричастие) 'потерявшись' (perfective reflexive form) — 'having lost oneself' / 'having lost one's bearings'; it continues the list of adverbial circumstances explaining how he supposedly acted.
segments.p2-10-g115: 'сдуру' is an adverb meaning 'out of foolishness' or 'foolishly'; it is colloquial/idiomatic and concludes the string of adverbial phrases giving the supposed reason for the deed.
segments.p2-10-g116: A compound main clause: 'Но' ('but') contrasts with the previous thought; 'я не хочу лгать' is present-tense ('I do not want to lie'); 'и потому говорю прямо' ('and therefore I say plainly') links motive ('не хочу лгать') to the speaker's frank statement. 'прямо' here is adverbial: 'straight out' or 'frankly.'
segments.p2-10-g117: Reported content introduced by 'что' ('that'): 'я разжал ей руку' — 'I opened/unclenched her hand' (разжать = open a closed hand; 'ей' is dative 'to her'); 'и положил в нее' — 'and put into it' (the object is omitted in the sentence and must be understood from context). The verbs are past tense, simple narrative.
segments.p2-10-g120: 'со злости' is a short adverbial phrase meaning 'out of anger' or 'from spite' ('со' is a variant of 'с' used before certain consonant clusters; 'злости' is the genitive singular of 'злость' expressing cause).
segments.p2-10-g121: 'Мне' is dative ('to me') and combines with the impersonal past 'пришло' to form the idiom 'мне пришло в голову' = 'it occurred to me'; 'это' is the thing that occurred ('this/it'); 'в голову' literally 'into the head' and 'сделать' is the infinitive expressing the action that occurred to him ('to do').
segments.p2-10-g122: 'когда' introduces the temporal clause. 'я бегал' is the imperfective past ('I was running' / 'I ran about'), and the fixed adverbial pair 'взад и вперед' means 'back and forth.' 'по комнате' (preposition 'по' + locative) means 'around the room' or 'about the room.'
segments.p2-10-g123: 'а' here contrasts the two simultaneous actions ('while' / 'and'), 'она сидела' is past continuous/simple past ('she sat/was sitting'), and 'за ширмами' uses 'за' with the instrumental plural 'ширмами' to mean 'behind the screens' (ширмы = folding screens/dividers).
segments.p2-10-g149: The verb крикнул is past tense, masculine, singular, perfective — 'shouted/cryed out'. It appears before the pronoun (inverted word order) for emphasis; я is the nominative pronoun 'I'. Громче is the comparative adverb 'louder' (comparative of громко) modifying the verb.
segments.p2-10-g150: Нет here is the impersonal negative 'there is no'. The following noun ответа is in the genitive singular because Russian uses genitive after нет: 'no answer' / 'there was no answer.'
segments.p2-10-g151: Но is the conjunction 'but/however'. В ту же минуту is a temporal phrase meaning 'at that very moment' — ту (accusative feminine of тот) + же (emphatic particle) stresses 'the same moment'. Я услышал is 'I heard' (past tense); снизу is an adverb 'from below' indicating the source of the sound.
segments.p2-10-g152: Как here is the exclamatory/manner adverb 'how' (introducing the manner of opening). Тяжело is an adverb meaning 'heavily' / 'with effort' describing how the door opened.
segments.p2-10-g153: С визгом ('with a squeal') uses с + instrumental to indicate manner. Отворилась is the past tense, reflexive/intransitive verb 'opened' (the verb agrees with дверь, feminine). Тугая, наружная, стеклянная are attributive adjectives (nominative feminine singular) modifying дверь 'door'. На улицу is directional 'to/onto the street' (на + accusative). И joins the second verb туго захлопнулась: туго is an adverb 'tightly' modifying захлопнулась, which is past reflexive 'slammed shut.'
segments.p2-10-g154: Гул is the nominative subject 'a low hum / murmur'. Поднялся is past tense, reflexive/perfective 'rose / ascended' describing upward motion. По here means 'along' (motion along a path) and лестнице is the noun in the dative/locative form used after по to indicate the route — 'up the staircase.'
segments.p2-10-g124: Но вот что introduces a resumed statement: 'Но' = 'but/however' and 'вот' is a discourse particle ('here/now' or 'well') pointing to what follows. 'что' begins the content clause 'what I can say'; 'я' is the subject; 'наверно могу сказать' is a modal cluster: 'наверно' (probably) modifies 'могу' (can/be able to) plus the infinitive 'сказать' (to say). The colon is natural in Russian to mark the quoted content that follows.
segments.p2-10-g125: 'я сделал эту жестокость' is a simple past clause: 'я' subject, 'сделал' past tense perfective of 'сделать' (I did/committed), and the direct object 'эту жестокость' ('this cruelty'). In colloquial/literary Russian 'сделал жестокость' means 'committed a cruel act' — 'сделал' here expresses a completed action.
segments.p2-10-g126: 'хоть и нарочно' is a concessive adverbial phrase: 'хоть' (although/even if) combined with particle 'и' (here adding emphasis) plus the adverb 'нарочно' (deliberately). Together they mean 'even though (it was) deliberate' and qualify the preceding clause.
segments.p2-10-g127: 'но не от сердца' contrasts with the previous concession: 'но' = 'but', 'не' negates, and 'от сердца' literally 'from the heart' (genitive) indicates source or motive; the phrase means 'but not from the heart' (i.e., not from genuine feeling).
segments.p2-10-g128: 'а от дурной моей головы' continues the contrast with 'а' ('but/whereas'), using 'от' (from) to mark the source; 'дурной' (foolish/bad) modifies 'моей головы' (my head, genitive), an idiomatic way to say 'from my foolish mind' rather than from feeling. The adjective precedes the possessive, a natural order in Russian.
segments.p2-10-g129: Эта жестокость была ...: 'Эта' (this) + noun 'жестокость' (cruelty) and past copula 'была' (was) form the predicative clause. 'до того' is an intensifier meaning 'to such an extent' / 'so (much)' and governs the adjective 'напускная' (affected/put-on) as the predicate adjective agreeing in gender/number with 'жестокость'.
segments.p2-10-g130: The repeated 'до того' again intensifies the following adjective 'головная'. Here 'головная' is used predicatively to characterize the cruelty (literal options in the lexicon are limited, but in context it conveys a kind of 'heady' or self-important quality). The structure is parallel to the previous segment.
segments.p2-10-g131: 'нарочно подсочиненная' is a compound adjectival description: 'нарочно' (deliberately) modifies the participial adjective 'подсочиненная' (literally 'added-on' / 'contrived, concocted'). Grammatically it's a past passive participle used as an adjective describing the cruelty as intentionally concocted.
segments.p2-10-g132: 'книжная' is a single adjective (bookish, literary) further qualifying 'жестокость'. Placed in the same list as the earlier adjectives, it adds the notion that the cruelty was literary or textbook-like rather than genuine.
segments.p2-10-g133: The result clause introduced by 'что' ('that') follows the 'so ... that' construction ('до того ... что'). 'я сам' ('I myself') is emphatic; 'не выдержал' is past tense negative of 'выдержать' (to endure/stand), and 'даже минуты' ('even a minute') intensifies the impossibility of enduring. Together: 'that I myself could not endure even a minute.'
segments.p2-10-g134: The dash leads into the narrator's immediate reaction. 'сначала' ('at first') sets temporal order; 'отскочил' is perfective past 'sprang back/jumped back'; 'в угол' ('into a corner') marks direction with preposition 'в' + accusative. Subject 'I' is carried over from the previous clause (elliptical subject).
segments.p2-10-g135: 'чтоб не видеть' is a purposive subordinate clause: 'чтоб' = 'so that/in order that', 'не' negates, and 'видеть' is the infinitive 'to see' — 'so as not to see.' It gives the motive for jumping back.
segments.p2-10-g136: 'а потом' ('and then/later') continues the narrative sequence; 'со стыдом и отчаянием' ('with shame and despair') is an instrumental phrase describing the narrator's emotional state; 'бросился' is perfective past of 'броситься' ('threw himself/rushed'), 'вслед за' + instrumental ('Лизой') means 'after Liza' or 'in pursuit of Liza.' The whole clause means that afterward, ashamed and desperate, he rushed after Liza.
segments.p2-10-g137: This is a single coordinated clause. Я is the nominative subject 'I'. Отворил is the past tense, masculine singular of отворить meaning 'opened'; it is a perfective past action. Дверь is the direct object in accusative. В + сени takes the accusative/locative sense 'into the entryway' (в as motion/toward here). И connects the two predicates; стал + infinitive (стал прислушиваться) is a periphrastic construction meaning 'began to...' (стать + infinitive marks the onset of an action). Прислушиваться is the imperfective infinitive 'to listen closely'.
segments.p2-10-g138: This is a vocative/exclamatory utterance: the name Лиза is called aloud. No grammatical markers are needed in Russian for a vocative; the punctuation and context show it is an address or outcry.
segments.p2-10-g80: Я имел is the past-tense, literally 'I had/possessed' and here introduces an idiom meaning 'I had the audacity/cheek.' Бессовестность is the direct object in accusative and names that audacity. Тихонько (adverb) precedes the infinitive постучать (perfective infinitive 'to knock/tap'), showing manner of the intended action; в + ширмы expresses direction/target ('toward/at the folding screens'). Word order puts manner before the verb and the object of the preposition last.
segments.p2-10-g81: Чтоб is a colloquial form of чтобы and here introduces purpose ('in order to'). Напомнить is the perfective infinitive 'to remind' and takes the dative ей ('to her'), so the whole phrase means 'in order to remind her.'
segments.p2-10-g84: Она is the subject 'she.' Вдруг is an adverb 'suddenly.' Встрепенулась is the past reflexive/perfective of встрепенуться and denotes a sudden start or agitation ('gave a start; started up'); the reflexive suffix marks the intransitive, sudden bodily movement.
segments.p2-10-g85: Схватилась с места literally 'seized herself from the place' and idiomatically means 'jumped up from her seat' — с + genitive места = 'from (her) seat/spot.' И connects to бросилась искать, where бросилась + infinitive (искать) means 'hurled herself into / rushed to look for.' Свой is the reflexive possessive agreeing with the subject (her own), and платок is the direct object.
segments.p2-10-g86: Свою is the reflexive possessive emphasizing that the following item belongs to the subject; шляпку is the diminutive accusative of шляпка ('little hat'), another direct object in the series.
segments.p2-10-g87: Шубу is the accusative singular of шуба ('fur coat') and continues the list of things she hurriedly looks for; the possessor is understood from the preceding свою.
segments.p2-10-g88: Точно here functions as a particle meaning 'as if.' Спасаясь is the present adverbial participle (деепричастие) of спасаться ('to save oneself/escape'), giving the manner ('as if escaping'). От меня is the prepositional phrase 'from me,' and куда-то is an indefinite adverb ('to somewhere') formed by adding the particle то to куда.
segments.p2-10-g91: Через две минуты is a temporal phrase 'in/after two minutes' (через + accusative expresses time span). Она is the subject; медленно is an adverb modifying the verb. Вышла is the past perfective of выйти ('came out/stepped out'); из-за combines из + за ('from behind') and takes the genitive ширм ('the screens'). И links the second clause; тяжело is an adverb meaning 'gravely, heavily' modifying поглядела (perfective past 'glanced/looked'); на меня marks the object of the glance ('at me').
segments.p2-10-g92: Я is the subject. Злобно is an adverb 'maliciously' modifying усмехнулся, which is the past perfective reflexive 'smirked' or 'gave a slight, scornful smile' (усмехнуться denotes a restrained, often hostile smile).
segments.p2-10-g93: Впрочем is a linking particle meaning 'however' or 'though' here introducing a qualification of the previous action ('I smirked, though...').
segments.p2-10-g94: Насильно is an adverb 'forcibly' or 'by force' indicating that the smile was not spontaneous but forced.
segments.p2-10-g95: Для + приличия ('for decency' / 'for the sake of appearances') states the motive; приличия is in the genitive as required by для and names 'decency/propriety.'
segments.p2-10-g96: И is 'and.' Отворотился is the past reflexive of отворотиться meaning 'turned away' or 'averted oneself.' От + ее взглядa uses от with the genitive to mean 'away from her gaze' (ее is the possessive/attributive pronoun referring to 'her'; взгляда is genitive of взгляд 'gaze').
segments.p2-10-g179: Что is the interrogative pronoun 'what' used here as part of a short exclamatory/question ('what? / well?'). The enclitic particle ж follows it and adds emphasis/expectant tone (like 'then' or 'indeed'); together 'что ж' functions idiomatically as 'well, then?'.
segments.p2-10-g180: Я is the subject 'I'. Мог is the past-tense, masculine singular form of мочь and here works as a modal 'could / could have' indicating (lack of) possibility. Ожидать is the infinitive 'to expect/await'; together 'я мог ожидать' yields 'I could (have) expected', with the comma signalling a following subordinate clause.
segments.p2-10-g181: Что is the subordinating conjunction 'that' introducing reported content. Она is the subject 'she'. Это is a demonstrative pronoun 'this/it' placed for emphasis (somewhat redundant in Russian). Сделает is future, 3rd person singular of сделать ('to do/make') and here reports what she would do — 'that she would do it.'
segments.p2-10-g182: Мог is again past of мочь used as a modal 'could' in a rhetorical question; the subject 'I' is omitted (understood from context). Ожидать is the infinitive 'to expect' — the bare infinitive after the modal makes a concise rhetorical question meaning 'Could I have expected (that)?'.
segments.p2-10-g183: Нет is a simple negative response meaning 'no' or 'not so'; here it stands alone as a blunt denial of the possibility just raised ('No, I could not').
segments.p2-10-g155: Она is the third-person feminine pronoun 'she'. Ушла is the past tense, perfective, feminine singular form of уйти (with the -ла ending), meaning a completed action 'left' or 'went away'; the finite verb agrees in gender with the implied subject and completes the simple past clause.
segments.p2-10-g156: Я is the subject 'I'. Воротился is the past tense, masculine singular, reflexive form of воротиться (a variant of 'to return/turn back'), indicating that the speaker returned; the reflexive ending -ся is part of the verb. В комнату uses в + accusative to mark motion into the room ('into the room'). В раздумье is a frozen adverbial phrase meaning 'in (a state of) thought/meditation' (в + noun), functioning as an adverbial modifier describing the speaker's mental state as he returned.
segments.p2-10-g157: Ужасно is an intensifying adverb here meaning 'terribly' or 'extremely'. Тяжело is a predicative adverb/short-form adjective meaning 'hard' or 'burdensome'. Мне is the dative 'to me', marking the experiencer in the impersonal construction. Было is the past tense neuter of 'to be' used impersonally ('it was'); together the phrase means 'it was very hard for me.'
segments.p2-10-g158: Я is 'I'. Остановился is the past tense, masculine singular, reflexive form of остановиться ('to stop; to pause'), indicating the speaker ceased moving. У + noun in the genitive (у стола) means 'by/at the table' (position near the table). Возле + genitive (возле стула) is another locative phrase 'near the chair'; both phrases together describe the exact location where the speaker stopped.
segments.p2-10-g159: На introduces the relative clause and takes the prepositional case here. Котором is the masculine singular relative pronoun in the prepositional (locative) case ('on which'), agreeing with стуле/chair understood from the previous phrase. Она is 'she'. Сидела is the past feminine singular of сидеть, 'sat' or 'was sitting', describing her prior posture on that chair.
segments.p2-10-g160: И is the conjunction 'and'. Бессмысленно is an adverb meaning 'pointlessly' or 'senselessly' and modifies the verb. Смотрел is the past tense, masculine singular of смотреть, 'looked' or 'stared' (imperfective, describing the ongoing action). Перед собой is a fixed spatial phrase 'in front of oneself' (перед + reflexive собой), here meaning he looked ahead of him, literally 'before himself.'
segments.p2-10-g197: Было is an impersonal past-tense form of быть used to state a general condition ('it was'); тихо is an adverb (from тихий) describing that condition. The order (verb before adverb) is natural for an impersonal weather/scene statement: 'It was quiet.'
segments.p2-10-g198: Валил снег uses валил (imperfective past) as a colloquial weather verb meaning 'was pouring/down' about continuous/heavy snowfall; снег is the grammatical subject. The second verb, падал, also in imperfective past, continues to describe how the same snow behaved ('fell'); почти перпендикулярно is an adverbial modifier ('almost perpendicularly').
segments.p2-10-g199: Настилая is a деепричастие (imperfective gerund) from настилать, expressing a concurrent, ongoing action: 'while / thereby laying down'; подушку is the accusative object (a figurative 'cushion' of snow). На + accusative (тротуар, улицу) gives the surface covered; the gerund links this action to the preceding verbs (the snow fell, thereby laying a cushion).
segments.p2-10-g200: Никого ... не было is an impersonal negative-existential construction: 'there was no one.' Прохожих is genitive plural ('passersby') used with не было to indicate absence. Word order (никого before было) emphasizes the negation.
segments.p2-10-g201: Никакого звука is another negated existential in genitive ('no sound / not a sound'); слышалось is an impersonal past-passive/mediopassive form meaning 'was heard' or 'could be heard.' Together they mean 'not a sound was audible.'
segments.p2-10-g202: Уныло and бесполезно are adverbs modifying the past-imperfective verb мерцали ('they were flickering' / 'flickered'); фонари is the plural nominative subject. The adjective order (adverbs before verb, subject after verb) gives a slightly literary inverted word order: 'gloomily and uselessly flickered the lamps.'
segments.p2-10-g203: Отбежал is past tense, perfective/endpoint sense 'ran off / ran a short distance'; конструкция 'шагов двести' is a colloquial quantitative phrase with шагов in genitive and the numeral following it, meaning 'about two hundred steps.' До + перекрестка marks the endpoint ('as far as the intersection'). И остановился links the next completed action: 'and stopped.' The verbs are masculine past, locating the narrator as male.
segments.p2-10-g247: Ненавистью is the singular instrumental form of ненависть. The instrumental here expresses manner or means (i.e., 'with/through hatred') and is used elliptically as a one-word sentence to answer or qualify the previous thought.
segments.p2-10-g250: Гм is an interjection representing a thoughtful or skeptical murmur (equivalent to English 'hm' or 'hmm'). It functions as a discourse marker and has no inflection.
segments.p2-10-g253: Здесь 'может' is an elliptical, impersonal use of the verb мочь meaning 'perhaps' or 'maybe' (short for может быть). It expresses possibility rather than ability.
segments.p2-10-g254: 'И' here has the sense 'even/also', adding emphasis ('perhaps even ...'). 'Прощением' is the instrumental singular of прощение, indicating means or manner ('by/with forgiveness'). Together they mean 'perhaps even by forgiveness.'
segments.p2-10-g257: А is a coordinating particle/conjunction used contrastively here ('and/but/then') to link the remark to what precedes and introduce a new turn of thought.
segments.p2-10-g258: Впрочем is an adverbial discourse particle meaning 'however/after all/anyway' that softens or qualifies the previous statement; here it signals a reconsideration or qualification of what was just said.
segments.p2-10-g259: Легче is the comparative adverb from лёгкий meaning 'easier' (used predicatively). The enclitic particle ль (archaic/spelling variant of ли) marks the yes–no question. Ей is the dative pronoun 'to her' (the person affected). От всего этого is a prepositional phrase 'from/all this' indicating cause or source, and будет is the future of быть ('will be'); together they form 'will it be easier for her from all this?' — i.e. 'will she be any better off for all this?'
segments.p2-10-g30: 'Я знаю' is a simple present 1st-person statement ('I know') used here as a discourse opener meaning 'I'm aware' or 'I admit'. The verb 'знаю' is finite, present tense, and the pronoun 'я' is the explicit subject.
segments.p2-10-g31: 'мне скажут' is an impersonal/indirect-future clause meaning 'they will say to me' (or 'people will tell me'). 'мне' is the dative of the experiencer 'to me', and 'скажут' is the 3rd-person plural perfective-future form of 'сказать' ('will say/tell').
segments.p2-10-g32: 'что это невероятно' is a subordinate clause introduced by the conjunction 'что' ('that'). 'это' is the demonstrative subject 'this/it', and 'невероятно' is a predicative (adverbial/adjective used predicatively) meaning 'is incredible'. The order is the neutral 'that this is incredible.'
segments.p2-10-g33: In '- невероятно быть таким злым,' 'невероятно' is an impersonal predicative meaning 'it is incredible' and is followed by the infinitive 'быть' ('to be') that introduces the state being judged. 'таким злым' is a descriptive phrase: 'таким' ('such/so') modifies the adjective 'злым' ('wicked/mean') — together 'to be so wicked.'
segments.p2-10-g34: 'глупым' continues the predicate qualities in the same construction and is an adjective in the same case/form as 'злым' (so: 'so stupid'). The adjective stands after a comma for rhetorical effect and completes the pair of qualities being judged 'incredible.'
segments.p2-10-g35: 'как я' is a short comparative clause: 'as I (am)' or 'like me' with the verb omitted (ellipsis of 'am'). 'как' introduces the comparison and 'я' is the pronoun subject of the implied comparison.
segments.p2-10-g36: 'пожалуй' is a parenthetical/epistemic particle meaning 'perhaps' or 'I suppose', marking a tentative addition to what the narrator expects people to say.
segments.p2-10-g37: 'еще прибавят' is future 3rd-person plural ('they will add still/more'); 'еще' signals 'in addition' or 'even further', and 'прибавят' here means 'will add (in speech)' — i.e., the imagined interlocutors will go on to say more.
segments.p2-10-g38: This closing clause is an impersonal past statement 'невероятно было' ('it was incredible') followed by two infinitival negative constructions: 'не полюбить её' ('not to have fallen in love with her' — 'ее' is the direct object 'her') and 'не оценить этой любви' ('not to appreciate/value this love'). 'по крайней мере' is the fixed phrase 'at least' introducing the mitigation.
segments.p2-10-g39: 'Отчего' is a single-word interrogative meaning 'why' or 'for what reason'; the particle 'же' adds emphasis (roughly 'then' or 'indeed'), and 'невероятно' is the predicative 'incredible'. Together: 'Why (then) is it incredible?'
segments.p2-10-g40: 'Во-первых' is a fixed introductory phrase meaning 'firstly' or 'first of all' used to structure the speaker's argument. Grammatically it is adverbial and does not inflect here.
segments.p2-10-g41: 'я и полюбить уж не мог' literally 'I even to-fall-in-love already not could' — the infinitive 'полюбить' ('to fall in love') follows the modal 'мог' ('could') in a past-tense negated construction 'не мог' ('could not'); 'и' here has an emphatic 'even/also' sense and 'уж' is an intensifier ('already/indeed'), so the whole line means 'I could not even fall in love.'
segments.p2-10-g42: 'потому что' is the two-word causal conjunction 'because' introducing the reason clause that follows.
segments.p2-10-g43: 'повторяю' is present 1st-person 'I repeat', a parenthetical marker underlining that the narrator has already made this point.
segments.p2-10-g44: The clause 'любить у меня - значило тиранствовать и нравственно превосходствовать' states a generalization: 'for me (у меня) to love meant to tyrannize and to be morally superior.' 'у меня' indicates 'in my case'/'with me', the dash links the infinitive subject 'любить' to the past-predicate 'значило' ('meant'), and the two infinitives after list what love 'meant.'
segments.p2-10-g45: 'Я всю жизнь не мог даже представить себе иной любви' — 'I, my whole life, could not even imagine for myself another love.' 'всю жизнь' is a time expression 'all my life'; 'представить себе' is the reflexive-collocation 'to imagine (for oneself)'; 'иной любви' is genitive 'another/other kind of love.' The clause ends with 'и до того дошел' ('and I came to that point'), where 'дошел' is past tense 'reached' and 'до того' means 'to such an extent.'
segments.p2-10-g46: 'что иногда теперь думаю' introduces a consequence clause: 'that I sometimes now think.' 'что' is the subordinating conjunction, 'иногда' = 'sometimes', 'теперь' = 'now', and 'думаю' is 1st-person present 'I think.'
segments.p2-10-g47: This clause explains the narrator's current thought: 'что любовь-то и заключается в добровольно дарованном от любимого предмета праве над ним тиранствовать.' 'любовь-то' uses the particle '-то' to emphasize 'love itself'; 'и заключается в' ('indeed consists in'/'consists precisely in') is the reflexive verb 'заключаться в' meaning 'consist in.' The noun phrase 'в добровольно дарованном ... праве' is prepositional ('in the right') and contains the passive participle 'дарованном' ('granted') modified by 'добровольно' ('voluntarily') and by the agent phrase 'от любимого предмета' ('by the beloved object'); 'праве над ним' = 'the right over him' and the final infinitive 'тиранствовать' is 'to tyrannize.'
segments.p2-10-g204: "Куда" is an interrogative adverb meaning 'to where' and selects a motion verb. "Пошла" is the past tense, feminine singular form of the perfective verb пойти (the -ла ending marks feminine past); it indicates a completed act of setting off. The pronoun "она" is placed after the verb (Куда пошла она?) for emphasis or sharpness; neutral word order would be Куда она пошла?
segments.p2-10-g205: "И" is the simple coordinating conjunction 'and' linking this question to the previous thought. "Зачем" is an interrogative adverb meaning 'for what purpose / why' (often rhetorical). "Я" is the first-person subject pronoun. "Бегу" is the present-tense, 1st-person singular form of the imperfective verb бежать, expressing ongoing or current running. "За ней" is a prepositional phrase with the preposition за meaning 'after/behind' in the sense of following someone, and "ней" is the oblique form of она used after prepositions to indicate the person being followed.
segments.p2-10-g206: A single-word rhetorical question: the adverb "Зачем" repeats the previous question in a more emphatic, incredulous tone and literally asks 'for what purpose' or 'why'. It stands alone here as an exclamation.
segments.p2-10-g207: "Упасть" is the perfective infinitive 'to fall' or 'to fall down / to prostrate oneself' and is used here as one item in a list of actions; "перед ней" is a prepositional phrase with перед meaning 'in front of / before, in the presence of' and "ней" the oblique form of 'she', so together it conveys 'to fall before her' or 'to throw oneself down in front of her.'
segments.p2-10-g208: "Зарыдать" is a perfective infinitive meaning 'to burst into sobs / to break out crying.' The phrase "от раскаяния" uses the preposition "от" meaning 'from / because of' to indicate cause, with раскаяния (genitive of раскаяние) 'remorse,' so together 'to burst into sobs from remorse.'
segments.p2-10-g209: "Целовать" is the imperfective infinitive 'to kiss' listed as another action. "Ее" is the unstressed accusative form of 'she' meaning 'her' (direct object). "Ноги" is the plural of нога ('leg/foot') and here means 'feet' (the body part) as the object being kissed; the word order is standard object after verb.
segments.p2-10-g210: "Молить" is an infinitive meaning 'to beg, to implore.' It governs the prepositional phrase "о прощении" where "о" means 'about/concerning' but idiomatically combines with прощении (prepositional of прощение) to mean 'for forgiveness'—so 'to beg for forgiveness.' The exclamation mark shows this is a passionate, rhetorical list of actions.
segments.p2-10-g211: Я (I) is the subject in nominative; и here is an emphatic/augmentative particle meaning 'even' or 'also' rather than a simple coordinating 'and', so Я и хотел этого reads 'I even wanted that.' Хотел is past tense, masculine singular, indicating a completed desire in the past; этого is the genitive/partitive form of это used after хотеть ('to want (of) this/that').
segments.p2-10-g212: вся грудь моя places the emphatic вся ('whole/all') before the noun and the possessive моя after it (a stylistic, slightly elevated order): 'my whole chest.' Разрывалась is the imperfective past reflexive/passive-like form meaning 'was being torn' or 'kept tearing itself' (an ongoing/emotional state), and на части is the adverbial phrase 'to pieces/into parts.'
segments.p2-10-g213: и is the coordinating conjunction linking this clause to the next; никогда is the adverb 'never' and here marks the beginning of the emphatic negation repeated in the following segment (the comma signals a pause for rhetorical effect).
segments.p2-10-g214: никогда ... не вспомяну is a strong future negation: никогда (never) + не (not) + вспомяну (perfective future first person singular 'I will recall/remember'). Вспомяну is a literary/older variant (cf. вспОмню) meaning 'I will recall/remember'; the subject pronoun я follows the verb for emphasis. Равнодушно modifies the verb ('indifferently'), and эту минуту is the direct object 'this minute/moment.'
segments.p2-10-g215: Но is the adversative conjunction 'but/however' introducing a sudden counter-question; the dash marks a dramatic pause. Зачем is a single-word interrogative 'what for/why' asking about purpose or motive (here rhetorical).
segments.p2-10-g216: Подумалось мне is an impersonal past form of подуматься meaning 'it occurred to me' or 'I thought' (literally 'it thought itself to me'); the verb appears before the dative мне, which is the experiencer, a common order for impersonal constructions.
segments.p2-10-g217: Разве introduces a rhetorical question and often implies disbelief or expectancy of a negative answer; here it frames 'Разве я не возненавижу ее' as 'Won't I (really) come to hate her?' Возненавижу is perfective future first person singular 'I will come to hate'; ее is the feminine accusative object 'her.'
segments.p2-10-g218: может быть is a frozen parenthetical phrase meaning 'perhaps' or 'maybe'; grammatically it's composed of the modal verb root может ('may/possible') plus быть ('to be'), but together they function as an adverbial hedge here.
segments.p2-10-g219: завтра means 'tomorrow'; the particle же after it gives immediacy or emphasis ('as soon as tomorrow' / 'tomorrow itself') and strengthens the speaker's suggestion that the event could happen quickly.
segments.p2-10-g220: именно is an emphatic adverb 'precisely' or 'exactly.' За то here is part of the causal phrase 'за то, что' meaning 'for the fact that / because,' where за is the preposition 'for (because of)' and то is the demonstrative 'that' that will be linked to the following clause introduced by что.
segments.p2-10-g221: что introduces the subordinate clause giving the reason ('that/because'); сегодня is 'today.' Целовал is imperfective past masculine singular 'kissed' (it describes the past action without perfective completion nuance), with the subject omitted (understood from earlier 'I'); ее ноги is the direct object 'her feet/legs' (ноги plural commonly used for 'feet'). Word order is standard object after verb.
segments.p2-10-g260: "А в самом деле:" is a fixed, colloquial discourse phrase meaning roughly "but really" or "indeed." Here А is a linking particle, в + самом + деле is a prepositional phrase (в + дело in the prepositional with the emphatic сам) literally 'in the very matter/thing' but functioning idiomatically as 'in fact' or 'really.' The colon marks the speaker's shift to a self-addressed remark.
segments.p2-10-g261: вот я теперь уж от себя задаю один праздный вопрос: — "вот" here is a discourse marker ('well/now') drawing attention; the clause's subject is я and задаю is present-tense, 1st-person singular of задавать ('I ask' / 'I pose'). Уж is an intensifying particle ('already/indeed'), теперь 'now.' От себя literally 'from myself' = 'to myself' (idiomatic 'I ask myself'). Один праздный вопрос is the direct object in accusative: 'a single/one idle (i.e. pointless) question.'
segments.p2-10-g262: что лучше - дешевое ли счастие или возвышенные страдания ? — This is an elliptic interrogative: the copula 'is' (есть) is omitted, typical in Russian, so 'что лучше' = 'what/which is better.' The dash sets off the two alternatives. Дешёвое ли счастие uses the enclitic ли attached after дешевое to form the 'whether ... or ...' alternative ('whether cheap happiness'), and или connects the second alternative возвышенные страдания ('sublime/elevated sufferings') in the nominative; the comparative лучше functions as the predicate.
segments.p2-10-g263: Ну-ка is a colloquial prompting particle meaning 'well now' / 'come on' used to urge a response or to challenge someone to answer; it is an inseparable two-part particle (ну + ка) functioning like an interjection and has no grammatical subject or verb.
segments.p2-10-g264: что лучше? — A brief interrogative clause where что ('what/which') is the question word and лучше is the comparative adverb/adjective functioning predicatively ('better'). As in the previous sentence, the copula is omitted, so the meaning is 'Which is better?'.
segments.p2-10-g265: Так is an adverb 'thus/so' specifying the manner. Мне is a dative experiencer 'to me'. Мерещилось is an impersonal/past form of мерещиться meaning 'it seemed (illusory)' or 'I imagined' — the verb commonly appears with the experiencer in dative and the verb in neuter past, conveying that something appeared to the speaker.
segments.p2-10-g266: Когда introduces a temporal subordinate clause 'when'. Я is the subject; сидел is past tense masculine singular 'was sitting' or 'sat'. В тот вечер is a time adverbial 'that evening' (в + тот + вечер). У себя дома is a reflexive possessive phrase meaning 'at my own home' (у + себя emphasizes one's own place; дома here functions as 'at home').
segments.p2-10-g267: Едва is an adverb 'barely/scarcely' modifying the predicate adjective живой 'alive'. The phrase is a verbless predicative construction: the speaker (I) is implicitly the subject described as 'scarcely alive'. От + genitive (душевной боли) indicates cause 'from/because of' mental or emotional pain; душевной is an adjective meaning 'mental/spiritual' or 'of the soul', and боли is the genitive of 'pain'.
segments.p2-10-g268: Никогда at the start is a sentence adverb meaning 'never' (emphatic). The verb выносил is past tense, imperfective, masculine form agreeing with the male narrator 'я' and here renders a past English perfect sense ('had borne'); не negates it. Ещё here (after the negation) gives the sense 'yet/before' — together: 'had never yet borne so much...'. Столько governs the genitive plural that follows (столько страдания и раскаяния).
segments.p2-10-g269: Но is the adversative 'but'. Разве is a rhetorical particle introducing a question of doubt ('really?/could it be?'). Могло быть is an impersonal past construction ('could there be/was it possible'); хоть какое-либо is a lit. 'even any' intensifier meaning 'any' (хоть = 'even/any'), and сомнение is the noun 'doubt' — together: 'but could there possibly have been any doubt'.
segments.p2-10-g270: Когда introduces a temporal subordinate clause ('when'). Выбегал is the imperfective past of выбегать ('ran out' / 'was running out'), describing the event from which the certainty follows; из квартиры gives the source location 'out of the apartment'.
segments.p2-10-g271: Что introduces a content clause (the clause thought to be undoubted): 'that I would not return...'. Возвращусь is the future tense of the perfective возвратиться (1st person singular), so in English 'I would/will not return'; the negation не applies to the verb. С полдороги is a fixed adverbial/phrase meaning 'from halfway' (i.e. 'turn back halfway' / 'not even get back from halfway'), and домой marks direction 'home'.
segments.p2-10-g161: Прошло is an impersonal past tense (perfective) meaning ‘(it) passed’ used here of time. С минуту is an adverbial time phrase ('for/about a minute') with с + accusative; the whole segment means 'a minute passed' and is set off by a comma.
segments.p2-10-g162: Вдруг is an adverb meaning 'suddenly'; я is the nominative subject 'I'; весь here intensifies the verb (≈ 'all over' / 'completely'); вздрогнул is past tense, perfective, masculine singular of вздрогнуть meaning 'to start/shudder'. Together: 'suddenly I gave a start' (весь modifies the manner of вздрогнул).
segments.p2-10-g163: Прямо is an adverb 'directly' or 'straight'; перед is a preposition meaning 'in front of'; собой is the reflexive form of себя used after the preposition to mean 'oneself' (here 'in front of me'). Word order (adverb + preposition + reflexive) stresses exact location 'directly in front of me'.
segments.p2-10-g164: На is the locative preposition 'on' (location); столе is the prepositional/locative form of стол ('on the table'). This is a short locative phrase specifying where the sighting occurs.
segments.p2-10-g165: Я is the subject 'I'; увидал is an older/colloquial past form meaning 'saw' (equivalent to увидел/увидать). The object is omitted but implied (hence my translation 'I saw it'). The short blunt clause follows the locative phrases for dramatic effect.
segments.p2-10-g168: Одним словом is an idiom ('in a word' / 'in short') formed with the noun слово in the instrumental (словом) indicating manner or summary. It introduces a concise conclusion.
segments.p2-10-g169: Я is the subject 'I'; увидал (past) = 'saw' (see note: archaic/colloquial form). Смятую синюю пятирублевую бумажку is a string of modifiers in accusative feminine singular (смятую = past participle 'crumpled'; синюю = adjective 'blue'; пятирублевую = numeral adjective 'five-ruble') modifying бумажку ('a little piece of paper' / here, a banknote). Word order is modifier sequence before the noun.
segments.p2-10-g170: Ту самую is a demonstrative + intensifier in feminine accusative: ту = 'that (one)' and самую = 'the very (one)'. Together they single out the exact same object mentioned earlier.
segments.p2-10-g171: Которую introduces a relative clause agreeing with бумажку (fem. acc.): 'which/that'. Минуту назад is a temporal phrase 'a minute ago' (назад = 'ago'); зажал is past perfective of зажать meaning 'pressed/clenched' or 'held tightly' (here 'pressed into her hand'); в ее руке = 'in her hand' (в + locative руке). The clause as a whole: 'which I had pressed into her hand a minute before.'
segments.p2-10-g172: Это была is a past-tense copular identification ('this/it was'); та бумажка is the demonstrative (ta = 'that') + noun 'paper/note' in nominative, identifying the object. The semicolon separates the emphatic follow-up statements.
segments.p2-10-g173: Другой = 'another/other' (masculine/neutral form used generically). И here functions as an emphatic 'even' / 'indeed'. Быть не могло is an impersonal past construction meaning 'could not be' / 'it was impossible': могло is past neuter of мочь used impersonally. Altogether: 'there could be no other.'
segments.p2-10-g174: Другой (another) + и (even) again provides emphasis; в доме = 'in the house' (в + locative доме); не было is the negative past existential 'there was not'. The sentence reiterates emphatically that no other existed in the household.
segments.p2-10-g175: Она is the nominative pronoun 'she' (subject). It begins the clause and is set off by commas for emphasis.
segments.p2-10-g176: Стало быть is a fixed phrase meaning 'therefore' / 'so' / 'it follows that'. Grammatically it's composed of стало (past of стать) + быть, but functions as a discourse connective rather than literal 'became to be'.
segments.p2-10-g177: Успела is past feminine of успеть meaning 'managed (in time)' or 'had time to'; it is followed by the infinitive выбросить 'to throw (out)'. Ее is the object pronoun 'it/her' — here referring to the note (feminine), so it's an object pronoun. Из руки = 'out of (her) hand' (из + genitive), на стол = 'onto the table' (на + accusative/locative depending on nuance), and в ту минуту = 'at that very minute' (tu = demonstrative). The sequence expresses that she managed to throw it onto the table at that precise moment.
segments.p2-10-g178: Когда introduces a temporal subordinate clause 'when'; я is 'I'; отскочил is past tense perfective of отскочить meaning 'to spring back / jump back'; в другой угол is a motion phrase with в + accusative 'into the other corner'. The whole subordinate clause gives the exact time of the action: 'when I sprang back into the other corner.'
segments.p2-10-g184: 'Я' is the subject 'I'. 'до того' is an adverbial phrase meaning 'to such an extent' or 'so (much)'; here it qualifies the predicate. 'был эгоист' is a past-tense copular clause: 'was a selfish person'.
segments.p2-10-g185: Again 'до того' marks degree ('so much'). 'не уважал' is the negated past imperfective 'did not respect' (habitual/general attitude). 'людей' is the direct object 'people' (plural). 'на самом деле' is a fixed phrase 'in fact / in reality' modifying the whole clause.
segments.p2-10-g186: 'что' introduces the result/consequence clause. 'даже и' is an emphatic combination meaning 'even'. 'вообразить' is the infinitive 'to imagine', and 'мог' is the past tense of 'can' ('could'): together 'вообразить не мог' = 'could not imagine.'
segments.p2-10-g187: This is the subordinate clause that gives the content of what he couldn't imagine. The second 'что' is another 'that'. 'и' here functions as 'even' / 'too' (emphatic), so 'и она' = 'even she' or 'she too'. 'это' is the demonstrative object 'this/that', and 'сделает' is the perfective future 'will do' (he couldn't imagine that she would do it).
segments.p2-10-g188: 'Этого' is the demonstrative pronoun in the genitive ('of this/that') referring back to the preceding idea; in negative constructions Russian often uses the genitive. 'я' is the subject. 'не вынес' is past tense of 'вынести' meaning 'could not endure / could not bear.'
segments.p2-10-g189: 'Мгновение спустя' is an adverbial time phrase meaning 'a moment later'; 'спустя' governs the preceding noun to express 'after (a time)'. 'я' is the subject starting the main clause.
segments.p2-10-g190: 'как безумный' is a simile 'like/as a madman' modifying the subject's manner; 'как' = 'as/like' and 'безумный' describes his frantic state ('mad' or 'frenzied').
segments.p2-10-g191: 'бросился' is the past reflexive/perfective 'threw himself' used idiomatically with an infinitive to mean 'hurried to' or 'rushed to'. 'одеваться' is the imperfective infinitive 'to get dressed' (he hurriedly began getting dressed).
segments.p2-10-g192: 'накинул на себя' is a short perfective action 'threw on over himself' (put on a garment quickly). 'на' is the preposition 'on/onto' and 'себя' is the reflexive accusative 'himself'.
segments.p2-10-g193: 'что' here means 'what' / 'that which' (i.e., 'whatever'). 'успел' is past of 'успеть' meaning 'managed (in time)'. 'впопыхах' is an adverb 'in a rush', so the phrase means 'whatever I managed in my haste.'
segments.p2-10-g194: 'и' links the final action ('and'). 'стремглав' is an adverb 'headlong'. 'выбежал' is past 'ran out' / 'darted out'. 'за' with the dative pronoun 'ней' expresses movement 'after her' (he ran out to follow her).
segments.p2-10-g195: 'Она' is the subject 'she'. The particle 'и' here functions as an intensifier meaning 'even' ('not even two hundred steps'). 'двухсот шагов' is a numeral phrase in the genitive ('two hundred steps' — genitive used after 'и' + negative). 'еще не успела уйти' means 'had not yet managed to go/leave'.
segments.p2-10-g196: 'когда' introduces the temporal clause 'when'. 'я' is subject. 'выбежал' is past 'ran out'. 'на улицу' is a movement phrase with 'на' ('onto/into') and 'улицу' accusative 'the street' — 'I ran out into the street.'
segments.p2-10-g272: This is a single clause: 'Никогда больше' = 'never again' (adverbial), 'я' is the subject, and the two coordinated predicates are 'не встречал Лизу' (past tense, masculine, 'I did not meet/see Liza') and 'и ничего не слыхал о ней' ('and I heard nothing about her'). Word order is standard S V O; the double negative structure ('ничего' with 'не') is normal in Russian to express 'heard nothing.'
segments.p2-10-g273: 'Прибавлю' is 1st person singular future (perfective) of 'прибавить' and here functions as 'I will add' (spoken addition). 'тоже' is an adverb meaning 'also/too' and simply modifies the verb.
segments.p2-10-g274: This is the content clause introduced by 'что' ('that'). 'Я надолго остался доволен фразой' = 'I for a long time remained satisfied with the phrase'; 'надолго' is a duration adverb, 'остался' is past tense 'remained,' and 'доволен' is a predicative adjective taking the instrumental 'фразой' ('with the phrase'). The phrase 'о пользе от оскорбления и ненависти' is a prepositional phrase 'about the benefit of insult and hatred.'
segments.p2-10-g275: This is the fixed connector 'несмотря на то,' which combines 'несмотря' + preposition 'на' + demonstrative 'то' to prepare the concessive clause that follows; as a whole it means 'despite (the fact).' It will be completed by the following 'что...'.
segments.p2-10-g276: 'что' introduces the subordinate clause giving the concession. 'сам' is emphatic ('I myself'), 'чуть не' is an idiom meaning 'almost,' and 'заболел' is past tense 'fell ill' (literal here). 'тогда' = 'then,' and 'от тоски' means 'from/with melancholy.' The clause reads 'that I myself almost became ill then from melancholy.'
segments.p2-10-g277: 'Даже и теперь' is an emphatic time phrase: 'даже' = 'even,' 'и' here adds emphasis ('even/indeed'), and 'теперь' = 'now.' Together they mean 'even now.'
segments.p2-10-g278: 'через столько лет' is a time expression meaning 'after so many years' or 'through so many years'; 'через' with an accusative time phrase indicates a period elapsing ('in/after (a period)'), and 'лет' is the genitive plural of 'год' used after 'столько' ('so many years').
segments.p2-10-g279: Subject 'всё это' ('all this') followed by adverbial modifiers 'как-то' ('somehow') and 'слишком' ('too/excessively') that modify 'нехорошо' ('unpleasantly/wrongly'). The verb 'мне припоминается' is impersonal/experiential: 'припоминается' takes the dative 'мне' (to me) and means 'is recalled to me' or 'comes to mind for me.' The whole clause: 'all this somehow comes back to me too unpleasantly.'
segments.p2-10-g280: 'Многое' is a neuter pronoun meaning 'many things' (the subject). 'мне' is the dative experiencer, 'теперь' = 'now,' and 'нехорошо припоминается' = 'is recalled to me unpleasantly' (same impersonal/experiential construction as above). Word order places the dative before the verb for emphasis.
segments.p2-10-g281: A single coordinating conjunction 'но' meaning 'but' or 'however,' left trailing to indicate a break or contrast; here it ends the sentence abruptly, implying continuation or an unspoken reservation.
segments.p2-10-g222: Разве is a particle introducing a rhetorical question (implying doubt or incredulity). дам is the simple future, 1st person singular of дать ('to give'); the normal order 'я дам ей счастье' is inverted here after Разве for emphasis. ей is dative 'to her', and счастье is the direct object 'happiness'.
segments.p2-10-g223: Разве begins a rhetorical question; я is the subject. не узнал is the past tense, masculine singular of узнать with negation ('did not learn/recognize'). сегодня опять places time adverbials 'today' and 'again' (опять stresses repetition).
segments.p2-10-g224: в + ordinal + раз (в сотый раз) is an idiomatic time expression meaning 'for the hundredth time'; сотый is an ordinal adjective and раз here means 'occurrence/time'. The phrase is parenthetical, emphasizing repetition.
segments.p2-10-g225: цены is the genitive of цена used idiomatically with узнать to mean 'learn/realize the value (of)'; себе ('to oneself') indicates the target of that value — 'one's own value'. The word order is idiomatic: 'узнал цены себе' = 'recognized my own worth.'
segments.p2-10-g226: Разве again marks a rhetorical question/exclamation; а here is a colloquial connective adding emphasis/contrast. не + замучу forms a rhetorical negative question with замучу = 1st person singular future ('I will torment/torment her'), and ее is the object pronoun 'her'. The tone is emphatic/exclamatory rather than a simple question.
segments.p2-10-g227: Я is the subject; стоял is the past tense, masculine singular of стоять ('stood'). на снегу uses the locative construction (на + снегу) meaning 'on the snow' to indicate location.
segments.p2-10-g228: всматриваясь is a present adverbial participle (деепричастие) of всматриваться meaning 'peering/straining to see'; it takes в + accusative (в мутную мглу) here, so мутную (accusative feminine) and мглу (accusative of мгла) render 'into the murky gloom'.
segments.p2-10-g229: и is the coordinating conjunction 'and'; думал is past tense masculine singular of думать ('thought'); об этом is the prepositional phrase 'about this' (об used before a vowel form of the pronoun это).
segments.p2-10-g284: This is a colloquial, rhetorical suggestion built around the negated infinitive + question particle: 'не кончить ли' = 'shall I not finish / why not finish'. 'не' negates the infinitive; 'кончить' is an infinitive meaning 'to finish' (perfective); the enclitic particle 'ли' turns it into a tentative/questioning suggestion. 'уж' is an emphatic particle meaning 'already/at this point', and 'тут' = 'here/at this point' (locative). 'Записки' is the direct object (title in quotes), same form as nominative but understood as the thing to be finished; the whole is a rhetorical question proposing to stop the work now.
segments.p2-10-g285: 'Мне кажется' is an impersonal perception phrase meaning 'it seems to me' or 'I think so'; 'мне' is a dative experiencer ('to me') and 'кажется' is the present tense impersonal form of 'казаться' ('seem'), the clause-signal that what follows is the speaker's opinion.
segments.p2-10-g286: Straightforward main clause: subject pronoun 'я' + past tense 'сделал' (perfective past 'made/did') + direct object 'ошибку' in accusative — 'I made a mistake.' The past tense here states a completed action.
segments.p2-10-g287: 'начав' is a perfective adverbial participle (деепричастие) from 'начать' meaning 'having begun'; it introduces the circumstance ('having begun to write them'). It governs the infinitive 'писать' (to write) and the clitic object 'их' ('them'), so the phrase literally reads 'having begun to write them' and modifies the main clause (explaining the mistaken act).
segments.p2-10-g288: 'По крайней мере' is a fixed discourse phrase meaning 'at least / at any rate'. The clause 'мне было стыдно' is an impersonal past construction ('it was shameful to me' → 'I was ashamed'), where 'мне' is the dative experiencer and 'было' is past neuter of 'быть' used impersonally; 'стыдно' is a predicative adjective meaning 'ashamed/embarrassed.'
segments.p2-10-g289: 'все время как' = 'the whole time that' (temporal clause). 'все время' = 'all the time/throughout', 'как' here introduces the subordinate temporal clause meaning 'as/when' or 'that' in the sense 'while', and 'я писал эту повесть' is the imperfective past narrative 'I was writing this tale' (ongoing action); 'эту повесть' is the direct object in accusative.
segments.p2-10-g290: 'стало быть' is an idiomatic connective meaning 'therefore/so/it follows that' formed from past neuter 'стало' + infinitive 'быть' but functioning as a discourse marker introducing the inference or conclusion.
segments.p2-10-g291: Predicate clause with demonstrative subject 'это' ('this/it') and predicate 'уж не литература' = 'is certainly/not anymore literature.' 'уж' is an emphatic particle ('already/indeed'), and the negation 'не' negates the noun predicate 'литература'.
segments.p2-10-g292: Contrastive clause introduced by 'а' ('but/rather'), completing the thought with the nominal phrase 'исправительное наказание' in the nominative — 'corrective punishment' (a noun phrase serving as the predicate complement to the previous demonstrative). No verb is repeated; the meaning is 'it is not literature but corrective punishment.'
segments.p2-10-g293: Ведь is a discourse particle ('after all/you know') drawing attention to what follows. Рассказывать is an infinitive used as the verbal noun/topic ('to tell' or 'telling...') — the clause begins to consider the act of telling such stories.
segments.p2-10-g294: Например is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'for example', setting off an illustrative instance of the kind of telling just mentioned.
segments.p2-10-g295: Длинные повести is a noun phrase 'long tales/novellas'; о том is a standard connective meaning 'about that' and introduces the subordinate clause that will explain what the long tales are about (cf. 'о том, как...').
segments.p2-10-g296: Как introduces the content clause 'how/that I ...'. Манкировал is past imperfective of манкировать ('shirked/evaded/neglected'), describing how the narrator treated his life. Свою жизнь is literal 'my life'; нравственным растлением is an instrumental phrase meaning 'with/into moral corruption' (describing the manner/result), and в углу 'in a corner' is locative, idiomatic for living in a tucked-away, marginal way.
segments.p2-10-g297: Недостатком среды uses the instrumental case to list causes or contributing factors: 'by the lack/deficiency of the milieu' (i.e. inadequate social environment).
segments.p2-10-g298: Отвычкой от живого and тщеславной злобой в подполье continue the list of causes in the instrumental: 'by a loss of habit of the living' (i.e. becoming unaccustomed to real life) and 'by vain malice in the underground.' В подполье here is the figurative 'underground' (not just cellar) — the narrator's subterranean life.
segments.p2-10-g299: Ей‑богу is a colloquial oath ('by God' / 'honestly, I swear'), here parenthetical; grammatically it's an interjection formed from the dative pronoun and бог.
segments.p2-10-g300: Не интересно is an impersonal predicative expression meaning 'it's not interesting' or 'it's boring' — не negates the adjective used adverbially/predicatively.
segments.p2-10-g301: В романе надо героя is an impersonal construction: 'in a novel one needs a hero' — надо + accusative expresses necessity ('a novel needs a hero').
segments.p2-10-g302: А тут нарочно собраны все черты для антигероя: a contrast 'but here' (а тут), нарочно 'on purpose/deliberately', and собраны is a short passive participle meaning 'are gathered/collected' (predicate with все черты as subject): 'all the traits for an antihero have been deliberately collected.'
segments.p2-10-g303: А главное is a connective phrase 'and (the) main thing' or 'and above all' introducing the most important point to follow.
segments.p2-10-g304: Все это произведет пренеприятное впечатление: 'all this' (все это) is the subject; произведет is perfective future 3rd person singular of произвести ('will produce/cause'); пренеприятное впечатление is the object 'a very unpleasant impression.'
