segments.p2-10-g305: Потому что introduces a causal clause 'because'. Мы все отвыкли от жизни: отвыкли is perfective past plural meaning 'have become unaccustomed' (not simply 'gave up' — here 'lost the habit of'), so 'we have all become unaccustomed to life.'
segments.p2-10-g306: Все хромаем is present-tense plural 'we all limp' (хромаем), used figuratively: 'we're all lame/defective' — a metaphor for being impaired in life.
segments.p2-10-g307: Всякий более или менее is a short concluding comment: всякий ('each/every person') + более или менее ('more or less') = 'each one, more or less.' It's colloquial and elliptic.
segments.p2-10-g400: Архипова is a feminine surname in the nominative used here in a surname-first bibliographic listing; the trailing comma separates the family name from the following initial. This is a conventional printed form: 'Surname, ...'.
segments.p2-10-g401: Н. is a single-letter abbreviation (capital letter plus period) representing the person's given name (or given-name initial); it follows the comma after the surname. The period marks the abbreviation and there is no inflection on initials.
segments.p2-10-g402: Ф. is another single-letter initial with a period, standing for a given name or a patronymic; here it appears alone (likely due to line breaks or listing), and functions as an abbreviated name element. As an initial it carries no grammatical case marking.
segments.p2-10-g403: Буданова is a feminine surname presented surname-first; the comma separates it from the initial that follows. The form is nominative and unchanged because this is a name listing rather than a grammatical phrase.
segments.p2-10-g404: Е. is the capital-letter abbreviation for a given name (or initial) followed by a period; here it stands immediately after the comma that follows the surname. Abbreviated initials do not inflect and are standard in bibliographic/name lists.
segments.p2-10-g405: И. is an initial (capital letter plus period) representing a given name or patronymic; appearing alone, it likely completes a multipart set of initials for a person. As with other initials, it is an abbreviation and not grammatically inflected.
segments.p2-10-g389: Он is the nominative subject 'he'. не выдержал is past tense, perfective (выдержать) with the negative particle не — here meaning 'didn't/ couldn't endure' or 'couldn't bear it' (a completed failure to endure). и coordinates the clause with the following verb. продолжал is past tense, imperfective (продолжать) indicating a continued action that followed, and далее is an adverb 'further/onward' modifying продолжал. The contrast of perfective (не выдержал) then imperfective (продолжал) signals that after failing to restrain himself he went on continuing.
segments.p2-10-g390: Но is the adversative conjunction 'but'. нам is the dative pronoun 'to us' and combines with кажется to form the impersonal construction 'it seems to us' (нам кажется). тоже 'also' adds that the feeling is shared or additional. кажется is 3rd person singular present of казаться, used impersonally here.
segments.p2-10-g391: что is the subordinating conjunction 'that' introducing the content clause. здесь is the adverb 'here' (in this place/context). можно is an impersonal modal ('it is possible' / 'one may'); with an infinitive it yields permission or possibility. и here functions as a particle giving the infinitive a slight concessive/emphatic sense ('even' / 'also') rather than the simple coordinating 'and'. остановиться is the perfective infinitive 'to stop' (to cease or pause).
segments.p2-10-g392: из is the preposition 'from' requiring the genitive. Собрания сочинений is a genitive noun phrase 'of the Collected Works' (Собрания 'collection' in genitive modifying сочинений 'works' in genitive plural). в 15-ти томах uses the preposition в with the prepositional case томах 'in volumes'; 15-ти is the numeral written in old/typical printed form meaning 'fifteen' and agrees with томах to indicate the number of volumes.
segments.p2-10-g393: Т. is an abbreviation (т. or Т.) for том 'volume' used bibliographically; it stands alone as 'Vol.' in English. It's a conventional short form, not a full word in this line.
segments.p2-10-g230: 'И' is a connective particle ('and' / rhetorical 'well'); the phrase 'не лучше ль' is a negative interrogative formula meaning 'wouldn't it be better'. 'ль' is the enclitic interrogative particle (short form of 'ли') attached to the comparative 'лучше' (here functioning as 'better'). The whole fragment is an elliptical rhetorical opening to a suggestion.
segments.p2-10-g231: Repeats the core of the rhetorical question: 'не лучше ль будет' — 'будет' is 3rd person singular of 'быть' in the future form but here serves in the idiom 'не лучше ли будет...' meaning 'would it not be better'. The particle 'ль' again marks the interrogative/conditional flavor.
segments.p2-10-g232: 'Фантазировал' is the past tense, imperfective, first-person verb meaning 'I was fantasizing' or 'I fantasized', and the subject 'я' follows the verb for emphasis (inversion). 'Уже дома' is a temporal/location phrase 'already at home'. The dash at the start marks reported thought.
segments.p2-10-g233: 'После' here is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'afterwards' or 'later', marking the time when the fantasizing took place; the comma shows it is an aside modifying the previous clause.
segments.p2-10-g234: 'Заглушая' is a gerund (деепричастие) meaning 'drowning out' or 'stifling' and indicates a concurrent action; the instrument 'фантазиями' (instrumental plural) shows the means ('with fantasies'). 'Живую' and 'сердечную' are accusative adjectives agreeing with 'боль' and mean 'vivid/acute' and 'heartfelt' respectively: 'raw, heartfelt pain'.
segments.p2-10-g235: A repeat of the rhetorical formula 'не лучше ль будет' reinforcing the thought; same grammar as earlier: negative interrogative with 'ль' and 3rd-person 'будет' giving the sense 'wouldn't it be better'.
segments.p2-10-g236: 'Если' introduces the conditional clause ('if'). 'Она' is the feminine subject ('she'). 'Унесет' is the perfective future of 'унести' — simple future 'will carry away' (i.e. remove entirely). Adverbs 'навеки' ('forever') and 'теперь' ('now/from now on') modify the verb; 'с собой' ('with her') indicates that she takes it away with her (instrumental reflexive idiom). 'Оскорбление' is the direct object 'the insult'.
segments.p2-10-g237: A one-word exclamation in nominative: 'Оскорбление' is presented as the topic/subject ('The insult...') — an emphatic, appositive opening to the speaker's reflection.
segments.p2-10-g238: 'Да ведь' is an emphatic conversational formula ('why/indeed/after all'); 'это' is the demonstrative pronoun 'this/it', and 'очищение' is the predicate noun 'purification' (nominative). The clause asserts that an insult functions as a kind of purification.
segments.p2-10-g239: 'Это самое' = 'this very' (the emphatic demonstrative). 'Едкое' ('biting' or 'caustic') and 'больное' ('painful' / 'aching') are neuter adjectives agreeing with 'сознание' (neuter noun) and describe the kind of consciousness the insult produces: a sharp, painful awareness.
segments.p2-10-g240: 'Завтра же' places the action in the immediate future with emphasis ('tomorrow' / 'and tomorrow for sure'); 'же' is an emphatic particle. 'Я бы загрязнил' uses the particle 'бы' with the past-form verb to create a conditional/modal meaning ('I would sully'). 'Собой' (instrumental) indicates the agent by which the soul is sullied — 'by my presence'/'with myself'. The coordinate verb 'утомил' continues the past-form action in the same hypothetical ('and [I] would have tired her heart').
segments.p2-10-g241: 'А' is contrastive ('but'); 'оскорбление' is the subject. 'Не замрет' is future perfective (from 'замереть') with negation meaning 'will not cease' or 'will not die away'. 'В ней' ('in her') locates where it persists; 'теперь никогда' ('now never') intensifies the permanence.
segments.p2-10-g242: A concessive clause introduced by 'и как бы ни' + past tense 'была': 'и как бы ни была гадка грязь' = 'and however nasty the filth may be'. The construction 'как бы ни' + past marks 'no matter how...' and 'грязь' is the feminine noun 'filth', described as 'гадка' (nasty).
segments.p2-10-g243: 'Которая' is the feminine relative pronoun agreeing with 'грязь' and begins the relative clause 'that awaits her'. 'Ее' is the object 'her', and 'ожидает' is present tense 'awaits' or 'is in store for'.
segments.p2-10-g244: Restatement of the main idea: subject 'оскорбление' with two perfective future verbs 'возвысит' ('will exalt') and 'очистит' ('will cleanse/purify'), coordinated by 'и', and the object 'ее' ('her'). The verbs express the speaker's conviction that the insult will both elevate and purify her.
segments.p2-10-g361: Ведь is a discourse particle meaning "after all" that frames the statement as a reminder or appeal; мы даже не знаем is a simple present-tense clause ('we don't even know') with даже ('even') adding emphasis before the negative не + verb знать.
segments.p2-10-g362: This is a coordinate indirect question: где introduces a relative-place clause "where...lives"; the particle и here has an emphatic/augmentative sense ('even/also') and живое-то uses the noun/adj 'живое' ("the living [thing/person]") with the enclitic -то as an emphatic particle. Живет is 3rd-person singular present (lives), теперь is the adverb 'now', the second и simply links the next clause, and что оно такое is a copular-style nominal question ('what it is' literally 'what such it is') with такое functioning as predicate 'such/what sort'.
segments.p2-10-g363: Как is the interrogative 'how' used here in the fixed question "what is it called"; называeтся is the reflexive/passive-voice verb 'is called' (3rd-person singular present), so the two together ask for the name/label of the thing.
segments.p2-10-g364: Оставьте is the polite/plural imperative 'leave' (perfective) addressed to someone; нас одних is the accusative phrase 'us alone' (одних stresses 'alone/only us'). Together it's an imperative plea: 'Leave us by ourselves.'
segments.p2-10-g365: без takes the genitive, so без книжки ('without a book'); книжки is a colloquial/diminutive genitive form of книжка 'book', giving a slightly familiar or dismissive tone.
segments.p2-10-g366: и links this result to the previous command ('and'), мы is the repeated subject for emphasis, тотчас is an adverb 'immediately', and запутаемся is a perfective 1st-person-plural future/reflexive verb meaning 'we will become confused / entangled' (i.e., 'we'll get confused').
segments.p2-10-g367: Потеряемся is another perfective 1st-person-plural future reflexive verb 'we will get lost/lose our way'; it's coordinated as the next immediate consequence.
segments.p2-10-g368: This is a future negative periphrastic clause: не (not) + будем (1st-person-plural future auxiliary of быть) + знать (infinitive) = 'we will not know.' This construction emphasizes the continuing state of ignorance that follows the previous verbs.
segments.p2-10-g369: куда is an interrogative adverb 'to where' introducing an indirect question; примкнуть is an infinitive perfective verb 'to attach oneself/join', so the phrase functions as one of the things we 'won't know' — i.e. 'where to join/attach ourselves.'
segments.p2-10-g370: чего is the genitive form of что used after the verb придержаться (which governs genitive); придержаться is an infinitive 'to adhere to/hold on to', so this asks 'what to hold on to' as another object of 'не будем знать.'
segments.p2-10-g371: A pair of parallel infinitival objects introduced by repeated что ('what'): что любить 'what to love' and что ненавидеть 'what to hate'. The infinitives serve as complements of знать in the earlier clause (what we will not know).
segments.p2-10-g372: Another parallel pair in the same structure: что уважать 'what to respect' and что презирать 'what to despise'. Again, repeated что introduces each nominalized question expressed by infinitives that are objects of 'не будем знать.'
segments.p2-10-g373: Мы (first-person plural) is the subject; даже и is a doubled emphatic particle combination meaning 'even' (и here strengthens даже). Человеками is the instrumental plural 'as human beings' and the particle -то after it (человеками-то) gives a contrastive/emphatic shade ('precisely as humans' / 'as humans, of all things'). Быть is the infinitive 'to be' and тяготимся (1st pl. present reflexive) means 'we feel burdened/reluctant' — the construction тяготиться + infinitive expresses reluctance to assume that state.
segments.p2-10-g374: This is a continuation/clarification: человеками repeats the instrumental plural 'as human beings'; с is the preposition 'with'; настоящий is an adjective in the instrumental (настоящим) meaning 'real/genuine' and stands before a noun that is postponed to the next segment (i.e. 'with a real...').
segments.p2-10-g375: Собственным is an adjective in the instrumental agreeing with the following noun and means 'own'; телом is the instrumental singular 'body' (literal physical body); и connects the coordinated instrumental noun кровью 'blood' — together 'with their own body and blood' (instrumental used after с to indicate possession/ accompaniment).
segments.p2-10-g376: Стыдимся is 1st person plural present of стыдиться (reflexive) meaning 'we are ashamed' or 'we are embarrassed'; этого is the genitive demonstrative pronoun 'of this/that' — стыдиться typically takes a genitive object, so 'we are ashamed of that.'
segments.p2-10-g377: Считать за (here за + noun) functions as 'to consider as' — за is the preposition used in the idiom считать за; позор is the noun 'disgrace/shame' and is what they 'count as' (object of считать за). И links the second verb clause: норовим (1st pl. present of норовить) means 'we are inclined/are apt' or 'we try/endeavour' and is followed by the infinitive быть 'to be'. Какими‑то is an indefinite phrase ('some kind of') formed with the particle -то giving vagueness; небывалыми and общечеловеками are both in the instrumental plural agreeing as predicative complements of быть — небывалый 'unprecedented' (or 'extraordinary') modifying общечеловеками, a coined/compound plural 'all-human' or 'general-human' types (stylistically marked).
segments.p2-10-g378: Мы is the plural pronoun 'we' used generically to include the narrator and others. Мертворожденные is an adjective/participle used predicatively (no copula) meaning literally 'stillborn' but here used figuratively — 'born dead' or 'doomed from birth' — agreeing in number with мы.
segments.p2-10-g379: Да и is a conjunctive particle combination meaning 'and besides' / 'moreover' (да in the sense 'and'); рождаемся is present tense 1st-person-plural reflexive verb 'we are born' (habitual/ongoing); -то after the verb (рождаемся-то) is an emphatic particle adding contrast or insistence. Давно уж is an adverbial cluster 'for a long time already'; не от живых отцов is a negative origin-expression with от + genitive ('not from living fathers'), where живых and отцов are genitive to indicate source.
segments.p2-10-g380: И это нам ... нравится is a simple clause meaning 'and this pleases us' (нравится is 3rd-person-singular 'to please', with the experiencer in the dative нам). Всё более и более is a repetitive adverbial phrase 'more and more' / 'increasingly', intensifying нравится.
segments.p2-10-g394: A standalone numeral with a period used as an item or note number; no grammatical structure to analyze beyond its function as a label.
segments.p2-10-g395: "Л." is an abbreviation of лист (leaf/page) used in bibliographic or editorial notation; the capital letter plus period marks it as an abbreviation rather than a full word, and it stands alone as a label (no inflection here).
segments.p2-10-g396: A comma serving as a punctuation separator between bibliographic elements; it does not carry grammatical content itself.
segments.p2-10-g397: Наука in quotation marks is the title/name of the publisher (Nauka); the quotes signal a proper title and the trailing comma separates this element from the year that follows.
segments.p2-10-g398: 1989 is the publication year; "Составители примечаний" is a nominative plural noun phrase meaning "compilers of the annotations" (примечаний is genitive plural governed by составители). "к тому" uses the dative тому (= "to that [volume]") to indicate the object of those annotations, and the following name is given in apposition (nominative) as the person responsible. This is a compact bibliographic style that omits a copula (e.g. "are").
segments.p2-10-g399: A single capital letter with a period, functioning as an initial or abbreviated element (likely an initial of a name or volume marker) in the bibliographic line; it is an abbreviation marker rather than an inflected word.
segments.p2-10-g308: Даже (an emphatic adverb) + до того (a fixed prepositional phrase meaning 'to such an extent') together intensify the past-predicate отвыкли (past plural of отвыкнуть). The whole unit functions as a fronted, concise clause 'we have even become so unaccustomed'.
segments.p2-10-g309: Что introduces a result/complement clause. Чувствуем is present plural 'we feel'; подчас is an adverb 'sometimes' modifying the verb; к governs the dative phrase настоящей "живой жизни" ('toward real "living life"'), where настоящей is an attributive adjective and живой emphasizes immediacy/realness. Какое-то is an indefinite modifier ('some kind of') with омерзение as the direct object 'disgust'.
segments.p2-10-g310: А потому is a connective meaning 'and/for that reason' leading to the consequence; и here adds emphasis ('even/also'); терпеть не можем is a fixed idiom with the modal можем (present plural) and the infinitive терпеть meaning 'cannot tolerate' or 'cannot bear'.
segments.p2-10-g311: Когда begins a subordinate temporal clause 'when/whenever'; нам is dative 'to us'; напоминают is 3rd person plural present 'they remind' (an impersonal/general 'people remind us'); про нее is a prepositional phrase 'about it/her', referring back to the 'living life' just mentioned.
segments.p2-10-g312: Ведь is an emphatic discourse particle 'after all'; мы is the subject; до того дошли is an idiom with дошли (past plural of дойти) meaning 'have come to the point' or 'have reached such a stage'. The phrase sets up the following 'что' clause.
segments.p2-10-g313: Что introduces the content clause. Настоящую "живую жизнь" is the accusative direct object ('real "living life"'); чуть не is an adverbial cluster meaning 'almost' that modifies считаем (present 'we consider'); за + noun here means 'as/regarded as' and труд is the noun 'labor/work'.
segments.p2-10-g314: Почти что is a reinforcing pair meaning 'almost/virtually'; за службу repeats the pattern 'as a service/duty' with службу in the accusative. The phrase intensifies the previous 'чуть не считаем за труд' idea.
segments.p2-10-g315: И все мы 'and all of us' is followed by про себя, an idiom meaning 'to ourselves' or 'privately,' and согласны is a short-form adjective 'are agreed' = 'agree'. Word order places the emphasis on the collective private agreement.
segments.p2-10-g316: Что begins a subordinate clause giving the content of that private agreement. По книжке is an adverbial expression 'according to the book' or 'by the book' (по + dative/locative form книжке), and лучше is the comparative 'better' asserting the preference.
segments.p2-10-g317: И is a simple connective 'and'; чего is colloquial/exclamative 'what/why' (here implying 'for what reason'); копошимся is a reflexive present 'we fidget/rummage' with the subject мы placed after the verb for emphasis; иногда is the adverb 'sometimes'.
segments.p2-10-g318: Чего again functions as an exclamative 'what/why'; блажим is a colloquial verb meaning 'we act foolishly' or 'play the holy fool' (present plural). The short clause is elliptical and rhetorical.
segments.p2-10-g319: Чего as before ('what/what for'); просим is present plural 'we ask/request' — a brief direct question asking what it is that we request or desire.
segments.p2-10-g320: Сами is an emphatic reflexive 'ourselves' (emphasizing that the speaker/group are the ones who don't know); не знаем is present 'we don't know'; чего is colloquial/exclamative 'what' (the clause is elliptical: 'we ourselves don't know what').
segments.p2-10-g381: This short clause uses 'во' (a variant of the preposition 'в') before a consonant cluster for euphony and means 'into/into the state of.' 'Вкус' is a noun here meaning 'taste' or 'a liking' (accusative/nominative form looks identical) and is the object/goal of entering. 'Входим' is first-person plural present of входить (imperfective), used figuratively as 'we enter / we come into,' so the whole phrase means 'we are coming into a taste' → 'we're getting a taste for it.'
segments.p2-10-g382: Скоро is an adverb 'soon' modifying the main verb. Выдумаем is first-person plural future of выдумать (perfective) meaning 'we will think up / invent / imagine' — a single, completed future action; the following infinitive рождаться is the thing to be contrived. Рождаться is the infinitive 'to be born' (reflexive form indicating the passive/process). Как-нибудь is a compound meaning 'somehow / in some way' (как = 'how',-нибудь a generalizing/indefinite particle). От is the preposition 'from' taking the genitive, and идеи is the genitive singular of идея 'idea.' Grammatically: 'we will think up (выдумаем) to be born (рождаться) somehow/from an idea.'
segments.p2-10-g383: Но is the conjunction 'but/however.' Довольно here functions as an interjectional adverb meaning 'enough' or 'that's enough' (not merely 'quite'); together the phrase interrupts the speaker to stop the current line of thought.
segments.p2-10-g384: Не is the sentential negation for the verb хочу. Хочу is first-person singular present 'I want' (here negated: 'I do not want'). The pronoun я follows the verb for emphasis/stylistic effect but remains the subject. Больше in negative constructions means 'no longer' (not 'more'). Писать is the infinitive 'to write' and serves as the complement of хочу. Из governs the genitive Подполья — here the quoted phrase is the title/phrase 'из Подполья' ('from the Underground'), so the speaker says he no longer wants to write those pieces.
segments.p2-10-g387: Впрочем is a discourse particle meaning 'however' / 'besides' that signals a qualification or correction of the previous statement; it links the next clause to what was just said.
segments.p2-10-g388: Здесь is an adverb 'here' (in this place/context) and еще 'still/yet' indicates continuation; не negates the verb. Кончаются is third-person plural present of кончаться 'to end / to come to an end' and agrees with the nominative plural subject записки ('notes' / 'writings'). Это-го + парадоксалиста is a genitive phrase 'of this paradoxalist' showing possession: 'the notes of this paradoxalist are not finished here yet.' Word order places the adverbials before the predicate for emphasis.
segments.p2-10-g348: Позвольте is the polite imperative (plural/formal) of позволить meaning 'allow' or 'permit'; here it functions as a brief request 'allow me' or 'permit me to say'.
segments.p2-10-g349: Господа is the vocative/invocative plural 'gentlemen' (lit. 'sirs'); it's an address to the audience and does not change the clause's grammar.
segments.p2-10-g350: This clause is a topical comment: ведь 'after all' gives a justificatory tone; не + оправдываюсь (first person, present, reflexive) = 'I am not making excuses'; же is an emphatic particle reinforcing 'I am not'; я is the subject 'I'; этим is the demonstrative in the instrumental 'by this'; всемством is an instrumental noun meaning 'the collective/allness' (literally 'this whole body/we-ness'). Together: 'after all I am not excusing myself by this collective "we".'
segments.p2-10-g351: Что... касается is a topicalizing construction meaning 'as for...' or 'with regard to...'; же adds emphasis; собственно ('strictly/actually') narrows the focus; до меня ('as to me') uses до + genitive to mark the topic; касается is present 3rd-singular 'concerns' (it agrees with 'что' as a dummy subject here).
segments.p2-10-g352: То is a correlative particle linking this result to what follows ('then/so'); ведь 'after all' reiterates the speaker's tone; я is the subject 'I'; только 'only/merely' restricts the action; доводил is past imperfective (masculine) meaning 'I used to bring/push (something) to a state' — here 'pushed to an extreme'; в моей жизни is a temporal/place phrase 'in my life'; до крайности is the phrase 'to the extreme' (до + noun in genitive); the final то anticipates the subordinate clause that follows ('that which...' — correlative to the first то).
segments.p2-10-g353: Что introduces the relative clause 'that which...'; вы is the addressed 'you' (plural/formal); не осмеливались is the past imperfective reflexive 'did not dare' (you didn't venture); доводить is the infinitive 'to bring/drive (to a state)' repeating the verb from the main clause; и here functions as an intensifier 'even/also'; до половины means 'up to half' (до + genitive).
segments.p2-10-g354: Да еще is a colloquial linking phrase meaning 'and moreover' or 'and what's more'; трусость свою is the accusative 'your own cowardice' (свою refers back to вы); принимали is past plural 'took/regarded (something) as' and combines with за + accusative (за благоразумие) to mean 'regarded as'; благоразумие is the noun 'prudence/good sense'.
segments.p2-10-g355: И тем utешались: и 'and'; тем is ablative/instrumental demonstrative 'by that' or 'with that' indicating the means; утешались is past plural reflexive 'comforted themselves' — 'they comforted themselves by that.'
segments.p2-10-g356: Обманывая is a gerund (деепричастие) 'deceiving' that indicates the manner of the previous action (they comforted themselves by deceiving themselves); сами себя is emphatic reflexive 'themselves'.
segments.p2-10-g357: Так что is a result clause meaning 'so/therefore' introducing the conclusion; я is the subject 'I'. The comma after я marks a pause before the parenthetical particle пожалуй.
segments.p2-10-g358: Пожалуй is a modal particle meaning 'perhaps' or 'I suppose', softening or hedging the statement.
segments.p2-10-g359: Еще here works as an intensifier 'even/more'; "живее" is the comparative of живой ('more alive' or 'livelier') placed in quotes for ironic effect; вас is the comparative object 'than you' (accusative used after comparative); выхожу is present 1st-person singular of выходить used idiomatically 'I come out / I turn out' (i.e. 'I prove to be').
segments.p2-10-g360: Да here is an optative/exhortative particle (let/may / 'do') used to urge the listener; взгляните is the polite plural imperative 'look' or 'take a look'; пристальнее is the comparative adverb meaning 'more intently' or 'more closely', forming the imperative's manner 'look more closely'.
segments.p2-10-g321: Нам is a dative pronoun meaning 'to us' and marks the person affected; же is an emphatic particle adding contrast/insistence; будет хуже is an impersonal future construction ('it will be worse'), with хуже as the comparative 'worse'. The whole phrase literally: 'For us, (it) will be worse.'
segments.p2-10-g322: Если introduces a conditional clause ('if'); наши is the plural possessive 'our'; блажные here is an adjective modifying просьбы and carries the ironic/softly approving sense 'blissful' or 'pleasant'; просьбы is the noun 'requests/pleas'; исполнят is 3rd person plural future (perfective) 'will fulfill/grant' — the agent is not expressed, so the verb functions like 'will be granted' in English.
segments.p2-10-g323: Ну is an interjection here used to urge or prompt the listener ('well', 'come on'); it prefaces the imperative that follows.
segments.p2-10-g324: Попробуйте is the 2nd person plural/polite imperative of попробовать: 'try (it)'. It invites the addressees to attempt the experiment the narrator suggests.
segments.p2-10-g325: A second, brief instance of the interjection ну, repeating the urging tone ('well, come on').
segments.p2-10-g326: Дайте is the 2nd person plural/polite imperative of дать used in the sense 'grant' or 'allow'; нам is dative 'to us' (the beneficiaries of the giving). Together: 'grant us' / 'allow us'.
segments.p2-10-g327: Например is an adverb meaning 'for example' and signals that what follows is an illustrative case.
segments.p2-10-g328: Побольше is a colloquial comparative meaning 'a bit more' and самостоятельности is a genitive noun meaning 'independence' or 'self-reliance' — together: 'a bit more independence.'
segments.p2-10-g329: Развяжите is the 2nd person plural/polite imperative of развязать used figuratively here as 'loosen/release' (untie). Любому из нас is a dative phrase 'to any one of us' (the beneficiary); руки is the direct object in accusative plural 'hands' (metaphorical for 'freedom of action'). The literal structure: 'Untie the hands of any one of us.'
segments.p2-10-g330: Расширьте is the imperative plural of расширить 'expand/broaden'; круг деятельности is a set phrase 'the circle/scope of activity' meaning 'the range of what we may do.'
segments.p2-10-g331: Ослабьте is the imperative plural of ослабить and here means 'loosen' or 'relax'; опеку is the accusative of опека 'guardianship/protection' — 'relax the guardianship' (i.e., reduce supervision).
segments.p2-10-g332: И мы is a short, trailing clause 'and we' (literally 'and we...'), leaving the result unspoken; и is the coordinating 'and/also' and мы is the nominative pronoun 'we' (here the narrator and like-minded people).
segments.p2-10-g335: Да here is a particle used to add emphasis or an exclamatory tone ('indeed' / 'well'); уверяю is first person singular present 'I assure' or 'I assure you'; же is an emphatic particle reinforcing the assertion; вас is the accusative 'you' (object of уверяю).
segments.p2-10-g336: Мы is the nominative 'we'; тотчас же is an adverbial phrase meaning 'immediately' or 'at once' (же again intensifies immediacy); попросимся is the future 1st person plural reflexive of попроситься, meaning 'we will ask (to be taken in / to be received)'; опять обратно in combination 'again back' stresses return; в опеку is a prepositional phrase 'into/under guardianship' (в + accusative here indicating movement/placement into a state).
segments.p2-10-g337: Знаю is the 1st person singular present of знать: 'I know' or 'I am aware.' It introduces the speaker's knowledge of the reaction to follow.
segments.p2-10-g338: Что introduces a subordinate clause ('that'); вы is the 2nd person plural/formal subject 'you' addressed by the narrator.
segments.p2-10-g339: Может быть is a fixed phrase meaning 'perhaps' or 'it may be'; грамматически это сочетание модальности (может) + быть, functioning adverbially to indicate uncertainty.
segments.p2-10-g340: На меня is a prepositional phrase with на + accusative meaning 'at me' (the target of anger); за это means 'for this' (за + accusative indicating cause/reason); рассердитесь is 2nd person plural future of рассердиться 'become angry' — 'you may be angry with me about this.'
segments.p2-10-g341: Закричите is the 2nd person plural future (perfective) of закричать: 'you will shout/cry out' — it lists another possible reaction of the addressees.
segments.p2-10-g342: Ногами is the instrumental plural of нога 'with your feet' (expressing means); затопаете is 2nd person plural (future or frequentative imperfective here) of затопать/затопать meaning 'stamp (your feet)' — together: 'you will stamp your feet.'
segments.p2-10-g343: Говорите is 2nd person plural present 'you say' and here introduces the reported or mocked words the narrator imagines his listeners will utter.
segments.p2-10-g344: Дескать is a particle meaning roughly 'they say' / 'as if' / 'so you say', used to report or to distance the speaker from the reported opinion; it has an ironic or dismissive flavor.
segments.p2-10-g345: Про is the preposition 'about' or 'concerning'; себя одного literally 'about oneself alone' (себя = oneself, одного emphasises 'alone'/'only one'); и = 'and'; про ваши мизеры = 'about your miseries/petty sufferings' (мизеры here is more 'miseries' / 'petty troubles'); в подполье 'in the underground' uses в + locative to indicate place/realm ('the underground' as a social/psychological realm).
segments.p2-10-g346: А is the coordinating 'but/and' introducing contrast; не is the negation 'not'; смейте is 2nd person plural of сметь used in an admonitory sense 'dare' (here in the formula 'and you dare not...'); говорить is the infinitive 'to say' — together: 'and you do not dare to say.'
segments.p2-10-g347: Всё мы (quoted) literally 'all of us' — все is the plural adjective/pronoun 'all' and мы is 'we'; the quotation marks show the phrase the narrator attributes to his imagined critics ('you don't dare to say "all of us"').
segments.p2-10-g406: Впервые is an adverb meaning 'for the first time.' Опубликовано is a short passive (neuter) participle/impersonal passive form meaning 'was published' (no explicit subject). В + журнале puts the medium/place in the prepositional case: 'in the journal,' and the quoted title Эпоха is the name of the periodical (here functioning as a proper noun in the prepositional case).
segments.p2-10-g407: Here N is the printed abbreviation marking issue numbers (equivalent to 'No.'), followed by the inclusive range 1-2 and a comma; it functions typographically to introduce the cited issue numbers rather than as a lexical word in the sentence.
segments.p2-10-g408: The numeral and closing parenthesis '4)' continues the enumeration of issues; с is the preposition 'with' and подписью is the noun 'signature' in the instrumental case, here used to mean 'with the signature' or 'signed by' (instrumental indicates accompaniment/agent of signing).
segments.p2-10-g409: Федор Достоевский is the author's proper name in nominative used to show the signature; presented as the name that appears with the publication.
segments.p2-10-g410: The quoted title "Записки из подполья" functions as the subject; the dash stands in for the copula (is) and introduces the noun сказуемое произведение (a work). Произведение is nominative and names the literary work referred to by the title.
segments.p2-10-g411: Открывшее is a past active participle in the neuter singular agreeing with the noun произведение (which is neuter) and functions as a relative modifier: 'which opened' or 'that opened.' It introduces the effect or result attributed to the work.
segments.p2-10-g412: Новый этап is the direct object of the participle открывшее ('opened a new stage'); в творчестве uses the prepositional case to mean 'in the creative work/career' and Достоевского is the genitive possessive 'of Dostoevsky.' Together they form 'a new stage in Dostoevsky's creative work.'
segments.p2-10-g413: В центре повести is a locative prepositional phrase 'at the center of the tale/story' setting the scene. Характерный образ 'идеолога' is a nominative noun phrase 'the characteristic image/figure of the "ideologist"' — Russian often omits the copula 'is' in such existential descriptions.
segments.p2-10-g414: Мыслителя is an appositive noun in the same genitive/nominal construction specifying the previous noun: 'an ideologist, a thinker.' It further defines the kind of 'образ' already mentioned.
segments.p2-10-g415: Носителя is a noun in the same dependent chain ('bearer' of something) and here introduces what is being borne. Хотя и is a concessive phrase 'although even' or 'although', and странной is an adjective in the feminine form and appropriate case to agree with the feminine noun that follows (системы), so it means 'of a strange ...'.
segments.p2-10-g416: «парадоксальной» is the quoted adjective 'paradoxical' (in the same case and gender as странной), rhetorically set off in quotes: 'strange, "paradoxical"' — a label or characterization of the system that follows.
segments.p2-10-g417: Но в то же время is a fixed phrase 'but at the same time'; теоретически is an adverb 'theoretically' modifying замкнутой. Замкнутой is an adjectival past-participle meaning 'closed/isolated' agreeing in gender/case with системы (genitive feminine), so системы взглядов means 'a system of views/opinions' and the whole phrase describes the system as 'theoretically closed.'
segments.p2-10-g442: A single prepositional time/location phrase. В + prepositional (в письме) = 'in the letter'; к + dative (к брату) gives the recipient 'to his brother'; от + genitive (от 20 марта 1864 г.) indicates origin/date 'dated/from' and г. is the standard abbreviation for 'год' (year).
segments.p2-10-g443: он + past tense verb писАл (imperfective past, masculine singular) = 'he wrote.' Past tense simply reports what he did; the colon introduces a quotation.
segments.p2-10-g444: Сел is past tense of сесть ('sat down') used idiomatically with за + accusative to mean 'sat down to (do something)' or 'set to work.' За работу is the direct object in accusative, here 'to work' / 'to start working.'
segments.p2-10-g445: За + accusative повесть continues the object idea from 'сел за…' = 'set to (writing) the novella.' Гораздо is an intensifying adverb 'much'; труднее is the comparative 'more difficult' (comparative form of трудный); ее is the object pronoun 'it' (referring to the повесть); писать is the infinitive 'to write.' The clause means 'to write the novella is much more difficult (to write)'.
segments.p2-10-g446: чем introduces the comparative clause 'than'; я думал is past tense 'I thought.' Together: 'than I thought.'
segments.p2-10-g447: А между тем is a connective phrase meaning 'and yet' / 'meanwhile' / 'however.' Непременно is an adverb 'certainly/absolutely' and надо is an impersonal modal predicate meaning 'it is necessary' or 'one must.' The construction is impersonal: 'it is absolutely necessary.'
segments.p2-10-g448: чтоб introduces a subordinate clause expressing wish/requirement ('that'); она is the subject 'it/she' (the novella); была is the past form of быть used here in a subjunctive-like desiderative construction ('would be' / 'be'); хороша is the short-form adjective functioning as a predicate ('good'). Together: 'that it be good.'
segments.p2-10-g449: самому мне is emphatic dative: самому ('to the very/oneself' used emphatically) with мне ('to me') = 'to me personally'; это is the pronoun 'this/it' (the thing desired); надобно is again the impersonal predicate 'is necessary' — literally 'to me this is necessary' = 'I need this.'
segments.p2-10-g450: По + dative (по тону) means 'according to/by the tone'; своему is the reflexive possessive agreeing with the noun (literally 'by its own tone'); она is the feminine subject ('it/she' for the novella); слишком is the adverb 'too/excessively'; странная is the predicate adjective in nominative feminine 'strange.'
segments.p2-10-g451: Coordinated continuation: и introduces another comment; тон is nominative 'the tone'; резок and дик are short-form/adjectival predicates meaning 'sharp/abrupt' and 'wild/fierce' respectively. The pattern is nominal subject + predicate adjectives.
segments.p2-10-g452: может is a modal present form meaning 'it may' / 'might' (expressing possibility) and is followed by the infinitive/verb понравиться with negator не = 'may not be liked' / 'might not please.' The construction is impersonal/passive in effect.
segments.p2-10-g453: следовательно is a discourse adverb meaning 'therefore' / 'consequently,' linking inference from the previous clause to what follows.
segments.p2-10-g454: надобно again is impersonal 'it is necessary'; чтоб introduces the subordinate clause of necessity; поэзия is the nominative subject 'poetry'; все is the object 'everything/all' (here meaning the harsh elements of the text); смягчила and вынесла are past-tense forms used after чтоб to express the desired/required actions ('soften' and 'carry/endure' respectively). Together: 'it is necessary that poetry soften and bear/endure everything.'
segments.p2-10-g473: This is the single letter е used as the second part of the abbreviation т. е. (то есть, 'that is'). In older or broken-up print the two letters may appear on separate lines; here it functions as an abbreviated demonstrative/explanatory particle.
segments.p2-10-g474: Instrumental plural phrase: надёрганными фразами = 'with torn/fragmentary phrases', where надёрганными is a past-passive/participial adjective in instrumental plural describing the manner. The instrumental marks the manner or means.
segments.p2-10-g475: A short interrogative sentence: Но = 'but' (adversative), что = interrogative 'what', же = emphatic particle adding urgency or emphasis, and делать is the infinitive 'to do' used idiomatically to form the question 'what to do / what is to be done'.
segments.p2-10-g476: Nominal topical phrase: Свиньи цензора is literally 'the censor's swine'; цензора is in the genitive and modifies свиньи. The noun is used metaphorically/pejoratively ('swine' as an insult or metaphorical description of passages).
segments.p2-10-g477: там is an adverb meaning 'there' or 'in those places', marking the locative/contextual place referred to by the following relative clause.
segments.p2-10-g478: Relative clause introduced by где ('where/in which'): я глумился над всем = 'I mocked/ridiculed everything' (imperfect past), и иногда богохульствовал для виду = 'and sometimes blasphemed for appearance's sake' (для виду = 'for show'). The verbs are past tense, first person singular, describing the content of those 'places.'
segments.p2-10-g479: Dash plus то пропущено: то is a demonstrative pronoun referring back to the previously described passages ('those [parts]'), and пропущено is a short passive/neuter past form meaning 'was let through' or 'was passed' (an impersonal/passive statement about censorship action).
segments.p2-10-g480: Contrastive clause: а = 'but', where где introduces the contrasting places; из всего этого = 'out of all this' (source phrase), я вывел = 'I drew/derived' (past tense, first person), потребность веры и Христа = 'the need for faith and (for) Christ' (потребность + genitives).
segments.p2-10-g481: Dash plus то запрещено: again то refers to the contrasted parts just mentioned, and запрещено is the short passive/neuter form meaning 'was forbidden' or 'prohibited' (impersonal/passive censor action).
segments.p2-10-g434: A prepositional phrase locating the action in the text: "В" + locative singular "главе" = "in the chapter." The ordinal "1-й" (with hyphen) modifies "главе" and agrees in case (locative).
segments.p2-10-g435: "По-видимому" is a frozen adverb meaning "apparently" or "it seems"; grammatically it behaves as an adverbial modifier of the clause. Here "по" is the prefixal element and "видимому" is the dative-based form but taken together they form the idiom.
segments.p2-10-g436: A nominative noun used as a comment or apposition: "болтовня" = "chatter". Comma marks it off as an evaluative remark about the first chapter.
segments.p2-10-g437: Main clause: "но" introduces contrast; "вдруг" is an adverb "suddenly"; "эта болтовня" is the subject (demonstrative + noun). "в последних 2-х главах" is a locative/time phrase meaning "in the last two chapters" (numeral uses abbreviated form "2-х"). The verb "разрешается" is reflexive/passive-like here meaning "is resolved" or "comes to resolution," and the instrument/agent is expressed by the instrumental "неожиданной катастрофой" ("by/an unexpected catastrophe").
segments.p2-10-g438: Subject phrase: "Новая художественная структура" (adjectives + noun) with the title in genitive "Записок из подполья" specifying whose structure it is: "the structure of 'Notes from Underground'." The predicate "была" is past feminine singular, agreeing with "структура." "Столь необычна" uses the short-form adjective "необычна" with the adverb "столь" (= "so/so very").
segments.p2-10-g439: A subordinate clause introduced by "что" ("that"). "Даже искушенные в вопросах литературы современники" is a noun phrase: "даже" adds emphasis, "искушенные" is a past participle used adjectivally meaning "skilled/experienced" (here: "those experienced in literary matters"); "вопросах" is plural locative/genitive after "в" meaning "in matters of." "Не сразу поняли" is the past plural verb with negation meaning "did not immediately understand," and "ее суть" is the direct object "its essence."
segments.p2-10-g440: Coordinating clause introduced by "и" (and). "Пытались" is past plural (they tried) and takes the infinitive "отождествлять" (to equate). The direct object is "идеологию 'подпольного' человека" ("the ideology of the 'underground' man"). The preposition "с" + instrumental "мировосприятием" marks the thing it was equated with ("with the author's worldview"); "автора" is in genitive modifying "мировосприятием."
segments.p2-10-g441: "В процессе работы над повестью" is a temporal/adverbial phrase: "в процессе" + genitive "работы" and "над" + instrumental "повестью" = "in the course of working on the novella." "Достоевский осознал" is subject + past verb "realized." The object "трудность стоящей перед ним задачи" is a genitive attribute construction: "трудность" (accusative object) qualified by the genitive phrase "стоящей перед ним задачи" where "стоящей" is a present participle agreeing with "задачи" and "перед ним" marks "before him."
segments.p2-10-g550: The token is a cardinal numeral used alone to indicate the year (a bibliographic or datational label). It stands nominatively as an absolute date; the trailing period is just sentence punctuation. It functions as a standalone bibliographic date rather than as part of an inflected phrase.
segments.p2-10-g552: An Arabic numeral followed by a period used as an ordinal or list/section label (read as 'No. 2' or simply '2.'). It functions as an enumerative marker, not as an inflected word in a sentence.
segments.p2-10-g553: An abbreviation (С. = с.) for страница/страницы ('page' or 'pages') used in bibliographic notation; the capital letter plus period marks it as an abbreviation that typically precedes a page number. In English bibliographic equivalents this would be rendered 'p.' or 'pp.' depending on context.
segments.p2-10-g554: A cardinal numeral indicating a page number (here, page 555); it follows the page-abbreviation and the final period is sentence punctuation. It serves as the numeric part of a bibliographic page reference.
segments.p2-10-g418: "Не будучи" is the adverbial participle (деепричастие) of быть with negation: 'not being' (introduces a circumstance or concession). "единомышленником" is in the instrumental case (кем? — 'a like-minded person'), while "своего 'антигероя'" is a genitive phrase that identifies whose like-minded person (свой refers back to the subject of the main clause). The whole segment is a fronted concessive circumstantial clause dependent on the main clause that follows.
segments.p2-10-g419: Main clause: subject (Достоевский) + past-tense verb придал (perfective past of придать) meaning 'imparted/gave/lent.' "Его рассуждениям" is dative plural (the indirect object that receives the force: 'to his reasonings'), and "такую силу доказательности" is the direct object (accusative) — 'such a force (of evidentiality)'. Note the verb takes an accusative thing ('силу') and the dative target ('рассуждениям').
segments.p2-10-g420: "какой" here is a correlative/relative pronoun linking back to 'такую силу' ('such as/which'), introducing the clause that characterizes that force. "впоследствии" is an adverb 'subsequently'; "отличались" is past plural of отличаться meaning 'were distinguished by / were notable for' (the monologues had that distinguishing quality). "монологи главных героев его больших романов" is a noun phrase with genitives: 'monologues (of) the main heroes (of) his major novels.' The following dash begins a list of those heroes' names (in genitive).
segments.p2-10-g421: Part of the appositive list of names that exemplify the preceding phrase; the names are given in the genitive (they complete 'героев'). This segment is the first name in that enumerative series.
segments.p2-10-g422: Continuation of the enumerative list of characters (genitive form of the surname); functions as further specification of 'главных героев'.
segments.p2-10-g423: Another item in the list of character names (genitive); appended to the previous names by commas.
segments.p2-10-g424: Another name in the same enumerative apposition; genitive form used because it completes 'героев'.
segments.p2-10-g425: Final items of the list: first names in genitive (Дмитрия, Ивана) followed by the family name in genitive plural (Карамазовых), together specifying the two Karamazov brothers. The conjunction "и" simply connects the two given names.
segments.p2-10-g426: "Принцип построения повести" is a noun phrase in which "построения" (genitive) modifies "принцип": 'the principle of the construction (structure) of the novella.' The phrase is fronted (topic position) and will be picked up by the rest of the sentence.
segments.p2-10-g427: "основанный" is a verbal adjective (past passive participle) agreeing with "принцип" (masculine nominative singular): 'based/founded.' The prepositional phrase "на контрастах" (на + prepositional plural) states what it is based on: 'on contrasts.' Together this is a restrictive participial modifier.
segments.p2-10-g428: Main clause: subject (Достоевский) + past imperfective пытался ('attempted/tried') + infinitive объяснить ('to explain'). "в письме к брату" is a locative/dative phrase 'in a letter to (his) brother' and "от 13 апреля 1864 г." is a date expression introduced by от meaning 'of / dated', followed by the abbreviated 'г.' for 'year.'
segments.p2-10-g429: A colon introducing a direct quotation from the letter; punctuation only (no lexical items to map).
segments.p2-10-g430: Direct speech: "Ты понимаешь" is second-person singular present indicative ('you understand' / 'do you understand'), addressed to the letter's addressee. The comma marks continuation into a subordinate clause.
segments.p2-10-g431: Subordinate interrogative/explanatory clause functioning as the object of 'понимаешь': "что такое переход в музыке" = 'what a transition in music is' (что такое = 'what is/what kind of'). "в музыке" is prepositional 'in music.'
segments.p2-10-g432: Author tag after the quoted clause: dash + past tense писaл ('wrote') followed by pronoun он ('he') referring to Dostoevsky. Typical Russian layout places the verb before the pronoun for stylistic balance.
segments.p2-10-g433: Colloquial evaluative clause: "Точно так и тут" = 'exactly so here too' or 'it's exactly the same here.' "Точно" (exactly) modifies "так" (so/that way); "и" functions as an intensifier 'also/indeed'; "тут" means 'here' or 'in this case.' The dash at the start marks direct speech continuing from the preceding quotation.
segments.p2-10-g495: "Это факт" is a verbless predicative statement: the demonstrative pronoun 'Это' introduces the predicate noun 'факт' and asserts identity ('This is a fact'). Russians often omit the copula 'есть' in the present, so the short phrase functions as a full sentence.
segments.p2-10-g496: «против факта и говорить бы» is an idiomatic clause meaning 'to speak against the fact' with a softening particle. 'Против' governs the genitive 'факта' ('against the fact'). The conjunction/particle 'и' here links and adds a slight emphasis ('even/and'); 'говорить' is the infinitive 'to speak'; the modal particle 'бы' softens the statement and forms a hypothetical/conditional shade ('would be to speak'). Together this leads into the impersonal conclusion that follows.
segments.p2-10-g497: 'кажется' is a parenthetical impersonal verb meaning 'it seems/appears' and modifies the whole preceding clause; it signals the author's hedging or impression and is set off by commas.
segments.p2-10-g498: 'нечего' is an impersonal pronoun/adverbial predicate meaning 'there is nothing (to...)' — here it implies 'there is nothing to say'. It functions as the main predicative element of the impersonal construction begun in the previous segment.
segments.p2-10-g499: This is an impersonal clause with contrast: 'но' ('but') introduces the exception; 'спросить' is the infinitive 'to ask'; 'из любопытства' is an adverbial phrase 'out of/from curiosity' (preposition 'из' + noun in genitive); and 'можно' is an impersonal modal meaning 'it is possible' or 'one may'. The whole means 'but one can ask out of curiosity.'
segments.p2-10-g500: 'Так вот' is a discourse marker combining 'так' ('so/thus') and 'вот' ('well/now') to introduce a conclusion or emphatic comment. Word order 'не понимаю я' places the subject 'я' after the verb for mild emphasis; 'понимаю' is present tense 'I understand' with negation 'не' ('I do not understand').
segments.p2-10-g501: «чтоб» here is the colloquial subordinating particle (short form of 'чтобы') meaning 'that' and introduces the content (subordinate) clause. It links to the following noun phrase 'умный человек' which is the subject of that subordinate clause ('that an intelligent person...').
segments.p2-10-g502: «когда бы то ни было» is a fixed concessive phrase meaning 'whenever/at whatever time' formed by 'когда' + the subjunctive particle 'бы' and the emphatic sequence 'то ни' — together they express indefiniteness or any time at all. Grammatically it behaves as an adverbial modifier of time.
segments.p2-10-g503: «при каких бы то ни было обстоятельствах» is another frozen concessive expression meaning 'under whatever circumstances.' 'При' governs the locative ('обстоятельствах'); 'каких ... ни ...' with 'бы' produces the indefinite concessive sense ('whatever'). The whole phrase modifies the subordinate clause's condition:
segments.p2-10-g504: «не мог найти себе дела» is a past-tense modal construction: 'мог' is the past of 'мочь' ('could/was able'), negated by 'не' ('could not'). It takes the perfective infinitive 'найти' ('to find') to express inability. 'Себе' is a dative reflexive meaning 'for oneself', and 'дела' means 'a thing to do / work' — so 'could not find anything to occupy himself with.'
segments.p2-10-g505: В то же время is a fixed temporal adverbial ('at the same time') made from the preposition в + demonstrative то + particle же + noun время; it precedes the clause for emphasis. Достоевский is the nominative subject that comes immediately before the verb. Осознавал is the imperfective past tense, masculine singular form of осознавать, indicating an ongoing or general awareness rather than a single completed act ('was aware/realized'). И before трагичность functions here as 'also'/'too', linking this object to something else he recognized. Трагичность положения is the accusative object meaning 'the tragic quality/nature of the situation' (трагичность + noun in genitive); положения is genitive singular of положение ('of the situation'). "Лишних людей" is genitive plural modifying положение ('situation of the "superfluous people"') and is placed in quotes as a literary term; overall word order is standard (adverbial phrase – subject – verb – object).
segments.p2-10-g506: Герой 'Записок' is a nominative noun phrase where 'Записок' is the genitive plural form of the title (literally 'the hero of the "Notes"'). Говорит is a present tense, 3rd person singular verb meaning 'says' or 'speaks'; the prepositional phrase 'о себе' ('about himself') identifies the object of the remark. The colon signals a direct quotation follows.
segments.p2-10-g507: The main predication is the modal construction 'не может быть тщеславен' — 'cannot be vain' (present tense modal 'может' + infinitive 'быть'). 'Без' governs the following noun phrase in the genitive 'неограниченной требовательности' ('of unlimited exactingness'); 'к себе самому' is a dative phrase with 'самому' as an emphatic/reflective intensifier ('toward himself personally'). The second coordinated negative element 'и не презирая себя в иные минуты до ненависти' uses the imperfective gerund (деепричастие) 'презирая' with negation to mean 'and without despising himself'; 'в иные минуты' is a temporal adverbial ('at other/certain moments') and 'до' + genitive 'ненависти' means 'to the point of hatred.' Together the two coordinated negatives express that one cannot be vain except by being unboundedly hard on oneself and, at times, despising oneself to hatred.
segments.p2-10-g485: This whole phrase is a single clause built around the past tense verb 'указал' (perfective past, masculine singular) meaning 'pointed out.' The initial prepositional phrase 'В подстрочном примечании' ('в' + locative) locates the action 'in the footnote'; 'к журнальной публикации первой части "Записок"' is a dative phrase with 'к' specifying which publication the footnote belongs to; 'указал на связь' uses 'на' + accusative ('связь') meaning 'indicated/pointed to a connection'; 'своего "антигероя"' is a genitive phrase ('связь [чего]') meaning 'the connection of his antihero'; finally 'с типом "лишнего человека"' uses 'с' + instrumental ('типом') meaning 'with the type' (i.e., associated with that literary type).
segments.p2-10-g486: 'Проблема' is the feminine nominative subject 'the problem'; the following noun phrase '"лишних людей"' is in the genitive plural and modifies 'проблема' — literally 'the problem of the superfluous people' (a recognized literary type).
segments.p2-10-g487: This is a relative clause: 'на смену' is a fixed phrase meaning 'in replacement' or 'to the place of'; 'которым' is the relative pronoun in dative plural referring back to 'лишних людей' ('to whom/which'); 'шли' is the past plural of 'идти' used here as 'were coming/succeeding'; 'новые герои-деятели' is the nominative plural subject of 'шли' — 'new hero-figures' (i.e., new men of action) succeeded them.
segments.p2-10-g488: 'активно' is an adverb modifying the passive reflexive verb 'обсуждалась' (feminine singular past passive: 'was discussed'), which agrees with the feminine subject 'Проблема'; 'в русской публицистике' is a locative phrase 'in Russian publicistic writing'; 'на рубеже 1860-х годов' is a temporal phrase 'at the turn of the 1860s' with 'на' + locative 'рубеже' indicating that temporal boundary.
segments.p2-10-g489: The verb 'Проявлял' is past imperfective (masculine singular) 'showed/manifested'; word order is inverted here: the verb comes before the subject 'Достоевский' (placed after with 'и' meaning 'also'), which yields 'Dostoevsky also showed interest.' 'к ней' is 'toward it/her' (preposition 'к' + dative 'ней') referring back to 'the problem'; 'интерес' is the noun 'interest' that the verb takes as its object.
segments.p2-10-g490: 'Так' is an adverb/particle meaning 'thus' or 'so' and functions as a discourse marker introducing the example or explanation that follows.
segments.p2-10-g491: This is a gerundial phrase: 'имея в виду' comes from the idiom 'иметь в виду' ('to have in mind' / 'to mean'), here as 'having in mind'; 'Чацкого' is the (animate) object (form identical to genitive/accusative) meaning the character Chatsky; 'и последовавших за ним "лишних людей"' contains the past participle 'последовавших' ('who followed') with the prepositional phrase 'за ним' ('after him') and the noun phrase 'лишних людей' ('superfluous people'), all forming a coordinated object of 'имея в виду' — 'having Chatsky and the superfluous people who followed him in mind.'
segments.p2-10-g492: 'Достоевский писал' is straightforward: past imperfective 'wrote' with the author as subject; 'в "Зимних заметках"' is a locative expression naming the work ('in "Winter Notes"'). The semicolon introduces the quotation that follows.
segments.p2-10-g493: Quoted speech: 'Они все' = 'they all' (subject), 'ведь' is a modal particle often rendered 'after all' or 'you know', 'не нашли' is perfective past plural 'did not find', and 'дела' here means 'a cause/occupation/thing to do' (what fills one's life) — 'They all, after all, did not find a calling/work.'
segments.p2-10-g494: 'не находили' is the imperfective past plural 'had not been finding' or 'did not find' used here to stress continuity across generations; 'два-три поколения' is the measured phrase 'two or three generations'; 'сряду' is an adverb meaning 'in a row' or 'consecutively' — together: 'they had not found (a calling) for two or three generations in a row.'
segments.p2-10-g508: Я был + short-form adjective (развит) uses the past tense copula был with a short participial/adjectival predicate to state a past condition: "I was developed." Болезненно is an adverb modifying развит and placed before it for emphasis (literally "morbidly/sickly developed"). The clause presents a past state of the speaker rather than an action.
segments.p2-10-g509: Как и следует быть развитым человеку нашего времени is an idiomatic impersonal construction: следует (modal 'ought/should') governs the infinitive быть развитым ('to be developed'). Человеку is dative marking the person for whom the obligation/state holds ('for a person of our time'), and нашего времени is a genitive attribute 'of our time.' Как и here is a linking particle 'as (indeed)'; the caret ^ is an editorial insertion mark.
segments.p2-10-g510: The clause has the verb считать ('to consider') with Достоевский as subject and подобное трагическое мироощущение as the thing considered (direct object; neuter form identical in nominative/accusative). Характерной чертой is in the instrumental case and expresses the role/identity assigned by the verb ("considered X to be Y"). The quoted adjectives избранных and лишних modify люди in the genitive plural, naming the social types referred to.
segments.p2-10-g511: Это видно is an impersonal predicative phrase meaning "this is evident/visible." Из + noun (из его отзыва) indicates the source ('from his review'), and о + title marks the object of the review (about 'The Devils' by Turgenev). The whole segment states provenance: the evidence comes from that review.
segments.p2-10-g512: Которые is a relative pronoun referring back to "Призраках." Прочитал is perfective past (he read, completed action). В период работы над takes a temporal expression ('during the period of work on') with работа followed by над + instrumental (here the title Записками is in the instrumental because над governs the instrumental). Из подполья is part of the book title in the genitive/partitive sense ('of the Underground').
segments.p2-10-g513: В "Призраках" topicalizes the work's title ('in/with regard to
segments.p2-10-g455: Творческие трудности is the noun phrase subject in the nominative plural ('creative difficulties'). Усугублялись is the past tense, plural, reflexive/imperfective verb meaning 'were exacerbated' (passive sense). Еще и introduces an additional aggravating factor ('even/also'), and цензурными искажениями текста is an instrumental plural noun phrase ('by censorship distortions of the text') that functions as the agent/instrument causing the exacerbation.
segments.p2-10-g456: Разрушавшими is a past active participle in the instrumental plural that refers back to 'искажениями' ('the distortions'), meaning 'which were destroying'. Внутреннюю логику is the direct object in the accusative ('the internal logic'), and повествования is a genitive noun modifying логика ('of the narrative'). The whole participial phrase modifies the preceding instrumental noun phrase.
segments.p2-10-g457: Так is a discourse adverb here meaning 'thus' or 'so', used to draw the following description as a consequence or example.
segments.p2-10-g458: Получив is a perfective verbal adverb (деепричастие) 'having received', introducing a circumstantial clause; первый двойной номер 'Эпохи' is the direct object (accusative) identifying what was received—'the first double issue of Epokha' (name of the journal).
segments.p2-10-g459: Где begins a relative clause ('where/in which'). Было напечатано is an impersonal past passive construction ('was printed/published') and Подполье (in quotes) is the title printed there (neuter), the grammatical subject of the passive.
segments.p2-10-g460: И обнаружив is another verbal adverb 'and having discovered/found', linking a second circumstantial action; там is a locative adverb 'there' referring back to the issue.
segments.p2-10-g461: Помимо is a preposition meaning 'besides' or 'apart from', introducing an item found in addition; 'ужасных опечаток' is a genitive plural phrase ('horrible misprints'), the thing set aside as 'besides'.
segments.p2-10-g462: Вмешательство цензора is a noun phrase functioning as the direct object of обнаружив ('having found'); вмешательство is 'intervention' and цензора is the genitive 'of the censor'—together 'the censor's intervention'.
segments.p2-10-g463: Достоевский is the nominative subject; писал is the past tense verb 'wrote' (here introducing the date of the letter); the following items give the date '26 March 1864' with г. as the standard abbreviation for 'year'.
segments.p2-10-g464: Брату is the dative singular of брат and here marks the indirect object 'to (his) brother', indicating the addressee of the letter; the colon shows the letter's text follows.
segments.p2-10-g465: The segment contains only the opening quotation mark ("), marking the start of the quoted letter; there are no lexical items to assign senses to.
segments.p2-10-g468: Уж лучше было is an impersonal past construction meaning 'it would have been better' (уж adds emphasis). Совсем не печатать is the infinitive phrase 'not to print at all'; with negation Russian often takes the direct object in the genitive, hence предпоследней главы (genitive) 'the penultimate chapter'. The parenthetical starts immediately after to qualify which chapter is meant.
segments.p2-10-g469: Где begins a subordinate clause 'where/in which'. Самая-то uses the demonstrative самaя plus the enclitic particle -то for emphasis ('the very one'); мысль is the noun 'thought'; и высказывается is a reflexive present passive 'and is expressed/voiced'. The clause explains that the essential idea appears there.
segments.p2-10-g470: Чем introduces the comparative ('than') that contrasts the prior option with printing it; печатать is the infinitive 'to print', and так ('so') points forward to the manner being criticized.
segments.p2-10-g471: Как оно есть is an idiomatic phrase 'as it is' or 'as it stands': как 'as', оно 'it' (dummy/neutral pronoun), есть serving here as the copular/existential 'is' used in colloquial/formal expressions.
segments.p2-10-g472: Т. is an abbreviation (т.)—here the standard abbreviation for том ('vol.' or 'volume') as printed in the source; it appears as a clipped bibliographic/printing mark at the sentence end.
segments.p2-10-g517: много is an adverb/quantifier meaning 'many' or 'much' and here governs the following substantive; реального is an adjective used substantively in the genitive singular (реальное → реального) meaning 'the real' or 'what is real'. The phrase literally reads 'much of the real.'
segments.p2-10-g518: Это is the demonstrative 'this'; реальное is an adjective used as a substantive ('the real' / 'this reality'); the dash plus есть (the present form of 'to be' used for emphasis/identification) links the subject to the predicate тоска. Развитого и сознающего существа is a genitive phrase modifying тоска: 'of a developed and self-aware being', with сознающего a present participle functioning as an adjective.
segments.p2-10-g519: живущего is a present active participle in the genitive singular agreeing with существа ('of a ... being') and means 'living' or 'dwelling'; в наше время is a time expression 'in our time' (в + время), here functioning as an adverbial modifier of the participle.
segments.p2-10-g520: уловленная is a past passive participle (feminine singular) from уловить, agreeing with тоска and meaning 'caught' or 'perceived'; тоска repeats the noun and here functions appositively: 'a perceived melancholy.'
segments.p2-10-g521: Задумав is a perfective gerund (деепричастие) from задумать, meaning 'having conceived' or 'having planned', and it takes произведение as its direct object: 'having conceived a work...'. This construction sets up the circumstance for the main clause that follows.
segments.p2-10-g522: в центре которого is a relative phrase 'in the center of which', with которого in the genitive referring back to произведение. должен был стоять is the past tense of the modal verb construction (должен + был + infinitive) meaning 'was supposed to be placed' or 'was to occupy' (i.e. 'whose center was to be'). исповедующийся is a present participle used adjectivally ('confessing' or 'confessional'); лишний человек is the literary type 'superfluous man'.
segments.p2-10-g523: Достоевский is the subject. не мог не учесть is a double-negative idiom meaning 'could not but take into account' (literally 'could not not take into account'); учесть is the infinitive 'to take into account' and governs опыта своих предшественников in the accusative/genitive sense 'the experience of his predecessors' (опыта is genitive as the thing taken into account).
segments.p2-10-g524: создавших is a past active participle in the genitive plural modifying предшественников ('who had created'); классические образцы этого типа is the object of создавших: 'the classical exemplars/models of this type', with этого типа in genitive as 'of this type'.
segments.p2-10-g525: а также is a connective phrase meaning 'as well as' or 'and also'; суждения критиков is a noun phrase 'the judgments/opinions of critics' and functions in parallel with 'опыта своих предшественников' as another thing Dostoevsky had to take into account.
segments.p2-10-g526: объяснивших is a past active participle in the genitive plural modifying суждения критиков ('which explained'); it takes two coordinated objects: историческую сущность ('the historical essence') and закономерность появления 'лишних людей' ('the regularity/lawfulness of the emergence of "superfluous men"'), where появления is a genitive noun dependent on закономерность and лишних людей is quoted and in the genitive plural.
segments.p2-10-g527: Герой "Записок из подполья" is a noun phrase in which Записок из подполья is a title in the genitive after герой ('the hero of Notes from Underground'). По своему психологическому облику is a dative-like/advantage phrase introduced by по + dative (своему облику) meaning 'in respect of / with regard to his psychological make-up' (своему refers back to the hero). Ближе всего стоит is a figurative present-tense use of стоять ('to stand') with the adverbial comparative ближе всего ('closest of all'); the verb takes the dative introduced by к to mark the thing/person to which he is closest. Тургенева is genitive and links to "русским Гамлетам" as the author/possessor: 'Turgenev's "Russian Hamlets".'
segments.p2-10-g528: This clause continues the list of dative complements governed by к ('to / toward'). "Гамлету Щигровского уезда" is dative (Гамлету) with the genitive attribute Щигровского уезда ('of the Shchigry uyezd/district'), i.e. 'the Hamlet of the Shchigry district.' The parenthetical year (1849) is just a date. И к Чулкатурину из "Дневника лишнего человека" parallels the earlier structure: Чулкатурину is dative (to Chulkaturin), из + genitive (из Дневника) marks source 'from the Diary of a Superfluous Man', and 1850 is the date.
segments.p2-10-g540: Тургенев is the nominative proper-name subject. дал is the past-tense, perfective verb (he gave) and нам is the dative indirect object 'to us'. несколько образцов людей is a quantified noun phrase: несколько + genitive plural образцов ("several samples/exemplars") and людей is the genitive plural ("of people") modifying образцов.
segments.p2-10-g541: страдающих is a present active participle (страдающий) in the genitive plural, agreeing with людей and functioning as a postpositive attribute: 'people suffering...'. этой язвою uses the demonstrative этой + the instrumental form язвою; with страдать the instrument expresses 'to suffer from' (here used metaphorically).
segments.p2-10-g542: таковы is a short-form demonstrative/predicate meaning 'such are' (summarizes the preceding clause). его is the possessive 'his' referring back to Turgenev and modifies the quoted titles. The quoted phrases are titles treated as nominal objects; Г-н Ф. is an abbreviated address 'Господин Ф.' (Mr. F.), appended as an attribution/credit.
segments.p2-10-g543: Достоевский is the nominative subject (proper name) and stands alone here as the agent of the following verb.
segments.p2-10-g544: в параллель тургеневскому Гамлету is a prepositional/adverbial phrase meaning 'in parallel to' or 'by way of parallel with'. Here в + noun phrase expresses a comparison; тургеневскому Гамлету is dative (adverbial dative after 'в параллель') and тургеневскому is the adjectival form 'Turgenev's' agreeing with Гамлету.
segments.p2-10-g545: написал is the past-tense perfective 'wrote' taking героя as its direct object. с + instrumental (с большою яркостию) expresses manner: 'with great vividness'; note the archaic/older orthography and endings (большою, яркостию) for the instrumental. своего agrees with героя and is the reflexive/possessive 'his own' referring back to Достоевский; подпольного is an adjective in quotes modifying герой.
segments.p2-10-g548: Отеч. is an abbreviated, parenthetical form of Отечественные (part of the journal title). It appears here as the first piece of a printed abbreviation and should be read as 'Отечественные...' (the beginning of the journal name).
segments.p2-10-g549: зап. is the continuation of the abbreviation (Записки) so that Отеч. зап. together abbreviate the journal title Отечественные записки ('Otechestvennye zapiski' — 'Domestic Notes'). It is printed in fragmentary abbreviated form here.
segments.p2-10-g555: Наша (possessive adjective) + изящная (qualifying adjective) agree in gender, number and case with словесность (feminine singular). Словесность is a feminine noun in the nominative (here a short fragment or parenthetical nominal phrase); the order is typical Russian: possessive + adjective + noun. The closing parenthesis/period are punctuation from the source, not affecting the grammar.
segments.p2-10-g556: Следует отметить is an impersonal construction: следовать here functions modally ('it should/ought to be noted') and is followed by the infinitive отметить. The phrase as a whole introduces a remark; отметить is the infinitive 'to note'.
segments.p2-10-g557: что introduces a content clause ('that ...'). "антигерой Достоевского" is a noun phrase in the genitive: Достоевского marks possession ('Dostoevsky's anti-hero'). "в отличие от" is a fixed prepositional expression meaning 'in contrast to' and takes the genitive (от + gen.).
segments.p2-10-g558: не представитель "меньшинства" negates the predicate noun 'representative' (представитель) with не. Меньшинства is genitive singular (the object of от in the preceding expression or apposition), meaning 'of the minority'.
segments.p2-10-g559: а мелкий чиновник is a contrasting nominative noun phrase introduced by a (but/rather), used to present the positive identity after the negations. Мелкий is an adjective modifying чиновник; here it carries the sense 'petty/insignificant official'.
segments.p2-10-g560: страдающий ... и восстающий ... is a coordinate pair of present active participles (страдающий, восстающий) that modify the preceding noun мелкий чиновник. Страдающий + от + noun means 'suffering from X' (the preposition takes the noun in the genitive), and восстающий против + noun means 'rising up against' (против + genitive). Обезличивающих is a present active participle in the plural that agrees with условия (it is adjectival, 'depersonalizing'), and его is the pronoun 'him' referring back to the official.
segments.p2-10-g561: Социально-психологическую суть is a compound adjective (social-psychological) in the accusative feminine singular modifying суть (the direct object of пояснил). Это-го бунта uses the genitive (этого + бунта) to specify 'of this revolt'. The compound adjective is formed adverbially (социально-) + adjective (психологическую) and agrees with суть.
segments.p2-10-g562: принимавшего is a past active participle (masculine, genitive singular) that refers back to бунта ('of this revolt') and means 'that was taking on / that assumed'. It governs the direct object уродливые формы (forms taken on by the revolt); уродливые here is an adjective in the accusative/plural agreeing with формы.
segments.p2-10-g563: парадоксальные формы is the direct object (plural) of принимавшего; парадоксальные is an adjective in the plural agreeing with формы (nominative/accusative plural form). The comma closes the participial phrase that modifies бунта.
segments.p2-10-g564: Достоевский пояснил is a simple past-tense clause: пояснил (perfective past) 'explained' with Dative/Accusative object previously stated. В начале 1870-х годов is a temporal adverbial ('at the beginning of the 1870s'); в + locative (начале) + genitive plural (1870-х годов) forms the time phrase.
segments.p2-10-g565: Отвечая is the adverbial participle (деепричастие) of отвечать and expresses a concurrent or attendant action: 'answering/responding (to)'. Критикам is dative plural and is the indirect object: 'to/against the critics' — here 'to the critics.'
segments.p2-10-g566: Высказавшимся is a past participle (agreeing with критикам) in the dative plural and means 'who had expressed themselves / who had spoken up'; it restricts which critics are meant. По поводу is the fixed prepositional phrase 'regarding / concerning' and governs the genitive напечатанных частей ('the printed parts'); напечатанных is a past passive participle modifying части.
segments.p2-10-g567: Он писал is past imperfective 'he wrote' (a simple narrative past). В черновом наброске is a prepositional phrase with в + prepositional ('in a rough draft'), and "Для предисловия" is the title of that draft indicated by для + genitive ('for the preface'). The year in parentheses is the date of the draft.
segments.p2-10-g568: Я is the first-person singular pronoun 'I'. Горжусь is the present-tense reflexive verb гордиться, 'I am proud (of)', with the reflexive ending -сь marking the verb's reflexive/pronominal form.
segments.p2-10-g569: Что introduces the content clause ('that'). Впервые is an adverb 'for the first time'. Вывел is past tense perfective of вывести here meaning 'brought out / presented / introduced (a character)' — i.e. 'I first presented.' Настоящего человека русского большинства is the direct object: настоящего (accusative singular) 'real/genuine' modifies человека (accusative 'a person'), and русского большинства is a genitive attribute 'of the Russian majority.' И впервые разоблачил ... сторону: разоблачил is perfective past 'exposed/unmasked'; его ('his') refers back to the person just mentioned; уродливую и трагическую сторону is the accusative object 'ugly and tragic side.'
segments.p2-10-g672: В этой идее is a prepositional phrase ('in this idea') with the demonstrative этой agreeing in gender/case with идея in the prepositional case. Есть is the existential 'there is/there exists' (present). Нечто is an indefinite neuter pronoun ('something') and is modified by the compound descriptive phrase неотразимо-прекрасное, where неотразимо is an adverbial form ('irresistibly') joined to прекрасное (neuter adjective used substantively) to produce a single hyphenated, poetic description 'irresistibly-beautiful.'
