segments.p2-10-g673: Сладостное is another neuter adjective (agreeing with нечто) continuing the series of qualities describing that 'something.' Its form is adjectival (here used substantively/predicatively) and adds the nuance 'sweet/delightful.'
segments.p2-10-g674: Неизбежное и даже необъяснимое continue the list of neuter adjectival descriptors ('inevitable and even inexplicable'). The following word социалист begins the main clause: социалист не может себе представить — 'a socialist cannot imagine.' Здесь не is the negation before может (present tense of мочь, 'can/be able to') and себе представить is a reflexive construction meaning 'to picture/ imagine to oneself.' Даже is an intensifier ('even').
segments.p2-10-g675: Как можно introduces an indirect interrogative clause dependent on представить: 'how is it possible / how could one...' Можно is an impersonal/modal expression meaning 'it is possible / one can.' Добровольно is an adverb ('voluntarily') modifying отдавать (imperfective infinitive 'to give/surrender' — here sense 'surrender/cede' fits), себя is the reflexive accusative 'oneself,' and за всех is a prepositional phrase 'for/on behalf of everyone' indicating the object/beneficiary of the sacrifice.
segments.p2-10-g676: По его is an elliptical colloquial phrase short for по его мнению ('according to him / in his opinion'); the dash marks the authorial aside. Это is the demonstrative subject ('this/it') and безнравственно is a predicative (adverbial/short-form adjective) meaning 'immoral.' Together: '— in his view, this is immoral.'
segments.p2-10-g677: А вот introduces a contrastive turn (
segments.p2-10-g678: вся-то ... в том и заключается is a fixed, emphatic construction meaning “the whole point/essence is this.” The particle -то attached to вся gives a contrastive/emphatic shade. над социализмом uses над + instrumental to mark superiority/comparison (i.e. ‘over socialism’), and бесконечность is used figuratively as the vastness/immensity of Christianity.
segments.p2-10-g679: что introduces the subordinate clause (content clause) that states what the point is. The parenthetical (идеал) simply appends an appositive gloss: the author clarifies that he speaks of the Christian as an ideal.
segments.p2-10-g680: все отдавая is a деепричастие (adverbial participle/gerund) meaning “giving everything (away).” It describes an action concurrent with the main verb and shares its subject (христианин). Всё/все here means “everything/all.”
segments.p2-10-g681: ничего ... не требует is a standard negation pattern: ничегo (negative pronoun in genitive) with the negated verb не требует (“does not demand”). себе is a dative reflexive indicating ‘for himself,’ and сам is an emphatic particle reinforcing that it is the subject himself who asks for nothing.
segments.p2-10-g682: The phrase begins with the dative singular 'Второй части' ('to the second part'), indicating the recipient or position to which something is attached. The quoted subtitle '"По поводу мокрого снега"' is set off by dashes as an appositive title of that part. The nucleus of the clause is the nominative plural subject 'стихи' and the short-form passive 'предпосланы' ('were prefixed'), with 'в качестве эпиграфа' ('as an epigraph') expressing the role; 'Некрасова' is a genitive attributive meaning 'by/of Nekrasov', and the final quoted phrase is the opening line of the poem being quoted.
segments.p2-10-g685: A parenthetical date in Arabic numerals; here it functions as a bibliographic or compositional year for the quoted verse. Parentheses simply mark it as an aside; the numeral is read as the year 1845.
segments.p2-10-g529: Эта is the demonstrative pronoun 'this' (feminine nominative singular) modifying общность. общность here is a nominative noun meaning a shared trait or commonality. была отмечена is a past passive construction: была (past of быть) + отмечена (past passive participle of отметить) = 'was noted'; ещё here means 'already/previously'; Н. is an abbreviated personal initial (an author’s initial) referring to who noted it.
segments.p2-10-g530: Страховым is the instrumental form of the surname Страхов (Strakhov). In Russian, the instrumental marks the agent in passive constructions and is also used in apposition here: 'by Strakhov.' The comma shows this is the start of a parenthetical relative clause about him.
segments.p2-10-g531: Которым is the relative pronoun in the instrumental, agreeing with the instrumental Страховым and introducing the relative clause 'who...'. в 1867 г. is a temporal prepositional phrase meaning 'in the year 1867' (в + year + г. abbreviation).
segments.p2-10-g532: в статье is a prepositional phrase: в + статья (prepositional case) = 'in the article' or 'in an article.' The prepositional case here marks the location/medium in which something was written.
segments.p2-10-g533: посвященной is a past passive participle agreeing with статье (feminine prepositional) meaning 'devoted to.' выходу is dative (посвящённый чему?) and together with в свет (a set phrase 'to come into the light/appear in print') means 'the appearance/publication.' Собрания сочинений Достоевского is a genitive chain: 'the Collected Works of Dostoevsky.' 1865-1866 гг. are the publication years (abbreviated).
segments.p2-10-g534: Punctuation mark (comma) linking the preceding clause to the verb 'писал'.
segments.p2-10-g535: писал is the past tense, masculine, singular of писать — 'he wrote.' It introduces the following direct quotation and is a straightforward narrative past tense.
segments.p2-10-g536: Отчуждение is a nominative noun meaning 'alienation' or 'estrangement.' от жизни is a prepositional phrase with от + genitive (жизни) meaning 'from life.' The whole phrase names the first element of the quoted list.
segments.p2-10-g537: Разрыв (nominative) 'a rupture' is joined with с действительностью ('with reality'); с takes the instrumental (действительностью). The following эта язва is an appositive (this ulcer/this scourge) that restates the preceding idea; Russian often juxtaposes such appositions without a copula.
segments.p2-10-g538: очевидно is an adverb meaning 'obviously' or 'apparently' and comments on the assertion in the quotation.
segments.p2-10-g539: существует is the present tense of существовать 'exists.' в русском обществе is a locative/prepositional phrase meaning 'in Russian society' (в + prepositional общество with русский agreeing in case and gender).
segments.p2-10-g570: «Трагизм состоит в сознании уродливости» is a simple clause: трагизм (nominative subject) + состоит (3rd person singular present of состоять, meaning “consists (in)”) which governs the prepositional phrase в сознании уродливости (‘in the consciousness of ugliness’). The following clause «Только я один вывел трагизм подполья» uses Только я один for emphasis (‘only I alone’), вы́вел (past, perfective) as the verb ‘brought out/presented’, and трагизм подполья as the direct object with подполья in the genitive (‘the tragedy of the underground’).
segments.p2-10-g571: Состоящий is a present active participle (agreeing with трагизм) meaning ‘consisting (in)’, and it begins a participial phrase that further modifies ‘the tragedy of the underground’. В страдании is a prepositional phrase (‘in suffering’) governed by the participle.
segments.p2-10-g572: В самоказни is another prepositional phrase in the same series introduced by the participle; самоказни is a literary/archaic noun meaning ‘self‑punishment’ or ‘self‑expiation’ and functions as the object of the preposition в here.
segments.p2-10-g573: This is a coordinate pair of prepositional phrases: в сознании лучшего (‘in the consciousness/awareness of the better’—лучшего used substantively, ‘the better/what is better’) and в невозможности достичь его (‘in the impossibility of attaining it’). Достичь is the infinitive ‘to reach/attain’ and его is the accusative object referring back to ‘the better’. The final и simply links on another item in the list.
segments.p2-10-g574: Главное is a parenthetical/neuter nominal used adverbially here: ‘above all’ or ‘the main thing’. It introduces the most important point that follows.
segments.p2-10-g575: В ярком убеждении этих несчастных is a prepositional phrase: в + убеждении (prepositional) ‘in the conviction’, with ярком (prepositional adjective agreeing with убеждении) = ‘vivid’ and этих несчастных (genitive plural) = ‘of these wretches/these unhappy ones’, marking whose conviction it is.
segments.p2-10-g576: Что introduces a content clause: ‘that’. И in «и все» here has the sense ‘even/all’ (emphatic), все is the subject ‘everyone/all’, and таковы is a short-form plural predicate meaning ‘are such’ or ‘are like that’. Together: ‘that everyone is like that.’
segments.p2-10-g577: А стало быть is a fixed connective meaning ‘and so/therefore/it follows that’. А is a coordinating conjunction linking the clauses, and стало быть is an impersonal verbal phrase (past of стать + быть) used idiomatically to draw the conclusion.
segments.p2-10-g578: Не стоит и исправляться is an impersonal construction: не стоит (with стоить used impersonally) = ‘it’s not worth / one shouldn’t’, и here carries the sense ‘even’, and исправляться is the reflexive infinitive ‘to reform oneself / to mend one’s ways’. The whole means ‘and therefore it is not even worth trying to reform oneself.’
segments.p2-10-g580: This is a main-clause subject + verb plus an adverbial phrase: 'Достоевский' is the subject (proper name), 'утверждал' is the past imperfective verb meaning 'asserted/maintained' (used here to report his conclusion), and 'в заключение' is an adverbial phrase meaning 'in conclusion' that frames the remark which follows. The comma shows that a subordinate clause (introduced by что) follows.
segments.p2-10-g581: 'что' introduces the subordinate clause 'that ...'. The quoted phrase 'причина подполья' functions as the grammatical subject of the subordinate clause. 'Кроется' is the present tense, third-person singular reflexive verb крыться meaning 'is hidden / lies (to be found)'; it takes the prepositional complement introduced by 'в'. The noun phrase 'уничтожении веры в общие правила' is the object of that preposition and means 'the destruction of faith in general rules' (note the internal prepositional construction 'вера в ...' = 'faith in ...').
segments.p2-10-g582: This is an impersonal negative existential: 'Нет' (here the existential 'there is not') is used with the genitive 'ничего' (nothing) and the adjective 'святого' (holy/sacred) to yield the idiom 'there is nothing sacred.' The token 'ДНет' appears to be a typographical error; the standard form is 'Нет'.
segments.p2-10-g583: The quoted noun phrase '"Подпольный парадоксалист"' is the subject. 'Выступает против' is a present-tense verbal expression meaning 'speaks out against' or 'is opposed to' (выступает + preposition против). 'Просветительской концепции человека' is a genitive noun phrase 'the Enlightenment (просветительской) conception (концепции) of man/human (человека)', functioning as the object of 'против'.
segments.p2-10-g584: This continues the coordination with another object of 'выступает против': 'против' again marks opposition; 'позитивистской абсолютизации' is a genitive phrase 'the positivist absolutization' (an abstract noun formed from 'абсолютизация'), and 'естественнонаучных методов' is genitive plural 'of the natural-scientific methods' modifying what is being absolutized.
segments.p2-10-g585: 'Особенно' is an adverb meaning 'especially' that narrows the preceding noun phrase to a particular subset; 'математических' is an adjective in genitive plural agreeing with 'методов' (understood from the prior phrase) meaning 'mathematical (methods)'.
segments.p2-10-g586: 'При' plus the noun in the prepositional case ('определении') yields a circumstantial phrase 'in (the) determining' or 'when determining'; 'законов человеческого бытия' is a genitive phrase 'the laws of human existence' serving as the object of 'определении'. Altogether: 'in determining the laws of human existence.'
segments.p2-10-g699: This is the single Cyrillic letter 'е' printed as a separate segment; here it most likely functions as the second letter in the abbreviation 'т. е.' (т.е. = 'то есть', 'that is'). As printed alone it looks like a typographic fragment rather than a full word; grammatically it has no inflection and is not acting as a lexical word by itself.
segments.p2-10-g700: 'после' is a preposition meaning 'after' and takes a noun in the genitive. 'опубликования' is the gerundial noun (verbal noun) 'publication' in the genitive, so together 'после опубликования' = 'after the publication of...'. The quoted title '"Записок из подполья"' is in the genitive plural ('Записок') and the genitive of 'подполье' ('подполья'), giving 'of "Notes from Underground"'; the whole phrase is an introductory adverbial (temporal) clause, marked off by the comma.
segments.p2-10-g701: 'дополнилось' is the short past, reflexive/passive form of 'дополнять' (here neuter singular), so 'was supplemented' or 'became supplemented' — the sentence omits an explicit subject and uses an impersonal/passive construction. 'новыми штрихами' is instrumental plural and indicates what was added: 'with new strokes/nuances/details' (instrumental is the typical case after 'дополняться' to indicate the additions).
segments.p2-10-g702: 'Так' is an adverb meaning 'thus' or 'so', used to introduce an example or consequence; here it functions as a discourse marker starting the sentence.
segments.p2-10-g703: This prepositional phrase begins with 'в' + prepositional case 'подстрочном примечании' = 'in the footnote' or 'in the marginal/interlinear note' (подстрочный = interlinear/footnote). 'Достоевского' is the genitive form showing possession: 'Dostoevsky's'. 'к статье Н.' uses 'к' + dative 'статье' to mean 'to/toward the article (by) N.' where 'Н.' is an abbreviation/initial standing for a name. Grammatically the whole phrase specifies location/authority: 'in Dostoevsky's footnote to N.'s article.'
segments.p2-10-g704: 'Соловьева' is the genitive of the author's name (Solovyov), marking authorship/possession. The quoted title '"Теория пользы и выгоды"' is the name of the work. 'сказано' is the short neuter past/passive of 'сказать' used impersonally: 'it is said' or 'one says' (introducing a quotation). The construction 'Автор + "Title" сказано:' is a standard way to attribute a statement and introduce a quote.
segments.p2-10-g705: This begins a correlative comparative clause introduced by 'Чем ...' (the first half of the 'the more... the more...' pattern). 'Чем' here functions as the comparative particle ('the more / the higher...'). 'выше' is the comparative 'higher' (from 'высоко'). 'будет' is the future tense of 'быть' ('will be') agreeing with the nominal predicate that follows. 'сознание и самоощущение' are the two nominative subjects/predicates ('consciousness and self-perception/self-awareness'), and 'своего собственного лица' is a genitive phrase ('of one's own person/face') modifying them — literally 'consciousness and self-awareness of one's own person.'
segments.p2-10-g706: This is the second half of the comparative pair: 'тем выше' ('the higher/so much the higher'). 'и' adds emphasis ('also'). 'наслаждение' ('pleasure') takes the infinitive 'жертвовать' ('to sacrifice') as its complement: 'the pleasure of sacrificing.' 'собой' (instrumental/accusative reflexive) 'oneself' and 'всей своей личностью' (instrumental) 'and with one's whole personality' indicate what is sacrificed. The final prepositional phrase 'из любви к человечеству' ('out of love for humanity') gives the motive; 'из' + genitive 'любви' + 'к' + dative 'человечеству' is a common idiom meaning 'out of love for humanity.'
segments.p2-10-g587: В начале 1860-х годов is a temporal prepositional phrase ('in the beginning of the 1860s'). ЭТИ ВЗГЛЯДЫ is the plural subject ('these views'). ПОЛУЧИЛИ РАЗВИТИЕ is a past-tense (plural) perfective construction meaning 'received development' or idiomatically 'developed / found expression'; the coordinating И + В ИДЕОЛОГИИ links that same verbal phrase to another location where the development occurred ('and (also) in the ideology ...'). Adjectives (русских революционных) agree in number, gender and case with демократов.
segments.p2-10-g588: В частности is a fixed adverbial phrase meaning 'in particular' or 'specifically' and serves to single out the following example.
segments.p2-10-g589: В теории + genitive phrase разумного эгоизма means 'in the theory of "reasonable egoism".' Теории is governed by the preposition в ('in'); разумного эгоизма is a genitive noun phrase modifying теория. The final Н. is an abbreviated initial indicating the author associated with that theory.
segments.p2-10-g590: Single-letter initial with a period; an abbreviated initial of a personal name (here separated out from the surname that follows). It is a bibliographical/attribution convention.
segments.p2-10-g591: Surname in the genitive singular (Чернышевского) used to indicate authorship/attribution (i.e. 'of Chernyshevsky' or simply the name following initials). It links to the preceding initials to form the full name.
segments.p2-10-g592: Numeral '1' likely marks a footnote or enumerated point. 'Основной полемический тезис' is a noun phrase in nominative (main + polemical + thesis) that functions as the subject of the sentence; adjectives precede the noun and agree with it in gender, number and case.
segments.p2-10-g593: Сформулированный is a past passive participle ('formulated') that modifies тезис. Достоевским is in the instrumental case marking the agent in a passive construction ('by Dostoevsky'). Ещё в "Зимних заметках" places the act earlier ('back in / already in the "Winter Notes"').
segments.p2-10-g594: Сводился к следующему is an idiomatic impersonal construction meaning 'amounted to the following'; сводился is the past-tense reflexive verb 'amounted / boiled down,' and следующему is a dative form meaning 'to the following' (introducing what follows).
segments.p2-10-g595: This clause states the thesis. 'Социализм не может быть осуществлен' is a passive impersonal construction ('socialism cannot be realized'), with не + может + быть + past participle. 'На принципе разумного договора личности и общества' is a prepositional phrase 'on the principle of a reasonable contract between the individual and society.' 'По формуле' means 'according to the formula' and introduces the quoted formula.
segments.p2-10-g596: Что is the subordinating conjunction 'that' introducing the explanatory subordinate clause (the reason why socialism cannot be realized).
segments.p2-10-g597: Как утверждал Достоевский is a parenthetical clause ('as Dostoevsky asserted/maintained') introducing the author's reported claim; как functions as 'as'/'how' here, and утверждал is past tense 'asserted.'
segments.p2-10-g598: This begins a direct quotation. 'Не хочет жить человек' is a general statement with человек as an impersonal/generic subject ('a person does not want to live'). 'И на этих расчетах' means 'even/also on these calculations/plans' (i.e. according to such calculations). Ему is dative ('to him'); 'все кажется сдуру' means 'everything seems, foolishly, to him' — сдуру is an adverb 'foolishly / in a fit of folly.' Word order places Ему before кажется for emphasis.
segments.p2-10-g599: Continuation of the quotation giving the content of what 'seems' to him: 'что это острог' = 'that this is a prison/stockade'; 'и что самому по себе лучше' = 'and that it is better by oneself/alone.' Самому по себе is an idiomatic cluster meaning 'on one's own / by oneself,' with самому in a dative/emphatic sense and по себе forming the reflexive idiom.
segments.p2-10-g600: Final elliptical statement of the quotation: потому - полная воля. Потому ('therefore / for that reason') followed by a dash and полная воля ('complete freedom' / 'absolute will')—the dash marks an emphatic, summarized conclusion of the quoted thought.
segments.p2-10-g733: Пародия Щедрина is a noun phrase where Щедрина is in the genitive and modifies пародия ("the parody of Shchedrin"). The dash functions as a copula (Russian often omits a present-tense "to be" and uses a dash to link subject and predicate noun). Единственный непосредственный отклик is a predicate nominative (a noun phrase in the nominative) qualifying that parody. На governs the following title ("Записки из подполья") and here means "in response to / to," with the quoted title including из подполья as part of the name.
segments.p2-10-g734: Интерес критики is the subject: интерес (nom.) modified by критики in the genitive ("the interest of criticism / of the critics"). К takes the dative here (к этой повести — "toward this novella" / "in this novella"); therefore этой повести is dative. Пробудился is a past-tense, perfective, reflexive form meaning "awakened" used figuratively; уже ("already") precedes the temporal phrase. После governs the genitive, so опубликования (a nominalized verb "publication") is genitive and is further specified by романа Достоевского (both genitive), with the quoted title naming the novel and the year in parentheses.
segments.p2-10-g735: Н. is an initial/abbreviation written as a single capital letter with a period. It functions as an isolated editorial mark or signature fragment; grammatically it is a sentence fragment standing for a name or initial, not a full lexical sentence.
segments.p2-10-g736: As above, Н. is a single-letter abbreviation (initial) followed by a period. It appears as an isolated item, serving as a shorthand reference or signature; there is no verb — it's a fragmentary form used in printed notes.
segments.p2-10-g641: Воображаемый is an adjective in the nominative singular masculine, modifying оппонент; it here means a hypothetical or 'imagined' interlocutor. The adjective precedes the noun in normal Russian word order; the quoted phrase beginning immediately after ("подпольного,") identifies whose opponent he is.
segments.p2-10-g642: человека appears in the quotation as part of the epithet "подпольного, человека" (the quoted label 'underground man'); grammatically it functions as the quoted noun. говорит is 3rd person singular present 'says'; ему is the dative pronoun 'to him' marking the addressee of the speech.
segments.p2-10-g643: Вы is the 2nd‑person pronoun used in direct address (here as the opponent speaks to the protagonist). хвалитесь is the present tense, reflexive verb 'хвалиться' meaning 'boast (of)', and сознанием is in the instrumental case because Russian хвалиться takes an instrumental object: 'boast of/claim to have X.'
segments.p2-10-g644: но is the contrastive conjunction 'but'; вы repeats the addressed pronoun; только is an adverb 'only/merely' limiting the verb; колеблетесь is 2nd person plural present of 'колебаться', here meaning 'waver' or 'hesitate' (imperfective action in progress).
segments.p2-10-g645: потому что introduces a causal clause 'because'; хоть ... и is a concessive construction meaning 'although/even though'; ум is the noun 'mind/intellect'; у вас is the standard possessive construction 'you have' (у + pronoun in genitive); и here is an emphatic particle ('and/even') reinforcing that the mind does indeed work; работает is 3rd person singular present 'works/functions'.
segments.p2-10-g646: но again contrasts 'but'; сердце is the noun 'heart' meaning the seat of feelings; ваше is the possessive 'your'; развратом is in the instrumental case indicating the cause or instrument of the passive state ('by depravity'); помрачено is a short passive participle/predicative meaning 'is clouded/darkened' (with impersonal passive sense agreeing with neuter сердце).
segments.p2-10-g647: а is a contrasting conjunction often translated 'and' or 'but' depending on context; без takes the genitive and here means 'without'; чистого сердца is genitive 'a pure heart'; the dash and the following полно́го are appositive/emphatic: полного modifies сердце (genitive) and means 'full/complete' — the author stacks adjectives for emphasis before the following noun phrase.
segments.p2-10-g648: правильного сознания is a genitive noun phrase 'correct/proper consciousness' used with the negative existential construction не будет ('there will not be'); не is the negation; будет is 3rd person singular future of 'быть' meaning 'there will be'. The whole clause expresses nonexistence: 'there will be no proper consciousness.' The ^ is a printing/annotation mark, not part of the grammar.
segments.p2-10-g649: Очевидно is an adverbial sentence‑introductory word meaning 'obviously' or 'it is evident'; it modifies the whole sentence and signals the author's assessment of what follows.
segments.p2-10-g650: в + noun in the prepositional (варианте) gives the locative phrase 'in the version'; доцензурном is an adjective in the prepositional agreeing with варианте meaning 'pre‑censorship/uncensored'; эта мысль is the nominative subject 'this thought/idea'; была развита is a past passive construction ('was developed/elaborated') with была marking past time and развита a past passive participle; еще более определенно is a comparative phrase 'even more clearly/definitely' (еще 'even/more', более 'more', определенно 'clearly/definitely').
segments.p2-10-g651: По + dative (утверждению) is a standard construction meaning 'according to (the statement)'; автора is the genitive indicating whose assertion it is: 'according to the author's assertion.' This entire phrase functions as an adverbial qualifier for the main clause.
segments.p2-10-g652: места is the nominative plural 'places' or 'passages' (here: textual places/passages) and serves as the subject of the clause that continues; the comma marks the relative clause that follows (где...).
segments.p2-10-g653: где is a relative pronoun 'where/in which'; он is the 3rd person pronoun 'he' (referring to the author or narrator); вывел is the past tense perfective of вывести meaning here 'brought out/set out/put forward' (presenting or deriving an idea); потребность is the noun 'need'; веры and Христа are in the genitive as the object of вывел ('the need of faith and of Christ'); см. is the abbreviation 'see' (reference).
segments.p2-10-g654: выше is an adverb used parenthetically to mean 'above' (referring to a previously cited place in the text); the closing parenthesis shows the parenthetical reference ends here.
segments.p2-10-g655: были is the past plural of 'to be' used here as an auxiliary for the passive construction; запрещены is the past passive participle meaning 'forbidden/prohibited' (the combination means 'were forbidden').
segments.p2-10-g656: "О том" is a prepositional phrase meaning "about that / concerning that." Here "о" is the preposition and "том" is the demonstrative pronoun in the prepositional/locational sense ('that' as the topic introduced by the following clause).
segments.p2-10-g657: This is a subordinate content clause introduced by "что" ("that"). "имел в виду" is the fixed expression "to have in view / to mean"; "имел" is past tense (masculine) of "иметь" and the subject "Достоевский" follows the verb (inverted word order is normal in Russian).
segments.p2-10-g658: "говоря о ..." is a gerundial (деепричастный) phrase meaning "speaking about / in speaking of ..." and sets the topic that Dostoevsky was referring to. The quoted noun phrase names what he was speaking about: "потребности веры и Христа" = "the need of faith and of Christ" (both "веры" and "Христа" are genitives inside the quoted phrase).
segments.p2-10-g659: This is an impersonal modal construction: "можно судить" = "one can judge / it is possible to judge." "по" + dative plural ("заметкам") means "by / according to the notes." "в записной тетради" is a locative phrase "in a notebook," and the years in parentheses specify the notebook's date.
segments.p2-10-g660: This segment is just a comma in the original; no lexical content to analyze.
segments.p2-10-g661: "сделанным" is the past passive participle (agreeing with "заметкам" in case/number) and together the phrase modifies the notes: "the notes, made shortly after...". "вскоре после опубликования 'Подполья'" means "shortly after the publication of 'The Underground'" — "после" takes the noun in genitive ("опубликования").
segments.p2-10-g662: "Упрекая" is a gerund (деепричастие) meaning "reproaching" or "while reproaching." It introduces the agent's action and is followed by the quoted object "социалистов-западников." "в том" here begins the clause "в том, что..." = "for the fact that..." (see next segment).
segments.p2-10-g663: "что они," begins the content clause "that they..."; "что" is the subordinating conjunction "that" and "они" is the pronoun subject "they." The comma closes the introductory clause leading to the reported content.
segments.p2-10-g664: "заботясь" is a gerund meaning "being concerned / caring" and describes the manner of "они" (they). "только о материальном благополучии человека" is the object of that concern: "only about the material well‑being of man" ("о" governs the prepositional case in Russian).
segments.p2-10-g665: This is a quoted proverbial phrase: "дальше брюха не идут." Literally "they do not go further than the belly;" figuratively "they do not get beyond the (physical) stomach." "дальше" is an adverb "further/any farther," "брюха" refers to "the belly," "не идут" is the verbal negation "do not go / do not proceed."
segments.p2-10-g666: "Достоевский писал" is the main clause: "Dostoevsky wrote." "в набросках к статье" means "in drafts/sketches for the article," where "набросках" is plural locative/dative of "набросок" (draft) and "к статье" indicates for/pertaining to the article titled after the colon.
segments.p2-10-g667: This is the quotation introduced by the colon. "Есть" functions as existential "there is," "нечто" = "something," "гораздо высшее" = "much higher / far higher" ("гораздо" intensifies the comparative "высшее"), and "бога-чрева" is a compound genitive "of the god of the belly" (literary/hypostatized phrase meaning the 'god' represented by appetite).
segments.p2-10-g668: "Это - быть..." uses the demonstrative "это" plus an infinitive to state a definition or explanation: "This is to be..." The infinitive "быть" acts as the predicate. "властелином и хозяином" are nominal complements in instrumental case meaning "a ruler and master." "даже себя самого" is emphatic reflexive "even of oneself" ("самого" intensifies "себя").
segments.p2-10-g669: "своего я" = "one's own 'I' / one's own ego." "своего" is the possessive pronoun agreeing with "я" in form (here in genitive/accusative context as part of the noun phrase); "я" is used as a substantive meaning the 'I' or ego rather than the personal pronoun in a clause.
segments.p2-10-g670: "пожертвовать" is an infinitive meaning "to sacrifice / to give up" and governs an instrumental (here "этим я" = "this I" in instrumental case) — Russian verbs like "пожертвовать" take the instrument to mark what is sacrificed. The gerund/infinitive sequence continues the definition begun by "Это - быть...": "...to sacrifice that 'I'."
segments.p2-10-g671: "отдать его - всем" continues the sequence of infinitives: "to give it / to hand it over to everyone." "его" is the pronoun referring back to "я" (object form: 'it/him'), and "всем" is dative plural "to everyone." The dash marks a slight pause/emphasis before the final object.
segments.p2-10-g601: Вся первая часть повести is the subject: an attributive chain ('вся' + ordinal 'первая' + noun 'часть' + genitive 'повести') meaning 'the whole first part of the novella'. The title in dashes ("Подполье") is an appositive renaming that subject. Является is the formal present-tense copula and развитием is a predicate noun in the instrumental case (used with являться) meaning 'a development of', followed by the genitive этой мысли 'of this thought/idea'.
segments.p2-10-g602: Оперируя is a деепричастие (gerund) meaning 'operating/working with' and sets up a circumstantial background action. Тезисами и понятиями are in the instrumental plural and indicate the means ('by/with theses and concepts').
segments.p2-10-g603: Близкими agrees in case and number with the preceding nouns (instrumental plural) and functions as a postmodifier: 'close/similar to'. В отдельных случаях is an adverbial phrase 'in particular cases'. К философским идеям takes the preposition к + dative 'идеям' and Канта is the genitive proper name 'of Kant'.
segments.p2-10-g604: Шопенгауэра is another item in the list of names in the same genitive case governed by 'к ... идеям' (i.e., 'to the ideas of Schopenhauer'). The trailing comma shows continuation of the enumeration.
segments.p2-10-g605: Штирнера continues the enumerated list in the same genitive case (ideas of Stirner).
segments.p2-10-g606: Герой 'Записок из подполья' is the main subject (hero/protagonist of the titled work); 'Записок из подполья' is a genitive construction specifying which hero. Утверждает is the present-tense verb 'asserts' that introduces the reported content (a subordinate clause follows).
segments.p2-10-g607: Что is the subordinating conjunction 'that' introducing the content clause. Философский материализм просветителей is a noun phrase (philosophical materialism of the Enlighteners) with просветителей in the genitive qualifying материализм.
segments.p2-10-g608: Взгляды is another coordinated subject noun ('views'); представителей is genitive ('views of representatives'), and утопического социализма и позитивистов are genitive attributes naming which representatives ('of utopian socialism and of the positivists').
segments.p2-10-g609: Равно как и is a conjunctional phrase 'as well as/just as' linking the previous coordinated subjects to the next one. Абсолютный идеализм Гегеля is another nominative subject phrase ('Hegel's absolute idealism').
segments.p2-10-g610: Неизбежно is an adverb 'inevitably' modifying the verb ведут (present plural 'lead to'). К фатализму and отрицанию свободы воли are the dative objects introduced by к and by the noun phrase 'denial of free will' (отрицанию + genitive свободы воли — 'freedom of will').
segments.p2-10-g611: Которую is a feminine accusative relative pronoun referring back to свободу/свободы (the idea 'free will'); он ставит is the main clause 'he places/puts', and превыше всего is an idiomatic adverbial phrase 'above all' (превыше + genitive всего). The relative clause states that he values free will above everything.
segments.p2-10-g612: Свое собственное is an emphatic double possessive: 'one's own, one's very own.' Both words are neuter singular to agree with the following noun (хотенье). This is a poetic/repetitive intensification.
segments.p2-10-g613: Вольное и свободное are coordinate adjectives ('voluntary and free') modifying хотенье, an older-form noun (хотенье = desire). The adjectives precede and agree in gender/number (neuter singular) with хотенье.
segments.p2-10-g614: A reporting clause inside the quote: dash + говорит он ('he says') — the verb-first order (говорит он) is common in spoken or quoted reported speech and the dashes mark the interruption of the direct quotation.
segments.p2-10-g615: Another emphatic possessive phrase: свой собственный ('one's own, personal') standing alone with dashes/commas as an appositive repetition for emphasis; it continues the list of what he values.
segments.p2-10-g616: Хотя бы is a modal/hypothetical phrase meaning 'even if' or 'even'; бы is the conditional/optative particle adding concession. Самый дикий каприз is a superlative noun phrase 'the very wildest caprice' (самый + adjective + noun).
segments.p2-10-g617: Своя фантазия is another item in the enumerated set: 'one's own imagination'. Своя is a possessive pronoun agreeing in gender (feminine) with фантазия.
segments.p2-10-g618: Раздраженная is a past passive participle agreeing with фантазия (feminine singular): 'irritated/angered'. Иногда is an adverb 'sometimes'. Хоть бы даже до сумасшествия is an idiomatic cluster meaning 'even to the point of madness', with до governing the genitive сумасшествия.
segments.p2-10-g619: Вот это-то все и есть is a strongly emphatic copular phrase: вот это-то ('this very thing/these very things') + всё ('all/entirety') + и есть ('is indeed/precisely'). Та самая continues as a demonstrative 'the very one' (feminine) pointing back to the enumerated qualities.
segments.p2-10-g620: Пропущенная is a past participle (feminine to agree with та самая) meaning 'omitted' or 'overlooked'. It functions as an adjective in this appositive chain.
segments.p2-10-g621: Самая выгодная выгода is a tautological emphatic phrase: 'the very most advantageous gain/benefit.' Самая is a superlative intensifier and выгодная modifies выгода ('advantage/benefit').
segments.p2-10-g622: Которая begins a relative clause modifying выгода. Ни ... не is a negation with ни adding emphasis ('not under any ...'). Под какую классификацию не подходит literally 'does not fit under any classification' (под + accusative here idiomatically means 'under/into a classification'). От которой uses the preposition от + relative pronoun (genitive) 'from which', and все системы и теории постоянно разлетаются к черту is a vivid idiom 'all systems and theories constantly fly apart to the devil' meaning they disintegrate in the face of that gain.
segments.p2-10-g623: Сопоставление is a verbal noun in nominative meaning 'the comparison' or 'juxtaposition'. "Записок из подполья" is in the genitive and names the work being compared; со статьями uses the preposition со (with) + instrumental 'статьями' indicating 'with the articles of Dostoevsky', and the years 1861–1864 гг. specify which articles.
segments.p2-10-g719: The single letter with a period is an abbreviation (capitalized) used in bibliographic citations: 'С.' stands for 'страница/страницы' (page/pages). It functions as a short label for the following numeral segment and is not declined here.
segments.p2-10-g720: A numeral with a closing parenthesis and punctuation; here it supplies the page number that the preceding abbreviation labels (i.e. completes the citation 'p. 13'). The parenthesis and period are punctuation, not part of the numeral's grammatical form.
segments.p2-10-g721: Многое is a neuter substantive-pronoun meaning 'much' or 'many things' and serves as the main topic; из того is a genitive prepositional phrase 'of that' modifying многое. Together they form 'much of that...' introducing a subordinate clause.
segments.p2-10-g722: Что introduces a relative/subordinate clause 'that/which'. В + Записках (locative plural of the title 'Записки') marks location 'in "Notes from Underground"'. Только is an adverb 'only/merely' and намечено is a short-form passive participle (neuter) meaning 'is outlined/sketched'; it agrees in gender/number with многое.
segments.p2-10-g723: Было развито is a past passive construction: было (past auxiliary/impersonal marker) + short perfective passive participle развито = 'was developed/elaborated'. В последующих романах Достоевского is a locative time/place phrase 'in Dostoevsky's subsequent novels' (последующих and романах are in the prepositional plural after в).
segments.p2-10-g724: И в частности is an idiom meaning 'and in particular'. В первом из них uses в + ordinal первый in the prepositional singular ('in the first') plus из них 'of them'—a construction meaning 'the first among them' (первом agrees with implied noun 'романе', masculine).
segments.p2-10-g725: В followed by the titles in the prepositional case: "Преступлении и наказании" are the prepositional forms of the nouns 'преступление' and 'наказание', so the phrase reads 'in "Crime and Punishment"' (the title of the novel).
segments.p2-10-g726: Вышедшая в свет is a participial phrase from выйти в свет meaning 'having come out into the (public) light' i.e. 'published'. В конце марта 1864 г. is a temporal prepositional phrase 'at the end of March 1864' (г. is the abbreviation for год/года). The participle is adjectival and typically modifies an implied noun (e.g. 'the work/book').
segments.p2-10-g624: "и" is the simple coordinating conjunction linking this clause to the previous one; the title phrase "Зимними заметками о летних впечатлениях" is in the instrumental case (Зимними заметками) and functions adverbially: literally 'by/with "Winter Notes on Summer Impressions"'; "со всей очевидностью" is an adverbial phrase 'with full obviousness / most clearly'; "убеждает" is present tense, indicative, active, 'convince/show', and "в том" (в + demonstrative pronoun том) introduces the content clause that follows (i.e. 'in that ...').
segments.p2-10-g625: "что" introduces the subordinate clause ('that ...'); "герой подполья" is the nominative subject ('the hero of the underground') where "подполья" is a genitive attributive to герой; "воплощает в себе" is a present-tense transitive verb phrase with the reflexive pronoun 'в себе' meaning 'embodies in himself'; "конечные результаты" is the direct object ('the final/ultimate results'); the noun phrase "Доторванности от почвы" names the quality/condition being embodied (literal: 'being torn away from the soil').
segments.p2-10-g626: "как" here means 'as' or 'in the way that'; "она" (feminine pronoun) refers back to the quoted notion (Доторванности) and functions as the grammatical subject of "рисовалась"; "рисовалась" is the imperfective, past, passive/reflexive form 'was drawn/was presented' or 'presented itself' — the dative "Достоевскому" indicates the person to whom it was thus presented ('as it appeared to Dostoevsky').
segments.p2-10-g627: "и потому" is a connective adverbial phrase 'and therefore/for that reason'; "этот персонаж" is the nominative subject 'this character'; "не только" begins a correlative negation ('not only'), introducing the first member of a 'not only ... but also' construction; "обличитель" is a noun meaning 'one who exposes or denounces (a moral accuser)'.
segments.p2-10-g628: "но и" completes the correlative construction ('but also'); "обличаемый" is a present passive-participial form used substantively here: 'the one who is exposed / who is accused' and grammatically agrees with персонаж as a predicate noun.
segments.p2-10-g629: "не герой," is a straightforward predicative nominal phrase: "не" negates the predicate noun "герой" — 'not a hero.'
segments.p2-10-g630: "но" ('but') introduces the contrastive predicate that follows; "антигерой" is a quoted noun ('antihero') used as the contrasting label for the character.
segments.p2-10-g631: "по выражению" is an idiomatic prepositional phrase meaning 'in the expression/phrase of' or more naturally 'in the words of'; "самого автора" is a genitive phrase emphasizing 'the author himself' (самого intensifies автора), so the whole phrase means 'in the author's own words.'
segments.p2-10-g632: The numeral '2' is a list marker; "Проповедуя" is a деепричастие (adverbial participle) 'preaching' (indicating an accompanying action or stance) and takes "свободу" in the accusative as its object — 'preaching freedom.'
segments.p2-10-g633: "подпольный человек" is the subject phrase 'the underground man/person' (подпольный as adjective); "в действительности" is an adverbial phrase 'in actuality/in reality'; "ратует за" is a fixed verbal construction meaning 'advocates/champions for' (ратует + за + accusative), followed by the quoted object "свободу от выбора" ('freedom from choice').
segments.p2-10-g634: "от" ('from') marks separation; "обязывающих" is a present active participle used adjectivally ('obligating/binding') modifying "решений" in the genitive plural — together: 'from binding/obligatory decisions.'
segments.p2-10-g635: "Его" is the possessive/genitive pronoun 'his' modifying the noun "эгоцентризм"; "порожден" is a short passive participle/past passive form meaning 'is produced/caused' (literally 'generated by'); "страхом" is the instrumental case ('by fear') and "перед" plus the following noun (жизнью — given in the quote) yields the phrase 'fear of life' or 'fear in the face of life.'
segments.p2-10-g636: "3" is a list numeral; "Заставляя" is a деепричастие (adverbial participle) 'by forcing/making' and here governs the following clause: it takes "своего героя" in the accusative ('his hero') as its direct object; "в качестве 'головного'" is a prepositional phrase 'in the capacity/as the "main"' — indicating the role assigned to the hero (головного is an adjective used substantively/quoted as 'main').
segments.p2-10-g637: "теоретического тезиса" is a genitive attribute 'of the theoretical thesis' qualifying the role; "проповедовать" is the infinitive 'to preach/advocate' which is what the hero is forced to do; "доведенную до логического предела программу" is an accusative noun phrase 'a program brought to the logical limit' where "доведенную" is a past passive participle agreeing with программу and "до логического предела" is the complement 'up to the logical limit'; "крайнего индивидуализма" is a genitive noun phrase 'of extreme individualism' modifying программу.
segments.p2-10-g638: "Достоевский наметил" is a past-tense clause 'Dostoevsky outlined/sketched'; "уже" 'already' qualifies the timing; "в первой части 'Записок из подполья'" is a locative specification ('in the first part of "Notes from Underground"'); the coordinating "и возможный" ('and also a possible') leads into the noun that follows (выход), so syntactically Dostoevsky 'outlined ... also a possible ...'.
segments.p2-10-g639: "с его точки зрения" is an idiom meaning 'from his point of view' or 'in his opinion': "с" here means 'from', "его" 'his' (referring back to Dostoevsky), and "точки зрения" is the fixed collocation 'point of view' (точка + зрения in the genitive).
segments.p2-10-g640: "выход" is the noun 'a way out / exit' (here: 'a possible way out'); "из этого состояния" is a prepositional-genitive phrase 'out of this state/condition' where "из" marks movement/separation and "этого состояния" is the genitive object 'of this condition.'
segments.p2-10-g686: "Тема этого стихотворения" is a noun phrase: тема (subject/theme) modified by the genitive attribute этого стихотворения ('of this poem'). "варьируется" is the reflexive present 3rd-person singular of варьироваться and here functions as a passive/impersonal: 'is varied' / 'varies'. The phrase "и в повести" uses и as a linking 'also' and the prepositional phrase в повести ('in the novella') indicates the domain where the variation occurs.
segments.p2-10-g687: Где is a relative adverb ('where / in which') referring back to в повести. Она is a feminine pronoun referring to тема. Подвергается is the reflexive imperfective present (подвергаться) in 3rd-person singular: 'is subjected to' / 'undergoes' and requires a dative object (here: переосмыслению).
segments.p2-10-g688: Однако is a discourse connective meaning 'however' / 'nevertheless' and here signals contrast with what came before; punctuation shows a slight parenthetical pause.
segments.p2-10-g689: Глубокому переосмыслению is a dative noun phrase ('to/into a profound rethinking') required by подвергается. Глубокому is dative singular adjective agreeing with the noun переосмыслению (a nominalization meaning 'reinterpretation'/'rethinking').
segments.p2-10-g690: Как и introduces a parallel: 'as (do) the themes ...'. Темы is nominative plural ('themes') and is governed in sense by the parallelism with the earlier 'тема'. Жоржсандовских повестей is a genitive phrase 'of George Sand's novellas', where жоржсандовских is an adjectival form and повестей is genitive plural; 1850-х годов is an attributive genitive specifying the decade ('of the 1850s').
segments.p2-10-g691: The dash plus the repeated noun переосмыслению places the word in apposition and emphasis: it restates the idea ('— a rethinking') as the thing undergone by those themes as well. The form is dative to agree with подвергается.
segments.p2-10-g692: Включающему is a present active participle (dative singular) modifying переосмыслению: 'including (in itself)'. В себя is the reflexive phrase 'in itself' / 'within it'. Сочувственное и одновременно полемическое отношение is the noun phrase 'a sympathetic and at the same time polemical attitude', where одновременно is an adverb 'simultaneously/at the same time'. К ним ('toward them') is a dative prepositional phrase referring back to the themes/novellas noted earlier.
segments.p2-10-g693: Между + instrumental (Лизой) marks the two parties in the conflict: 'the conflict between Liza' (Лизой). The dash introduces an appositive description: носительницей 'живой жизни' ('the bearer/representative of "living life"'), where носительницей is instrumental feminine agreeing with Лизой. The second party is given by the coordinated epithets "мертворожденным" "небывалым общечеловеком" in instrumental to parallel Лизой; these are quoted epithets attached to the opposing figure.
segments.p2-10-g694: Парадоксалистом is another appositive in instrumental (quoted term: 'paradoxalist' / 'contrarian') specifying the opposing figure; из подполья ('from the underground') is a prepositional phrase with из + genitive indicating origin. Кончается is a present 3rd-person singular reflexive verb meaning 'ends' / 'concludes (in/with)', and here governs the instrumental noun phrase нравственной победой героини ('with the heroine's moral victory'), where героини is genitive ('of the heroine').
segments.p2-10-g695: В облике этой героини is a locative phrase ('in the appearance/portrait of this heroine'), with этой героини in the genitive modifying облике. Нашли отражение is past plural of найти used impersonally: 'were found to be reflected' / 'found expression'. Некоторые черты ('some traits') is the subject of нашли отражение; strongly developed (сильно развитой) is an adjectival phrase in genitive agreeing with личности ('of a strongly developed personality').
segments.p2-10-g696: О которой is a relative pronoun ('about which') referring back to личности. Достоевский писал ещё в «Зимних заметках» uses писaл (imperfective past) to say Dostoevsky had written earlier in that work; ещё here conveys 'already' / 'previously'. И представление о которой в конце 1864 г. is another clause parallel to the previous relative: 'and the conception/idea of which (appears/is noted) at the end of 1864' — the sentence is elliptical after this point, but grammatically the second 'которой' again refers to личность.
segments.p2-10-g697: A comma; here punctuation only, no lexical content.
segments.p2-10-g698: Т. is an abbreviation (т. = том) used in bibliographic references to mean 'volume' (usually rendered in English as 'vol.').
segments.p2-10-g707: Здесь is an adverbial place/setting marker at the clause start ('here', 'in this respect'). Человек is the nominative subject ('a man/person') and is left bare because it will be modified by the following participial phrases.
segments.p2-10-g708: Пренебрегающий is a present active participle (from пренебрегать) agreeing with человек (nominative singular masculine) and meaning 'disregarding/neglecting'. Своими правами is the possessive + noun in the instrumental plural because the verb пренебрегать governs the instrumental case: 'neglecting his own rights'.
segments.p2-10-g709: Возносящийся is another present active (reflexive) participle modifying человек, meaning 'rising/exalting (himself)'. Над governs the instrumental, so ними ('them') refers back to права: 'above them'. The participle chain describes the same subject.
segments.p2-10-g710: Принимает is the main verb, 3rd person singular present indicative of принимать ('assumes/adopts'). Какой-то is the indefinite adjectival phrase ('some kind of' — какой + particle то), торжественный is an adjective ('solemn'), and образ is the noun ('appearance/air/form'); together: 'assumes some kind of solemn air'.
segments.p2-10-g711: Несравнимо is an adverbial intensifier 'incomparably' modifying высший. Высший is an elevated/comparative/superlative adjective ('higher, loftier'); образ is the noun 'image/form'. Всесветного is an adjectival modifier in the same noun phrase ('of the whole world' / 'world-renowned') and stands in the genitive because образ is followed by an of-phrase.
segments.p2-10-g712: Хотя бы и is a concessive phrase meaning 'even if' or 'even though' (хотя = 'although/though', бы = particle marking the concessive/conditional sense, и adds 'even'). Гуманного кредитора is in the genitive as a specification of образ: 'of a humane creditor (even so)'.
segments.p2-10-g713: Благоразумного is an adjective ('prudent') presented here as an appositive modifier of кредитора; it matches the noun's case and gender (genitive masculine singular) and emphasizes the creditor's prudence.
segments.p2-10-g714: Еще одно concessive parenthesis: хотя бы и гуманно ('even if humanely' / 'even if acting humanely'). Here гуманно is an adverb qualifying the manner (it comments on the creditor's behavior), while хотя бы и keeps the concessive tone.
segments.p2-10-g715: Занимающегося is a present active participle (agreeing with кредитора in case and gender) from заниматься: 'who occupies himself/engages (in)'. Всю свою жизнь is an accusative duration phrase 'all his life'. Определением is in the instrumental because заниматься governs the instrumental: 'engaged in the determination of'. Того is the genitive demonstrative referring forward to the following subordinate clause ('that which...').
segments.p2-10-g716: Что introduces the noun-clause 'what is mine and what is yours' as the content of того. Мое and твое are neuter substantive possessives acting as predicates ('what is mine' / 'what is yours'). Эпоха in parentheses is a source/title citation (here printed after a closing quotation mark).
segments.p2-10-g717: A year/date marker presented as a numeral; functions as a bibliographic or temporal label.
segments.p2-10-g718: The letter N here functions as a typographic placeholder/abbreviation (No.). It precedes the issue/number 11; the segment is a bibliographic label.
segments.p2-10-g727: This is a full noun-phrase plus predicate. "первая часть" is an ordinal + noun (subject). "Записок из подполья" is the title in genitive (literally "of Notes from Underground"). "тотчас же" is an adverbial phrase meaning "immediately" with "же" adding emphasis. "обратила на себя внимание" is a fixed transitive expression meaning "drew attention to itself" ("обратила" is past tense, feminine singular to agree with "часть"). Finally "революционно-демократического лагеря" is a genitive attribute "of the revolutionary-democratic camp" (the camp is the source/possessor of the attention).
segments.p2-10-g728: "Щедрин включил в свое обозрение 'Литературные мелочи' 'драматическую быль' - памфлет 'Стрижи'" is a main clause: "Щедрин" is the subject; "включил" is past tense, perfective, meaning "included"; "в свое обозрение" is a prepositional phrase "in his review" ("свое" agrees with subject); "'драматическую быль' - памфлет 'Стрижи'" is the direct object (a quoted genre label followed by an appositive naming the pamphlet). The participle "Высмеивая" that follows begins a participial phrase modifying the subject.
segments.p2-10-g729: "он под видом 'стрижа четвертого'" is a short clause where "он" (he) is the subject and "под видом" is a prepositional phrase meaning "under the guise/appearance of" (metaphorical 'under'). "стрижа" is the genitive of "стриж" (swift) used here as the noun inside the quoted guise; "четвертого" is an ordinal in genitive agreeing with "стрижа" ("the fourth swift").
segments.p2-10-g730: This continues the clause: "беллетриста унылого' изобразил Ф." — "беллетриста унылого" is a noun phrase in the accusative (animate masculine accusative identical to genitive) meaning "a gloomy/bitter belletrist (an author of light fiction)"; "изобразил" is the past tense verb "portrayed/depicted"; "Ф." is an initial/abbreviation standing for the person's given name (here part of the initials for Dostoevsky).
segments.p2-10-g731: A single-letter initial "М." here functions as an abbreviation (initial of the patronymic or middle name). In Russian printed names it's common to show initials like "Ф. М." before a surname; this is just the second initial.
segments.p2-10-g732: "Достоевского." is the surname in the accusative form used with an animate direct object; the form coincides with the genitive. It completes the name given by initials ("Ф. М. Достоевского") and identifies the person portrayed.
segments.p2-10-g785: A short nominal title: 'Толковый' is an attributive adjective in the nominative singular masculine agreeing with the noun 'словарь'. There is no verb; this is an elliptical label meaning an explanatory (i.e., defining) dictionary.
segments.p2-10-g788: '2-е' is the abbreviated ordinal numeral for 'второе' (second) written in short bibliographic form; 'изд.' is an abbreviation for 'издание' (edition). Together they form the bibliographic phrase '2-е изд.' = 'second edition.' The periods mark standard abbreviations.
segments.p2-10-g789: Abbreviated place-name 'СПб.' stands for 'Санкт-Петербург' (Saint Petersburg) in publishing information; the final period marks the abbreviation. Functionally this is a location label in the imprint.
segments.p2-10-g790: A punctuation segment: the semicolon separates items in the imprint (here, two publication locations).
segments.p2-10-g791: The form 'М.' is an abbreviation; in publishing contexts it would normally stand for 'Москва' (Moscow). The provided lemma/sense list does not match that meaning, so the sense is marked as not applicable here.
segments.p2-10-g792: A comma punctuation mark separating items in a bibliographic line (for example, separating a place or publisher from the year). It links the preceding element to the year that follows.
segments.p2-10-g793: A four-digit calendar year given as the publication date in a citation. The final period serves to end the bibliographic entry or sentence.
segments.p2-10-g794: Abbreviation (Т.) of том (tom) meaning “volume” in a bibliographic citation; the period marks the abbreviation. It is nominative and typically precedes the volume number in the next segment.
segments.p2-10-g795: Cardinal numeral indicating the volume number (here: 2). The trailing period is typographic, marking the end of the abbreviated element or the citation fragment; it pairs with the preceding abbreviation Т.
segments.p2-10-g796: Abbreviation (С.) for страница/страницы meaning “page(s)” in a bibliographic reference; the period marks the abbreviation. It normally precedes the specific page range or numbers (not shown here).
segments.p2-10-g797: This segment is just a printed reference number with closing punctuation. It functions typographically (a footnote or endnote number) and contains no grammatical elements to parse.
segments.p2-10-g801: всего is the genitive singular form of весь here meaning 'of all' (not 'only'), governing the following substantivized adjectives. прекрасного and высокого are adjectives used as nouns (substantivized) in the genitive to agree with всего, coordinated by и ('and'); the closing quote and punctuation indicate this is the end of a quoted phrase.
segments.p2-10-g804: Сочетание (nominative singular) is the subject meaning 'the combination' and takes a genitive complement (понятий = 'of notions'). The quoted phrase "прекрасное и высокое" are adjectives used substantively to name those notions. Восходит is present tense, 3rd person singular of восходить meaning 'traces back' or 'originates', and governs the prepositional phrase к + dative (к эстетическим трактатам = 'to/into the aesthetic treatises'). XVIII в. is an abbreviated time expression meaning 'the 18th century' (в. = век).
segments.p2-10-g805: см. is an abbreviation of 'смотри' or 'см.' used in references, meaning 'see' or 'cf.'; here it begins a parenthetical reference and carries no inflection.
segments.p2-10-g746: 'С.' here is an abbreviation used in citations meaning 'стр.' or 'страницы' (page(s)); it stands alone as a bibliographic marker and functions like 'p.' in English.
segments.p2-10-g747: A page number closing a parenthetical citation: Arabic numerals followed by a closing parenthesis and period. No lexical analysis is required here.
segments.p2-10-g748: 'Заслугу' is the accusative singular of 'заслуга' (the object: 'the merit'); placing it before the subject ('критик') is a topicalizing word order that stresses what is valued. 'Достоевского' is in the genitive modifying 'заслугу' ('Dostoevsky's merit'). 'критик видел' is past tense, masculine singular, with 'видел' agreeing with the noun 'критик'. 'в том,' ('in that,') is a fixed phrase introducing the content clause that follows ('в том, что ...').
segments.p2-10-g749: 'что' is the complementizer 'that' introducing the subordinate clause; 'он' is the explicit subject 'he' (referring to Dostoevsky) and is in nominative. The comma after 'он' marks the participial construction that follows.
segments.p2-10-g750: 'сумев' is a short-form past active participle (or gerund: деепричастие) from 'суметь' meaning 'having managed' or 'having succeeded'; it links to the main predicate 'умеет' and shows a prior accomplishment. 'заглянуть в душу' is a perfective infinitive 'to peer/ look into the soul' with 'в' taking accusative 'душу'. 'подпольного героя' is a genitive attributive phrase 'of the underground hero' (подпольного modifying герой). The participle + infinitive bundle describes what he has accomplished before the main verb.
segments.p2-10-g751: 'с такою же проницательностью' is an adverbial phrase 'with the same insight/penetration'; note the archaic/poetic instrumental form 'такою' (modern 'такой') and 'проницательностью' in the instrumental case. 'умеет' is present tense, 3rd person singular of 'уметь' meaning 'is able' / 'knows how'. 'изображать' is the imperfective infinitive 'to depict/portray'. The conjunction 'и' here has the sense 'also' (adding another ability). The direct object 'всевозможные варьяции этих нравственных шатаний' follows and names what he can depict.
segments.p2-10-g752: 'все виды страданий' is a noun phrase 'all kinds/kinds of suffering' with 'все' (all) modifying plural 'виды' (kinds/types) and 'страданий' in the genitive plural (objects of 'порождаемых' that follows). Word order places the generalizing phrase before the participial modifier.
segments.p2-10-g753: 'порождаемых' is a passive present participle (short attributive form) meaning 'generated/produced by' and governs the following instrumental or ablative cause phrase; here it takes the agent/cause expressed by 'нравственною неустойчивостью' (archaic instrumental ending '-ою' instead of modern '-ой'). '(там же)' is a parenthetical citation 'there/ibid.' indicating the same source. The following sentence 'Высокую оценку "Запискам из подполья" дал Ап.' uses fronted object 'Высокую оценку' (accusative) for emphasis, the title in dative 'Запискам из подполья' (to/for the Notes from Underground) and the verb 'дал' (gave) in past tense with the subject 'Ап.' (abbreviated proper name) placed after the verb — a common Russian word order for emphasis or stylistic inversion.
segments.p2-10-g754: 'Григорьев' is a proper name (here in nominative) — the surname of the person referred to; presented alone it functions as the subject of the preceding verb 'дал' by context.
segments.p2-10-g755: A short prepositional phrase: 'В' is the preposition 'in' governing the prepositional case 'письме' (singular of 'письмо'), and 'к' is the preposition 'to' governing the dative represented by the abbreviation 'Н.'; together they mean 'in a letter to N.' (Russian has no articles, so English adds 'a').
segments.p2-10-g756: A single-letter abbreviation used as a name-initial; here it stands alone as the conventional way to refer obliquely to a person (the period shows it's an initial/abbrev.).
segments.p2-10-g757: 'Страхову' is the dative form of the surname Strakhov, marking the recipient ('to Strakhov'); 'от' here introduces the dating of the letter ('dated' or 'of'), followed by the genitive date '18 (30) марта 1869 г.' (the parentheses show old/new-style dates).
segments.p2-10-g758: 'Достоевский' is the subject (proper name) and 'вспоминал' is the past-tense, imperfective verb meaning 'recalled' or 'was remembering'; the imperfective past here reports a recollection introducing reported speech.
segments.p2-10-g759: The conjunction 'что' introduces the content clause; 'Григорьев' is the subject of that clause, 'похвалил' is the perfective past ('praised' — a completed action), 'эту повесть' is the accusative direct object 'this tale/novella', and 'и сказал ему' links a second verb ('and said to him') with 'ему' marking the indirect object (dative).
segments.p2-10-g760: Direct quoted speech: 'Ты' is the informal 2nd-person pronoun subject, 'в этом роде' is a locative/prepositional phrase meaning 'in this kind/way' (i.e. 'in this vein'), 'и' here functions as a colloquial particle akin to 'just/and' adding emphasis before the imperative, and 'пиши' is the 2nd-person singular imperative 'write' (command/suggestion).
segments.p2-10-g737: Subject + locative phrase + verb: 'Страхов' is the author’s surname in the nominative; 'в статье "Наша изящная словесность"' is a prepositional phrase (в + prepositional) specifying where he wrote; 'подчеркивал' is past imperfective, masculine singular, indicating he repeatedly/emphatically stated this (hence the comma introducing reported speech).
segments.p2-10-g738: 'что' introduces reported content ('that'); the quoted phrase begins with the label '"подпольный человек"' (literally 'underground man'). 'со злобой' = with malice: 'со' is the variant of 'с' used before certain consonant clusters and governs an instrumental noun ('злобой'). 'относится' is present, 3rd person singular, imperfective, meaning 'treats/relates (habitually)'; 'к действительности' is 'toward/to reality' (preposition к governs the dative).
segments.p2-10-g739: Prepositional + dative phrase expanding what he treats with malice: 'к каждому явлению' ('to/at every phenomenon') — 'каждому' is dative singular modifying 'явлению'; 'скудной жизни' is a genitive attribute ('of the meager life'), so the whole means 'to every phenomenon of the meager life'.
segments.p2-10-g740: 'его окружающей' is a participial phrase attached to 'жизни' from the previous segment: 'окружающей' is the present active participle/adjectival form agreeing with 'жизни' (feminine), and 'его' is the pronoun functioning as the participle's object ('surrounding him'). Word order is literary: noun phrase followed by its participial modifier.
segments.p2-10-g741: 'потому что' = 'because' introduces the reason clause. 'Каждое такое явление' is the grammatical subject ('each such phenomenon'); 'его обижает' — 'обижает' is present, 3rd person singular transitive 'offends/hurts', and 'его' here is the accusative object 'him' (it offends him). 'как укор' = 'as a reproach' (simile/comparison).
segments.p2-10-g742: 'как обличение его собственной внутренней безжизненности' continues the comparison: 'обличение' (a noun) here means 'exposure' or 'exposure/revelation'; 'его собственной внутренней безжизненности' is a noun phrase in genitive ('of his own inner lifelessness') where 'собственной' ('own') intensifies the possessive 'его' and the adjectives agree in case, number, gender with 'безжизненности.' The parenthetical '(Отеч.' is an abbreviation of the source.
segments.p2-10-g743: 'зап.' is an abbreviation (period included) standing for 'Записки' (the journal/title). In references it is left abbreviated; here it names the publication in which the cited article appeared.
segments.p2-10-g744: A year number; functions as bibliographic data indicating the publication year.
segments.p2-10-g745: 'N 2.' is a bibliographic/issue marker: 'N' (here a printed abbreviation/placeholder) indicates 'number' (issue number) followed by '2'; commonly read as 'No. 2.'
segments.p2-10-g841: A single-letter capitalized abbreviation followed by a period; in bibliographic contexts this stands for Москва (Moscow). The period marks an abbreviation, and this segment functions as the place of publication, typically followed by a comma and the year.
segments.p2-10-g842: A comma serving as punctuation to separate the place (preceding abbreviation) from the year that follows; it does not carry lexical meaning.
segments.p2-10-g843: Arabic numerals giving the year of publication; the final period serves as sentence-ending or bibliographic punctuation. Here it follows the place name (Moscow) to form a typical “Place, Year” publication line.
segments.p2-10-g844: An abbreviation (Т.) for том (volume) marked by a period; in English bibliographic style this corresponds to “Vol.” It functions as a label that will be followed by the volume number.
segments.p2-10-g845: A numeral indicating the volume number; the period may indicate end of the bibliographic item or simply follow printing conventions. Together with the preceding abbreviation (Т.) it reads as “Vol. 1.”
segments.p2-10-g775: Покиватель is a standalone noun in the nominative singular acting as an entry/headword; the suffix -атель forms an agent noun meaning "one who (verb)s". Here it names an agent (a person who nods) and is presented as an isolated lemma or label.
segments.p2-10-g776: Это слово is a demonstrative phrase in the nominative (это + noun) functioning as the sentence topic/subject: "this word." The comma marks a small pause before the comment that follows.
segments.p2-10-g777: Очевидно is an adverbial parenthetical (set off by commas) that qualifies the statement that follows—here meaning "apparently/evidently." It modifies the predication rather than forming part of the core noun phrase.
segments.p2-10-g778: Образовано is a short passive participle/predicative meaning "was formed" and agrees implicitly with слово (neuter); Достоевским is the agent in the instrumental case ("by Dostoevsky"); от + genitive (просторечного "киватель") indicates the source/root from which the word was formed ('from the colloquial "киватель"').
segments.p2-10-g779: Так назывался человек is an inverted, slightly literary order meaning "thus was called a person" or more naturally "it called a person such." Назывался is past imperfective reflexive meaning "was called/was known as," with человек as the grammatical subject in the nominative.
segments.p2-10-g780: Который begins a relative clause modifying человек: "who/which." Киает is present-tense, 3rd-person singular indicative (habitual/simple present) meaning "nods," and головой is in the instrumental case (кивать + instrumental) meaning "with the head" or "nods his head."
segments.p2-10-g781: Перемигивается is a reflexive present-tense verb meaning "exchanges winks" or "winks at one another"; или coordinates this action with the following clause. Даёт is present tense "gives" and takes знаки (accusative plural) as its object; скрытно is an adverb "secretly" modifying даёт; кому-либо is a dative with the particle либо creating an indefinite "to someone/somebody." См. is the abbreviated editorial instruction "see (cf.)."
segments.p2-10-g782: A colon serving as punctuation to introduce the bibliographic/reference attribution that follows.
segments.p2-10-g783: Даль В. is a compact bibliographic citation (surname followed by initial), here naming the lexicographer Vladimir Dal as the source. The two elements are presented in citation form (surname + initial).
segments.p2-10-g784: A single-letter initial followed by a period; presented alone it functions as an abbreviation or initial (likely an author's initial or the start of another entry).
segments.p2-10-g806: This segment is a lone comma; it functions as punctuation connecting this parenthetical remark to surrounding text (it begins a short parenthetical example).
segments.p2-10-g807: Например is an adverb meaning 'for example' or 'for instance'; the colon after it (kept with the word here) signals that what follows is an example or citation.
segments.p2-10-g808: This is an abbreviated author citation: a surname-like form followed by an initial. In printed references Russian often writes the surname then the initial (here garbled as 'Бгрк Э.'). Treat it as a name abbreviation rather than ordinary words.
segments.p2-10-g809: This whole chunk is a nominal title in Russian: 'Философское исследование' (a philosophical enquiry/study) followed by the prepositional phrase 'о происхождении наших представлений о высоком и прекрасном' ('concerning the origin of our ideas of the sublime and beautiful'). Grammatically, после 'Философское исследование' the preposition 'о' takes the noun 'происхождении' in the prepositional/locative sense 'about / concerning'; 'наших представлений' is a genitive modifier ('our ideas/conceptions'); the parenthetical year (1756) is appended at the end. The stray closing parenthesis after 'наших)' appears to be a typographic or scanning artifact and does not change the grammatical relations.
segments.p2-10-g810: This is an author-credit fragment: 'Кант И.' is the surname 'Kant' followed by an initial 'I.' (in Russian citations the surname comes first and the initial follows). It identifies the author of the preceding title.
segments.p2-10-g811: This is another title-like phrase: 'Наблюдение над чувством' = 'Observation on/over the feeling' where the preposition 'над' governs the instrumental 'чувством'. 'Высокого и прекрасного' are adjectives describing what the feeling is of ('the lofty/sublime and the beautiful'); the year (1764) is parenthetical, and 'и др.' means 'and others' (i.e., other works).
segments.p2-10-g813: The sentence begins with the prepositional temporal phrase 'После переоценки эстетики "чистого" искусства' ('After the reassessment of the aesthetics of "pure" art'); 'После' takes a noun in the genitive ('переоценки'). The time frame 'в 1840-1860-х годах' specifies when the reassessment occurred. The main clause 'это выражение приобрело иронический оттенок' has 'это выражение' as the nominative subject and the perfective past 'приобрело' ('took on/acquired') with the direct object 'иронический оттенок' ('an ironic shade/nuance').
segments.p2-10-g836: Фохтом is a proper name in the instrumental form (here used to indicate the author—an older or bibliographic style; the effect is equivalent to naming the writer 'Focht'). Писал is the past-tense imperfective verb (masculine singular) meaning 'wrote' or 'was writing'. О is the preposition 'about' and takes the noun происхождении in the prepositional case (о + prepositional), literally 'about the origin'. Разных рас людей is a genitive-phrase modifying происхождении: разнах (adjective) and рас (noun) are in the genitive plural meaning 'of different races', with людей in genitive plural specifying 'of people' (races of people). От introduces the source and takes the genitive: различных пород обезьян is genitive plural 'of various breeds/types of apes'. См. is the standard abbreviation 'see' (imperative/imperative-ellipsis in references) and signals a bibliographic citation started but not closed in this segment.
segments.p2-10-g837: A colon punctuation mark that continues the parenthetical reference begun by 'см.' in the previous segment; it introduces the cited author.
segments.p2-10-g838: Зайцев is a surname (here nominative as the cited author) and V. is the abbreviated initial of the given name; together they form a standard bibliographic author label ('Zaitsev, V.').
segments.p2-10-g839: Избр. is an abbreviation (short for избранные/избранное) used in bibliographic entries meaning 'selected' (as in 'selected works'); the period shows it's an abbreviation.
segments.p2-10-g840: Соч. is an abbreviation of сочинения/сочинения (or 'соч.' as short for 'сочинения'/'сочинение') meaning 'works' or 'writings' in a bibliographic entry; combined with the previous segment it yields 'Selected works.'
segments.p2-10-g766: Постепенно is an adverb meaning 'gradually' and here precedes the verb for neutral emphasis. Рос is the past tense, masculine/neuter singular form of расти and agrees with интерес (the understood subject) — literally 'grew'. К introduces the object of interest with the dative/goal meaning 'toward' or 'in', and этой повести is a genitive/attributive phrase ('this novella') modifying повести; the whole clause uses somewhat inverted word order (adverb + verb + subject) for stylistic effect.
segments.p2-10-g767: Мироощущение is a compound noun ('worldview' or 'world-feeling') in the nominative and serves as the main noun of the noun phrase. Подпольного человека is a genitive attributive phrase ('of the “Underground Man”'); подпольного is an adjective in the genitive agreeing with человека, and the quoted name is treated as the possessive/genitive modifier of мироощущение.
segments.p2-10-g768: Генетически is an adverb modifying the participle связанного. Связанного is a past passive participle in the genitive singular, agreeing with the head noun (мироощущение) through the genitive possessive construction (открытие Достоевского, генетически связанного ...). С + instrumental (лишними людьми) marks the thing to which it is 'linked'; 1840–1850-х годов is a genitive time specification ('of the 1840s–1850s').
segments.p2-10-g769: Заключало is the past tense neuter singular of заключать because it agrees with the neuter subject мироощущение; here it means 'contained' or 'enclosed'. В себе is an idiom meaning 'in itself' (себе is the reflexive pronoun used with verbs like заключать). Ростки is the direct object in plural ('sprouts'/'seeds'), and позднейшего буржуазного индивидуализма is a genitive phrase modifying ростки ('of later bourgeois individualism'); и эгоцентризма simply adds 'and egocentrism.'
segments.p2-10-g770: Художественное открытие is a nominal phrase: художественное is an adjective ('artistic' or 'literary') modifying открытие ('discovery'). Достоевского is the genitive attributive ('of Dostoevsky') expressing possession or authorship of that 'discovery.' The comma signals that a participial phrase will further describe this discovery/author.
segments.p2-10-g771: Впервые указавшего is a past active participle phrase ('having for the first time pointed') and is in the same case as Достоевского (genitive), functioning as an appositive modifier: 'Dostoevsky, who for the first time pointed to...'. На introduces the object of указать ('to point to'). Социальную опасность + превращения: опасность takes the noun превращения in the genitive ('the danger of the transformation'). Самостоятельного хотения is a genitive noun phrase ('of independent willing/will/desire') modifying превращения. В + noun phrase with a participial construction (сознательно выбираемый ею принцип поведения) expresses the target state: 'into a principle of behavior consciously chosen by it' — выбираемый is a present passive participle and ею is the instrumental/reflexive 'by it/her' referring back to личность.
segments.p2-10-g772: На рубеже is a temporal phrase meaning 'at the turn/threshold of'; рубеже here takes the preposition на + prepositional/locative sense. XIX и XX вв. are Roman numerals with the abbreviation вв. (веков) meaning 'centuries' in the appropriate case: together they give 'at the turn of the 19th and 20th centuries.'
segments.p2-10-g773: Получило is past tense neuter singular ('received') and refers back to the neuter subject in the previous clause (Художественное открытие). Подтверждение is the direct object ('confirmation'). В + noun in the prepositional (ницшеанстве) indicates the sphere or movement in which this confirmation occurred: 'in Nietzscheanism.'
